threads.finding_body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `stripHtml` function currently only strips HTML tags but does not decode common HTML entities (such as `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`). This results in notes containing raw HTML entities being displayed in the UI. Decoding these common entities improves readability.

```typescript
function stripHtml(html: string): string {
  return html
    .replace(/<[^>]*>/g, ' ')
    .replace(/&/g, '&')
    .replace(/</g, '<')
    .replace(/>/g, '>')
    .replace(/"/g, '"')
    .replace(/'/g, "'")
    .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
    .trim();
}
```

replies.author: dzackgarza

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Add a settle retry before declaring imports invisible**

When the Zotero write plugin returns before SQLite has flushed the new item, this immediate `loadLibrary()` can miss `result.item_key`; the live add-item diagnostic already waits before reloading for this reason. In that timing window the route returns 502 `zotero_visibility_failed` even though the import succeeded, so the modal leaves the user on an error and a retry can create duplicates. Poll/retry for the Zotero settle window before failing visibility.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

No substantive remediation is claimed by resolving this duplicate thread.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Recover when the selected collection disappears**

If a user is viewing a collection and that collection is deleted or removed in Zotero before the next reload, `App` keeps the stale `selectedCollectionId` while the refreshed `collections` no longer contains it; this throw then happens during render via `selectVisibleLibraryItems`, sending the whole app to the error boundary. For live DB reloads this stale UI state should reset to `all` or render an empty view instead of crashing.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). a stale selected collection is reconciled to live data before view derivation; the selector's loud guard is kept (not softened). Audit: commit d84cd45. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body: P1 POLICY.RUNTIME_DEFAULT / POLICY.NO_PARTIAL_SUCCESS: RawItemRowSchema admits null title/date fields, but ZoteroItem declares title, dateAdded, and dateModified as required strings. Mapping null title to "Untitled" and null dates to empty strings converts a malformed or unsupported DB row into a valid-looking library item. That weakens the preflight boundary this PR claims to add: bad DB shape can survive as synthetic domain data instead of failing at the row parser. The fix should make the required domain fields total at the boundary or fail loudly, not fabricate display placeholders inside queryLibrary.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). title made optional (a titleless item is a valid Zotero state), dates non-nullable, no fabricated placeholders. Audit: commit 11712f2. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body: P1 POLICY.NO_MOCK_PROOF / POLICY.NO_HELPER_PROOF: This test proves a mocked prop callback was called inside an isolated TopBar render. It does not prove the read-only app routes the Add Item workflow only through the owned identifier-ingestion path, and it would still pass if App wired this callback to the wrong modal, a dead callback, or a local mutation path elsewhere. For the read-only boundary, proof needs to exercise the real app composition or the server route contract, not a vi.fn call count on one leaf component.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). the Add-Item test now drives the real <App/> composition and asserts the actual POST /api/items/from-source. Audit: commit 7ab3837. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body: P2 NON-PROOF-TESTS / BLAST-RADIUS: This assertion is coupled to cmdk's private DOM attribute and only proves that some internal nodes exist and are under an arbitrary count. A broken palette could satisfy this while failing the actual latency fix, and an upstream cmdk markup change could fail it while user behavior remains correct. The prior bug was closed-state item indexing and mount pressure; assert the owned behavior directly, such as no item title reads/indexing while closed and searchable user-visible results after opening, rather than testing third-party DOM internals.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). the palette test now asserts user-visible result bounds via the owned PALETTE_RESULT_LIMIT, not cmdk DOM internals. Audit: commit 7ab3837. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Do not pass the All sentinel to imports**

When the modal is opened from the default My Library view, this predicate is true because the loaded collections include the synthetic `{ id: 'all', name: 'My Library' }`, so the add-by-identifier POST sends `collections: ['all']` and the server forwards it as `collection_keys`. Since `all` is only a UI sentinel rather than a real Zotero collection key, the primary add workflow tries to import into a non-existent collection instead of the library root; sentinel views should be filtered out here as before.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

A single owned capability (fuzzy string matching) should use one dependency, not two overlapping libraries with different APIs and bundle costs.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Correct, and likely the more correct behaviour here is to actually offload to a tighter OS integration: fzf in terminal, dmenu/rofi, etc. Or at least pick a canonical choice internally.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified in code: a single owned capability (fuzzy matching) is backed by two libraries — `fuse.js` (fuzzy.ts:2) for advanced search and `fzf` (fuzzy.ts:4) for the palette, both consuming the same `ZoteroSearchDocument` projection. The owner's direction on this thread is authoritative: pick a canonical choice internally (or offload to a tighter OS integration such as fzf-in-terminal / dmenu / rofi).
Remediation disposition: accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: One owned capability should not carry two overlapping engines with different APIs and bundle cost (dependency/search accretion).
Audit anchor: src/utils/fuzzy.ts:2, 4; owner direction at PR comment id 3448320385.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted policy-aligned defect; remediation to follow. (Thread remains OPEN.)

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: True. A single owned capability (fuzzy string matching) is realized through two overlapping libraries: Fuse (fuzzy.ts:2, used at fuzzy.ts:263) and Fzf (fuzzy.ts:4, used at fuzzy.ts:288).
  Policy basis: anti-slop Dependency Inversion / single-capability-one-dependency. The owner has explicitly confirmed the concern in PR comment 3448320385 (verbatim): "Correct, and likely the more correct behaviour here is to actually offload to a tighter OS integration: fzf in terminal, dmenu/rofi, etc. Or at least pick a canonical choice internally."
  Audit anchor: src/utils/fuzzy.ts:2,4 (imports) and :263,:288 (both engines instantiated). No remediation detail provided here per disposition-round firewall.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted — BLOCKED on owner decision (no commit; thread left open).

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim real — fuzzy matching is one owned capability implemented with two libraries (Fuse for advanced search, Fzf for the palette). Owner direction (comment 3448320385): pick a canonical choice internally (or OS integration).

Blocker: an independent remediation agent empirically verified that NEITHER single library can reproduce both currently-tested behaviors: Fzf cannot do the advanced-search threshold matchType:'any' (it lacks edit-distance thresholding), and Fuse cannot do the palette subsequence match ('agtf' uniquely ranking the Algebraic-Geometry item). Consolidating to one engine would silently break a tested behavior, which policy forbids. This needs an owner decision among: (1) consolidate and accept the behavior change (weaken one mode's matching), (2) keep two libraries, (3) offload to OS integration (fzf-in-terminal / dmenu / rofi). No code changed; thread remains open pending that decision.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted with modified remediation.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim real — fuzzy matching is one owned capability implemented with two libraries (Fuse + Fzf). Owner direction (comment 3448320385): pick a canonical choice internally.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / orchestrator): Root concern: one capability should use one engine. Code/action: advanced search now runs on fzf (the palette's engine) — each whitespace token matched independently, matchType 'all'=token-AND, 'any'=token-OR; fuse.js removed; the dead fuzzyThreshold setting removed (it was Fuse-only, no UI control). The earlier 'cannot consolidate' claim was wrong: it rested on internals tests pinning Fuse's edit-distance threshold output (a matchType:'any' query asserted to return []), not user stories. Those tests were replaced with user-story proofs (all/any over typed words, field scoping, fail-loud on unknown field). Proof: just test-ci green (119 tests).

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): Spec honored — only fzf is imported, fuse.js is gone from package.json, advanced search ranks on fzf. Commit: 61f121a.

threads.finding_body:
### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: c4d87244824672e5d59d9ced1a93e868ff26247c8d64b6b4fbd9e4776b3ae1ea -->

**Location:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:182-201`
**Violated invariant:** Every validation boundary must be necessary — redundant parsing on the production path is dead validation that masks whether the second check actually guards a distinct code path.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'parseBibTeXToMetadata' src/server/resolverPlugins.ts`
**Pattern:** redundant-validation — parseBibTeXToMetadata is called in runResolverPlugin (line 183) to validate plugin BibTeX output, then called again in importBibTeXToZotero (line 201). Since resolveSourceToZotero (lines 227-228) always chains runResolverPlugin before importBibTeXToZotero, the second parse validates already-validated data on the production path.
**Why this matters:** Redundant parsing adds latency to every resolver request and creates a false defense-in-depth narrative. If someone later calls importBibTeXToZotero directly (bypassing runResolverPlugin), the second parse provides a validation guard — but that path would also bypass stdin routing, timeout enforcement, and byte-limit enforcement, so the parse alone doesn't compensate for those.
**Evidence:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:180-190` (file-read), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:199-205` (file-read), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:219-229` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Duplicate disposition: Duplicate of the resolver validation-boundary thread: https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/pull/7#discussion_r3448313486. The canonical thread remains unresolved until the validation boundary is remediated or rejected with evidence.

No substantive remediation is claimed by resolving this duplicate thread.

threads.finding_body:
### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 2c987d4f406295da9ef64dc46381a1191877a3ebe1ec25c92d3568ecb1558f0d -->

**Location:** `resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:62-73`
**Violated invariant:** Generated BibTeX must be well-formed — field values wrapped in braces must have braces escaped so the output is always parseable by the BibTeX parser.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'bookBibTeX' resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs && grep -n 'text(' resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs`
**Pattern:** unescaped-output — bookBibTeX wraps field values in {value} without escaping embedded braces. If Open Library returns a title containing '}', the generated BibTeX is malformed. The text helper (line 13) only trims whitespace but does not escape delimiters.
**Why this matters:** Malformed BibTeX triggers parse failure in the resolver execution layer, turning a valid API response into a resolver error. In worst case, carefully crafted upstream data could produce BibTeX parsed differently than intended, leading to incorrect item metadata in the Zotero library.
**Evidence:** `resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:62-73` (file-read), `resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:13-16` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Generating BibTeX by hand-splicing field values into {value} without escaping embedded braces is a real well-formedness/correctness defect at an owned output boundary, and is the dependency-aversion / hand-rolled-serialization pattern the maintainer flagged directly on this resolver ("use the equivalent of a pydantic model that knows how to export itself, instead of hand-rolling assembly").
Remediation disposition: Deferred to the remediation round.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy (default to the dependency for solved serialization problems; crash/fail-loud at the boundary, never emit silently-malformed output).
Code/action taken: Accepted policy-aligned issue, remediation to follow. No fix detail recorded here; this thread remains open and is resolvable only by a commit-citing reply.
Audit anchor: resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs (bookBibTeX serialization boundary).

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Outdated (superseded by efaed4c + 4a56c60).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: described defect no longer present in HEAD.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy "Crash at the Boundary" / well-formed-output invariant. The finding (`isbn-lib.mjs:62-73`) flagged a hand-rolled `bookBibTeX` that built `@book{...}` by string interpolation, wrapping every field value in `{${value}}` with a `text()` helper that only trims and does not escape brace delimiters. That hand-rolled serializer was replaced by Citation.js (`new Cite([...]).format('bibtex')`) in efaed4c, which escapes string fields (title/publisher); the residual CSL name-field brace gap was then closed in 4a56c60 by `citeName` (isbn-lib.mjs:19-26), which rejects `{`/`}` in author names loudly.
- Audit anchor: current `bookBibTeX` at resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:71-85 serializes via Citation.js; brace guard at resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:19-26. Fixing commits: efaed4c (Citation.js migration), 4a56c60 (name brace invariant).

threads.finding_body:
### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 774e5af71a51479e5bd627a3247eecaddfb1326ddb0f9da32242fc1ad7963e44 -->

**Location:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:2-4`
**Violated invariant:** A single owned capability (fuzzy string matching) should use one dependency, not two overlapping libraries with different APIs and bundle costs.
**Proof:** `rg -c 'from.*fuse\.js|from.*fzf' src/`
**Pattern:** dependency-replication — both fuse.js (imported line 2) and fzf (imported line 4) provide fuzzy string matching. fuse.js powers rankDocuments/filterZoteroItems; fzf powers rankPaletteDocuments. Either library alone can serve both use cases. Two libraries mean two bundles, two API surfaces, and two maintenance tracks.
**Why this matters:** Each library adds to the production bundle (~7KB gzipped for fuse.js, ~5KB for fzf), increases the dependency audit surface, and duplicates a capability that a single library provides. When both are used for the same fundamental task, the project pays double for overlapping functionality.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:1-5` (file-read)

### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 6391f1c8b31d58d56584d8e8809f5f77a5626ddbbd3917521a9dd9d750b0bcfa -->

**Location:** `src/keyboardShortcuts.ts:1-1`
**Violated invariant:** Server configuration must not leak into the frontend bundle — frontend code must import only what it needs, not a file containing database URIs, storage paths, and write endpoints.
**Proof:** `rg 'import.*zotero-gui\.config' src/`
**Pattern:** config-leak — import appConfig from '../zotero-gui.config.json' inlines the entire server config at build time. The imported file contains zotero.databaseUri, zotero.storageDir, zotero.importEndpoint, resolverManifestPath, and resolverExecution limits. Only keyboardShortcuts is consumed by the frontend.
**Why this matters:** If zotero-gui.config.json is missing or malformed at build time, the frontend build fails. The server's database URI, storage directory layout, and write API endpoint are embedded in the frontend JS bundle. A config change requires a frontend rebuild even if only server values changed.
**Evidence:** `src/keyboardShortcuts.ts:1-10` (file-read), `zotero-gui.config.json:1-32` (file-read)

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Bind the Zotero API to loopback by default**

In a normal `just api` run, omitting the host makes the Express server listen on every interface while the log still advertises localhost. Any machine that can reach port 3001 can then call `GET /api/library` to read the user's Zotero metadata and attachment paths, or POST to the attachment/import routes to launch local attachments and write items into Zotero. For a local GUI, bind to `127.0.0.1` by default or make remote exposure explicitly configured and authenticated.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Wrong security model. Bespoke software => user likely WANTS other local apps to interact with it.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Rejected. bespoke single-user software intentionally allows local-app interop and owns no network-containment boundary; loopback-bind + auth is excluded enterprise hardening. No change. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Exclude standalone notes from top-level item scans**

When a Zotero library contains a standalone note, its `itemNotes.parentItemID` is `NULL`, so this exclusion leaves that note in the observed top-level item types. The preflight then sees item type `note` and `parseItemType` rejects it because `ITEM_TYPES` does not include notes, causing the whole library load to fail for an otherwise valid Zotero database; the raw item query repeats the same predicate, so standalone notes should either be modeled explicitly or excluded along with child notes.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Zotero libraries should not contain standalone notes: this is a malformed library by this app's standards (not citable or attached to a citable entry...)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). standalone notes now fail loud via a dedicated standalone_note contract error before serving. Audit: commit 11712f2. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body: P1 POLICY.NO_DYNAMIC_ARTIFACTS / DEPENDENCY-AVERSION: InspectorPanel exposes a BibTeX generation action backed by local string interpolation, and App.tsx also fabricates an APA-looking citation string. The project already declares citeproc, and Zotero/BetterBibTeX are the bibliographic authorities here. A user-facing citation/export action should use a real citation/export boundary, not local string assembly that hard-codes article shape, author formatting, year fallback, DOI/url fields, and key generation. This is dependency inversion, not display polish.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). BibTeX/citation now render via Citation.js + plugin-csl; bespoke string builders and silent 'unknown'/'N.D.' defaults removed. Audit: commit 911cf6f. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body: P1 POLICY.RUNTIME_DEFAULT / PARSER-REINVENTION: this parser uses bibtex-parse-js only as a tokenizer, then reimplements BibTeX value cleanup, author splitting, field aliasing, and Zotero item-type mapping by hand. The default item type is journalArticle for every unrecognized entry type, which turns unsupported input into a valid-looking domain type. The resolver route only needs to prove the plugin produced exactly one BibTeX entry before sending it to Zotero import; it does not need a second partial bibliographic mapper with fail-open type semantics.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). reduced to a validation gate (one entry + title + author); the fail-open journalArticle default and the dead hand-rolled mapper are gone. Audit: commit eb34474. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body: P1 DEPENDENCY-AVERSION / ACCRETED TABLE STATE MACHINE: this new table component and useColumnLayout hand-own column visibility, ordering, drag/drop, sizing, resizing, sorting clicks, visible column projection, row expansion, and context-menu column controls. TanStack Table owns these exact headless table state machines: column visibility, ordering, sizing/resizing, sorting, and row models. The current split across App, useColumnLayout, librarySelectors, and LibraryTable means every future table behavior change has to preserve bespoke table-engine plumbing. The finding is not to adopt a design system; it is that the PR wrote and spread a table engine instead of using the mature headless dependency for the hard part.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). migrated to @tanstack/react-table; useColumnLayout deleted; persistence fails loud on malformed state. Audit: commit 611ab5f. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

threads.finding_body:
P2 SPLIT-TRUTH / SEARCH ACCRETION: fuzzy.ts now materializes a separate ZoteroSearchDocument, uses Fuse for advanced search, uses Fzf for palette search, keeps SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS and PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS separately, while App.tsx derives default searchable fields from DEFAULT_COLUMNS. That is not one search contract; it is overlapping search systems sharing a hand-built projection. If palette and table search are intentionally different, the difference needs one explicit owner. If not, one search adapter should own the projection and ranking contract.

This is secondary cleanup and must not be treated as progress on the P1 citation, parser, or table-engine findings.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted with modified remediation. Remediation committed (fail-loud, no fallback). single canonical key source (PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS); App derives default search fields from it (desync-guard test). Audit: commit aaa4cb7. See the top-level **Review feedback disposition ledger** for full reasoning.

replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified in code: `assertZoteroDatabaseContract` runs `queryLibrary(db)` and discards the result (zoteroRepository.ts:283-290), and the only caller, `loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri`, then calls `queryLibrary(db)` again (zoteroDatabase.ts:8-9). Every library load therefore executes the full SQL pipeline (all queries + Zod row parsing) twice.
Remediation disposition: accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: The preflight contract check should not require re-running the entire data pipeline; this is owned boundary logic.
Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:283-290; src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts:8-9.

threads.finding_body:
### [General Review][tier1] CYCLE_DOUBLE_EXECUTION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: b92398895c832480eafb1f81e5c8095ad241a8bd6f3a2313e0ba90540b5f579b -->

**Location:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:277-291`
**Violated invariant:** assertZoteroDatabaseContract must not execute the full queryLibrary data pipeline only to discard the result, forcing the caller to repeat every SQL query and row parse.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'queryLibrary(db)' src/server/zoteroRepository.ts src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts`
**Symptom:** Every call to loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri executes queryLibrary twice: once inside assertZoteroDatabaseContract (result discarded), and once in loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri (result returned). The first execution runs 7+ SQL queries (each with MAX(CASE) PIVOTs and JOINs), parses all rows through Zod, builds Map indexes, and maps to domain types, then throws everything away.
**Source:** assertZoteroDatabaseContract at zoteroRepository.ts:284 calls queryLibrary(db) purely as a validation probe, returning void. loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri at zoteroDatabase.ts:8-9 then calls assertZoteroDatabaseContract followed by a second call to queryLibrary that actually returns the data. The contract verifier and the data path have no shared cache.
**Consequence:** For any Zotero library of non-trivial size, every load or reload executes the full SQL pipeline twice. This doubles DB I/O, Zod parsing, and map construction latency. A 1000-item library scanning ~20 field PIVOTs, 5 relational queries, and 5 Map-building passes does all that work twice per page view or reload.
**Evidence:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:277-291` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts:5-13` (file-read)

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Expose Zotero collection keys for imports**

For real user collections, this exposes the internal numeric `collectionID` as the UI `selectedCollectionId`; `App` then passes that value to `/api/items/from-source`, and the server forwards it unchanged as `collection_keys` to the Zotero write endpoint. The write API distinguishes `item_key`/`item_id` and reports `collection_keys`, so a selected collection like `100` is not the Zotero collection key and imports into a selected collection will fail or target nothing; read and propagate `collections.key` (and map memberships/parents to keys) instead of publishing the database id as the collection id used by the add flow.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified in code: collections are published with `id: String(row.collectionID)` (zoteroRepository.ts:510), and the `rawCollections` query selects `collectionID, collectionName, parentCollectionID` only — it never reads `collections.key` (zoteroRepository.ts:409-416). For a real user collection the UI `selectedCollectionId` is therefore the numeric database id, and the server's `/api/items/from-source` accepts any non-sentinel string as a `collection` and forwards it as `collection_keys` to the Zotero write endpoint (server.ts:40-44), which expects actual Zotero collection keys. The prior sentinel-rejection change (d84cd45) addresses only library-view sentinels, not the numeric-id-vs-key mismatch for real collections, so this P1 concern is still live.
Remediation disposition: accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: Imports into a selected real collection silently target nothing / fail — a correctness defect at the owned add-flow boundary.
Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:510, 409-416; src/server/server.ts:40-44.

The repository publishes the internal numeric `collectionID` as the collection `id` (src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:510, `id: String(row.collectionID)`), and collection memberships are likewise keyed by `String(row.collectionID)` (line 435). The query selects `collectionID`, not `collections.key` (lines 409-416). This database id, not the Zotero collection key, then flows to the add-item write path as `collection_keys`.
- Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:409-416 (query selects collectionID), :435 (membership uses collectionID), :510 (id published as collectionID).

threads.finding_body:
**[CRITICAL]:** Command search mode is broken — `shouldFilter={false}` disables all cmdk filtering, and no manual replacement is applied

`CmdK` (cmdk's `Command` component) has `shouldFilter={false}`, which disables its built-in query-to-item filtering for all nested `CmdK.Item` children. In command mode (triggered by `>`), line 187 renders `commands.map(renderCommandItem)` without any manual filtering, so ALL commands appear regardless of what the user types. The empty-state message at line 182-184 is never reachable in command mode either. This is a functional regression from the prior implementation, which used `fuse.search(...)` to actually narrow commands by name/category. The test at `src/components/CommandPalette.test.tsx:107-124` passes regardless because it only verifies that one command is rendered — it would pass even with the filter regression.

Fix: either remove `shouldFilter={false}` and rely on cmdk's built-in `value`-based filtering (each `CmdK.Item` already has `value={command.name}`), or add a manual `.filter(...)` before the `.map` at line 187.
---
Reply with `@kilocode-bot fix it` to have Kilo Code address this issue.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified in code: `CmdK` is rendered with `shouldFilter={false}` (CommandPalette.tsx:162), and in command mode the list renders `commands.map(renderCommandItem)` (CommandPalette.tsx:186-187) with no manual query-based narrowing. `query` is tracked for command mode (line 63) but never used to filter `commands`, so every command shows regardless of input, and `CmdK.Empty` (lines 182-184) is unreachable in command mode. Item mode is filtered via `rankZoteroSearchDocumentsForPalette` (line 51), so the existing test only exercises item-mode bounds and does not cover command-mode filtering.
Remediation disposition: accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: This is a functional correctness defect in an owned UI workflow (command-mode search), not enterprise hardening.
Audit anchor: src/components/CommandPalette.tsx:162, 186-187.

replies.body:

  Remediation disposition: n/a — already fixed before this disposition round.
  Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Loop and Convergence — "A thread on code that a later commit replaced -> Outdated (superseded by <commit>), closed with that pointer."
  Audit anchor: src/components/CommandPalette.tsx:162 at HEAD reads `shouldFilter={mode === 'commands'}` (git blame: introduced by 09d3964). cmdk built-in value-based filtering is now enabled in command mode, with each CmdK.Item carrying value={command.name} (line 107). The `shouldFilter={false}` defect this thread describes is gone.

### [General Review][tier2] INCORRECT_ERROR_CLASSIFICATION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 5af8734b18c2aab145197ee1b90525babd56ab976c1d2c79a5560111123f069e -->

**Location:** `src/server/server.ts:109-114`
**Violated invariant:** A request with a malformed JSON body sent to any POST endpoint must return HTTP 400 with kind invalid_request, not HTTP 500 with kind internal_error. The error middleware must distinguish client-level parse errors from server bugs.
**Proof:** `Code inspection: express.json() at src/server/server.ts:118 throws SyntaxError on malformed JSON bodies. classifyError at line 113 maps every non-ApiError exception to 500 internal_error, so the SyntaxError becomes 500 instead of 400.`
**Symptom:** POST /api/items/from-source with an unparseable JSON body returns HTTP 500 with kind internal_error and leaks the SyntaxError message.
**Source:** src/server/server.ts:113 — classifyError returns 500 internal_error for any error that is not an instance of ApiError. express.json() SyntaxErrors are not ApiErrors, so they fall into this catch-all.
**Consequence:** Client input errors (malformed JSON) are indistinguishable from server bugs at the HTTP boundary. The API promises structured error kinds via ApiErrorKind but cannot deliver 400 for request-level parse failures.
**Evidence:** `src/server/server.ts:109-114` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified: classifyError (src/server/server.ts:106-111) maps every non-ApiError to 500 internal_error, and the express error middleware (server.ts:179-187) returns that classification. express.json() (server.ts:115) throws SyntaxError on a malformed body, which is not an ApiError, so request-level parse failures surface as 500 internal_error instead of 400 invalid_request.
Remediation disposition: Accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: boundary/contract correctness (test-guidelines boundary logic) — the HTTP boundary must distinguish client parse errors from server bugs; maintainer ledger marks this "Remediation required."
Audit anchor: src/server/server.ts:106-111 (classifyError), 115 (express.json()), 179-187 (error middleware).

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Accepted (policy-aligned defect, to be remediated).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: true; defect still present in HEAD.
- Policy basis: incorrect error classification at an owned boundary. The server defines a structured `ApiErrorKind` taxonomy including `invalid_request` (400), but `classifyError` maps every non-`ApiError` to `500 internal_error` (src/server/server.ts:106-111). `express.json()` (line 115) throws a `SyntaxError` on a malformed JSON body, which is not an `ApiError`, so a client-level parse error is reported as a server-internal error and the SyntaxError message leaks through. The error taxonomy is the single source of truth for error kinds, and SyntaxError silently escapes it.
- Audit anchor: src/server/server.ts:106-111 (classifyError catch-all), :115 (express.json()), :14-22 (ApiErrorKind taxonomy).

This thread is left OPEN as an accepted policy-aligned defect for the remediation round. No fix is prescribed here.

threads.finding_body:
### [General Review][tier2] UNNECESSARY_RENDER_SIDE_EFFECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 1ccbfeb7d3ee55a6e6c9b202e006f3eb232348b15fd841ea179d0cd7b99c3e0c -->

**Location:** `src/useColumnLayout.ts:65-65`
**Violated invariant:** Expensive side effects (localStorage read, JSON.parse, Zod schema validation, column key iteration) in readStoredColumns must not execute on every React component render. The lazy initializer form of useState must be used to defer evaluation to mount time only.
**Proof:** `Add a console.log('readStoredColumns') as the first line of readStoredColumns at src/useColumnLayout.ts:31, then render App.tsx and observe the log on every render cycle even after the initial mount.`
**Symptom:** readStoredColumns (which reads localStorage, parses JSON with JSON.parse, validates with StoredColumnsSchema.parse, and iterates defaultKeys for contract checks) executes on every App.tsx render, though only the first invocation's return value is used by useState.
**Source:** src/useColumnLayout.ts:65 — useState(readStoredColumns) evaluates readStoredColumns() immediately on each render. The correct pattern for deferring the initializer is useState(() => readStoredColumns()).
**Consequence:** Each App render triggers localStorage access, JSON parse, Zod schema validation, and column-key set comparison. For a single-user app with a simple column set this is a performance concern rather than a correctness issue, but the pattern is a latent source of render-time cost as the schema or storage logic grows.
**Evidence:** `src/useColumnLayout.ts:30-62` (file-read), `src/useColumnLayout.ts:65-65` (file-read)

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve parser error status codes**

When `express.json()` rejects malformed JSON (or an oversized body) before a route handler runs, it forwards an HTTP error carrying a 4xx status. This fallback ignores those status fields and converts the client error into a 500 `internal_error`, so bad API requests look like server failures instead of request errors; preserve the parser error status before using the generic 500 fallback.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Resolve Zotero linked-attachment paths**

When Zotero stores linked files relative to its linked-attachment base directory, `itemAttachments.path` can be `attachments:...`; the repository exposes that value as `attachment.path`, but this branch rejects anything that is not `storage:` or absolute, so the new open-attachment route fails for valid linked attachments. Handle `attachments:` with a configured base directory, or avoid offering the open action for those paths.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified: resolveAttachmentFilePath (src/server/main.ts:14-29) handles only storage: prefixes and absolute paths, and throws at main.ts:28 for any other value. Zotero linked-attachment paths of the form attachments:... are valid and are surfaced through attachment.path, so the open-attachment route rejects valid linked attachments.
Remediation disposition: Accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy — support the path shape through declared linked-attachment base-directory config, or do not expose the open action for those paths; no silent fallback. Maintainer ledger: "Accepted with modified remediation."
Audit anchor: src/server/main.ts:14-29.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Rejected.**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: objects to an owner-directed scope choice; no silent failure exists. `resolveAttachmentFilePath` enumerates exactly two supported schemes (`storage:` and absolute) and throws a loud `unsupported Zotero path` error on anything else, including `attachments:` linked-file paths. The app config schema (src/server/config.ts) defines `zotero.storageDir` only and has no linked-attachment base-directory field, so supporting `attachments:` is a new feature requiring new configuration, not a fix to a defect.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy "Crash at the Boundary" / fail-loudly. The current code already fails loud on an unsupported path rather than silently mishandling it; the user sees the error and decides whether to add an explicit `attachments:` branch + base-dir config. Adding linked-attachment-base support is a user-owned feature/scope decision, not a policy-aligned defect.
- Audit anchor: src/server/main.ts:12-27 (loud throw on unsupported path); src/server/config.ts AppConfigSchema (no linked-attachment base dir field).

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Verify Zotero visibility before reporting add success**

Fresh evidence in this revision is that the route now builds a 200 response solely from the write-plugin result and never calls `loadLibrary()` after the import. That contradicts the repo's stated add-item contract that success means the created key becomes visible through the read API after Zotero settles; if the write plugin returns before the DB flush, drops the collection assignment, or otherwise returns a key that the read path cannot load, the modal shows success and reloads to a library where the item is still absent, encouraging duplicate retries. Poll/reload for `result.item_key` visibility before returning success, and fail the request if it never appears.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Claim disposition: Partially aligned on the symptom description, but the requested behavior conflicts with the maintainer's authoritative direction. The reviewer asks to "Poll/reload for result.item_key visibility before returning success, and fail the request if it never appears."
Remediation disposition: Misaligned and rejected. The post-write read-back was deliberately removed in this revision. Success is now determined solely from the authoritative write-boundary result (src/server/server.ts:152-161); the racy loadLibrary() read-back and the zotero_visibility_failed path were intentionally deleted, with no settle/retry. A poll/reload-until-visible loop is exactly the settle/retry fallback that was rejected.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy — no fallback / no settle-and-retry against an eventually-consistent snapshot; the write boundary already returns the created key, id, and title under a strict schema (CreatedItemResponseSchema). Re-introducing visibility polling would re-add the racy fallback this PR removed. Maintainer ledger 4762615855: success determined solely by the authoritative write result; racy read-back deleted; "No retry/settle (that was the rejected fallback)."
Code/action taken: No code change. Re-adding visibility verification is rejected.
Audit anchor: src/server/server.ts:142-161 (write-result-only success; documented rationale at 152-156).

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve slashes in old-style arXiv IDs**

The manifest still accepts old-style arXiv IDs such as `math.AG/0601001`, but this new `/bibtex/` request encodes the entire ID, turning the slash into `%2F`. For those accepted inputs the resolver no longer requests the normal `/bibtex/math.AG/0601001` path and can fail before import even though `pluginAcceptsInput` allowed the value; keep the slash as a path separator for old-style IDs or use a query endpoint for them.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. The resolver manifest (resolver-plugins.json) accepts old-style arXiv IDs containing a slash (pattern [a-z-]+(?:\.[A-Z]{2})?/\d{7}, e.g. math.AG/0601001), but resolver-plugins/arxiv.mjs builds its request with encodeURIComponent, which percent-encodes the slash. The accepted input shape and the URL construction disagree at an owned boundary. (The maintainer separately recorded this thread as remediation-required.)
Remediation disposition: Deferred to the remediation round.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy (the boundary must accept exactly the inputs the manifest admits, or reject them; an input the gate admits must not silently mis-resolve).
Code/action taken: Accepted policy-aligned issue, remediation to follow. No fix detail recorded; thread remains open.
Audit anchor: resolver-plugins/arxiv.mjs:25 vs resolver-plugins.json arxiv-id pattern.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Rejected.**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: false premise. The finding asserts that `encodeURIComponent` turning the old-style slash into `%2F` causes the request to fail. Verified otherwise against arxiv.org: `https://arxiv.org/bibtex/math.AG%2F0601001` returns HTTP 200 with the same BibTeX as the raw-slash form, so the encoded form resolves old-style IDs correctly. The alleged failure does not occur.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Verification Rule (a finding must identify a concrete defect that exists now; this one does not). Note `encodeURIComponent` was already the code at the time of the finding (4a56c60~1) — 4a56c60 made a comment-only change recording the verified arxiv.org contract, so no behavior changed.
- Audit anchor: resolver-plugins/arxiv.mjs:16-23 (encoded fetch + recorded verified contract); commit 4a56c60 (comment-only) documents the live HTTP 200 verification.

## ai-review/general / Route-level tests must exercise the production code path (loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri -> assertZoteroDatabaseContract -> queryLibrary), not bypass the contract preflight by passing queryLibrary(db) dir

Route-level tests must exercise the production code path (loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri -> assertZoteroDatabaseContract -> queryLibrary), not bypass the contract preflight by passing queryLibrary(db) directly.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/17)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Claim disposition: Misaligned. The finding asserts the route test "bypasses the contract preflight by passing queryLibrary(db) directly." This misreads the dependency-injection seam. The `/api/library` route owns only `deps.loadLibrary()` -> `LibraryPayloadSchema.parse` -> HTTP 200/JSON (server.ts:121-125). `loadLibrary` is a declared DI seam (`AppDeps.loadLibrary(): LibraryPayload`, server.ts:53); `loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri` and the contract preflight live behind that seam, not at the route boundary.
Remediation disposition: Misaligned. Forcing `loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri` into the route test would re-prove the file-open glue and the contract preflight at the wrong layer, both already proven at their own owned boundaries (test-guidelines Interlocking Rule: test the owned interlock, not the dependency).
Policy basis: test-guidelines Interlocking Rule and Owned-Surface Test. The route's owned boundary is the injected loader + schema parse + response, which this test exercises with concrete mapped-value assertions (lines 215-261). The preflight contract is independently and exhaustively proven in the same file (Zotero DB contract preflight block, lines 80-172), and the production composition `loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri` (assert + query against a real on-disk SQLite file) is independently proven in zoteroDatabase.test.ts:53-60. The test does not skip the preflight: beforeAll calls assertZoteroDatabaseContract(db) at line 177 before serving, mirroring production startup ordering (main.ts:46).
Code/action taken or explicit non-change: No code change. The cited construction is the correct DI seam, not a bypass.
Audit anchor: src/server/server.ts:53,121-125 (DI seam + route boundary); src/server/libraryApi.test.ts:80-172,177,215-261; src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts:5-13; src/server/zoteroDatabase.test.ts:53-60; src/server/main.ts:46,49.

### [General Review][tier1] UNTESTED_PRODUCTION_PATH
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 91020ecd04d703ae0db2e596c089c73619187398492bc3a44bf1e3bc6f128772 -->

**Location:** `src/server/libraryApi.test.ts:174-180`
**Violated invariant:** Route-level tests must exercise the production code path (loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri -> assertZoteroDatabaseContract -> queryLibrary), not bypass the contract preflight by passing queryLibrary(db) directly.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'loadLibrary:' src/server/libraryApi.test.ts`
**Symptom:** The /api/library route test injects queryLibrary(db) directly instead of loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri(dbUri), so assertZoteroDatabaseContract is never exercised through the route layer. A broken contract preflight would not be caught by these tests.
**Source:** libraryApi.test.ts:179 passes a direct queryLibrary(db) lambda to createApp instead of the production loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri(dbUri) that includes assertZoteroDatabaseContract.
**Consequence:** If assertZoteroDatabaseContract is modified to reject a previously-valid DB shape, the route tests would still pass even though the production path fails before serving data. The integration between the contract preflight and the Express routes is untested.
**Evidence:** `src/server/libraryApi.test.ts:174-180` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts:5-13` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:277-291` (file-read)

### [General Review][tier2] SURPRISING_ZERO_RESULTS
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 7e8a80b6ccee1d229886d794a6126b1a4933a38a1e58cb5e61f4f7fe68b2a7ef -->

**Location:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:279-283`
**Violated invariant:** When all search fields are disabled and a non-empty query exists, the user expects zero filtering (no fields to filter on = show everything) rather than zero results. An empty search-keys list should short-circuit to the full items list, not to an empty list.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'keys.length === 0' src/utils/fuzzy.ts`
**Symptom:** filterZoteroItems returns an empty array when settings.searchFields has no enabled fields, even with a non-empty query. The user sees 'Library is empty or filter returned zero matches' instead of the unfiltered library.
**Source:** fuzzy.ts:282: keys.length === 0 returns empty array. Zero enabled search fields means 'no documents can match' from the engines perspective, but the UX expectation is 'no filter is applied.'
**Consequence:** A user who enters a query after disabling all search fields in Advanced Search sees an empty table with a reset-filters button. The button clears the query but the empty result suggests no data exists, which is a UX trap.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:279-290` (file-read), `src/types.ts:154-160` (file-read)

### [General Review][tier2] INCONSISTENT_DUPLICATE_MEMBERSHIP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 6d491759b731a2932e6243fca1f85e0b35153e6b322e23917dd993f072b09953 -->

**Location:** `src/librarySelectors.ts:56-76`
**Violated invariant:** An item with undefined title is excluded from the duplicate-count map (count phase skips it via early return), but in the filter phase it is treated deterministically as not-a-duplicate (returns false). If a duplicate pair consists of one titled item and one title-less item, the title-less item is invisible to the duplicate detector.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'item.title === undefined' src/librarySelectors.ts`
**Symptom:** A title-less item (null title in Zotero DB mapped to undefined by RowItemSchema) and a titled item with the same bibliographic identity are not grouped as duplicates. The count phase excludes undefined-title items entirely.
**Source:** librarySelectors.ts:63: if (item.title === undefined) return; -- title-less items excluded from counting. librarySelectors.ts:69: if (item.title === undefined) return false; -- never classified as duplicate.
**Consequence:** The Duplicate Items view may miss real duplicate pairs where one member has no title in the DB. Since zoteroRepository.ts maps null title to undefined, any item with a missing title field is invisible to the duplicate detector.
**Evidence:** `src/librarySelectors.ts:56-76` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:473-505` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:21-49` (file-read)

### [General Review][tier2] RENDER_TIME_CITEKEY_COMPUTATION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: a8cd0851fee3366178a323d164e94d01ba48a7f0866518486113f33eb18dd73e -->

**Location:** `src/librarySelectors.ts:99-111`
**Violated invariant:** getStandardCitekey performs CSL.parseParticles + transliteration + fold-to-ascii per creator. When called on every render for every active item via nonstandardCitekeyItems (inside selectVisibleLibraryItems), this does O(creators*items) work on every state change, even when the nonstandard-citekey view is not selected.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'getStandardCitekey' src/librarySelectors.ts`
**Symptom:** selectVisibleLibraryItems unconditionally calls selectItemsForCollection, and for the nonstandard-citekey view case, getStandardCitekey runs for every active item on every render. For large libraries, every sort/search/filter change recomputes standard citekeys.
**Source:** librarySelectors.ts:100: getStandardCitekey(item) inside the filter callback, running for every active item on every derivation of selectVisibleLibraryItems.
**Consequence:** When the nonstandard-citekey view is selected, every render cycle recomputes standard citekeys for every active item without memoization. For libraries with thousands of items and creators, this produces a perceptible freeze on every interaction.
**Evidence:** `src/librarySelectors.ts:99-111` (file-read), `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:20-27` (file-read), `src/App.tsx:170-178` (file-read)

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Guard citation actions for attachment items**

When the selected record is a top-level Zotero attachment, this call throws because `itemToCsl()` intentionally treats `itemType === 'attachment'` as non-citable. This PR also surfaces standalone attachments as selectable library items, so clicking the inspector's BibTeX copy button raises an uncaught exception instead of disabling the action or showing a toast; the APA copy command has the same failure mode for the selected attachment.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified in code: `copyBibtex` calls `toBibTeX(item)` (InspectorPanel.tsx:88), which routes through `itemToCsl`, and `itemToCsl` throws for `attachment` (citation.ts:128-131, `ITEM_TYPE_TO_CSL.attachment === null`). Standalone attachments are surfaced as selectable top-level items (the top-level query excludes only child rows with `parentItemID IS NOT NULL`, so standalone attachments are not excluded; zoteroRepository.ts:334-340, 391-407). The `copyBibtex` promise chain has no rejection handler, so selecting an attachment and triggering BibTeX/APA copy raises an uncaught error rather than disabling or surfacing the action.
Remediation disposition: accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: Fail-loud is correct at `itemToCsl`; the defect is the unguarded call site exposing a non-citable item type to a citation action.
Audit anchor: src/components/InspectorPanel.tsx:88; src/utils/citation.ts:128-131.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted policy-aligned defect; remediation to follow. (Thread remains OPEN.)

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: True, verified at HEAD. Standalone attachments (parentItemID IS NULL) are surfaced as selectable top-level library items by the repository query (src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:334-344 excludes only child attachments/notes/annotations; src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:463-471,473-475 build standalone-attachment rows with itemType 'attachment'). itemToCsl throws for itemType 'attachment' (src/utils/citation.ts:128-131). The InspectorPanel BibTeX button calls toBibTeX(item) with no guard (src/components/InspectorPanel.tsx:87-93), and the APA command calls toFormattedCitation(selectedItem) with no guard (src/App.tsx:119-129). Both raise an uncaught exception when the selected record is an attachment.
  Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy — code must not raise an uncaught exception on a reachable user action; the citation action over a non-citable selectable item is an unhandled boundary the app owns. The two surfaces enforce the contract inconsistently with citation.ts's intentional fail-loud type.
  Audit anchor: src/components/InspectorPanel.tsx:87-93; src/App.tsx:119-129; src/utils/citation.ts:128-131. No remediation detail per disposition-round firewall.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — a standalone attachment is a selectable item with no CSL type, so invoking the copy-citation actions on it called the citation util and raised an uncaught throw in the UI.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The UI no longer offers a citation action for a non-citable selection. citation.ts exposes isCitable(item), derived from the same ITEM_TYPE_TO_CSL map that decides itemToCsl's throw-vs-render — the single source of truth, not a hardcoded item-type check in the component. The inspector's copy-BibTeX control is hidden for a non-citable item; App's copy-APA command is a no-op (toast) for one. itemToCsl's throw for non-citable types is unchanged. Proof: inspector copies real BibTeX for a citable item and offers no copy control for an attachment (no clipboard write, no escaped window error); App copies real APA for a citable selection and is a safe no-op for an attachment. Proven red->green per fix.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): Spec honored — citability flows through the citation module's predicate (no `itemType === 'attachment'` literal in the component/App); the non-citable-type throw is intact; tests assert user-visible outcomes (clipboard content, captured errors, affordance availability), not spy counts. Declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: f644c4c.

### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 01a97dc1a8fe72a4fbcd545b443af30eee5db5fc95ad404c33b1aa665ed7405e -->

**Location:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:12-14`
**Violated invariant:** Character transformation in citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail loud with an error that prevents silent data loss. The replacement-character fallback followed by silent removal at fuzzy.ts:77 violates the 'no silent data transformation' invariant.
**Proof:** `rg -n '\\uFFFD' src/utils/fuzzy.ts`
**Pattern:** Forced-success transliteration with bidirectional replacement-character substitution — bbtTransliterate uses transliterate(value, { unknown: '\uFFFD' }) to coerce every input to a string rather than failing loud when a character cannot be transliterated. The replacement character is then explicitly removed by bbtCleanCitekey at fuzzy.ts:77 via BBT_UNSAFE_CITEKEY_CHARS (line 8) which includes \uFFFD in its character class.
**Why this matters:** If an author name contains characters the transliteration library cannot handle (e.g., rare CJK, custom Unicode, unexpected input), the citekey silently loses those characters. The user sees a non-standard or shortened citekey with no way to trace the loss.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:8-8` (file-read), `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:12-14` (file-read), `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:76-78` (file-read), `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:97-100` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Duplicate.

This is the same citekey transliteration silent-data-loss finding tracked at fuzzy.ts:14 (https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/pull/7#discussion_r3448703940), which carries the accepted disposition. Resolved against that thread; no separate disposition.

No substantive remediation is claimed by resolving this duplicate thread.

threads.finding_body:
## ai-review/slop / Character transformation in citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail loud with an error that prevents silent data loss. The replacement-character fallback followed by silent r

Character transformation in citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail loud with an error that prevents silent data loss. The replacement-character fallback followed by silent removal at fuzzy.ts:77 violates the 'no silent data transformation' invariant.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/21)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified in code: `bbtTransliterate` calls `transliterate(value, { unknown: '�' })` (fuzzy.ts:12-13), coercing any untransliterable character to U+FFFD instead of failing; `bbtCleanCitekey` then strips U+FFFD via `BBT_UNSAFE_CITEKEY_CHARS` (fuzzy.ts:8, 76-78). A character the library cannot handle is therefore silently dropped from the citekey with no error and no trace.
Remediation disposition: accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: Silent data transformation violates fail-loud; citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail with a visible error rather than lose characters.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted policy-aligned defect; remediation to follow. (Thread remains OPEN.)

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: True, verified at HEAD. In citekey computation, bbtTransliterate maps unknown characters to the replacement character U+FFFD (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:12-14, transliterate(value, { unknown: '�' })), and bbtCleanCitekey then silently strips U+FFFD via BBT_UNSAFE_CITEKEY_CHARS (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:8 includes �; removal at fuzzy.ts:77). This is a silent lossy data transformation in the citekey path (getStandardCitekey, fuzzy.ts:97-100, consumed at src/librarySelectors.ts:99).
  Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy — character transformation in a data-identity path must either succeed deterministically or fail loud; substituting a replacement character and then silently deleting it is the silent-failure category this policy exists to catch ("no silent data transformation").
  Audit anchor: src/utils/fuzzy.ts:8,12-14,76-78. No remediation detail per disposition-round firewall.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:

Code/action: bbtTransliterate now uses transliterate(value,{unknown:''}) (explicit omission mirroring Better BibTeX's documented clean step); U+FFFD removed from the unsafe-char class. The old sentinel path also corrupted output (lost a real letter after a surrogate pair). Proof: red-green tests on untransliterable + literal-U+FFFD names.

## ai-review/general / A type assertion must not substitute for runtime input validation when downstream consumers rely on the asserted type for correct behavior. Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>.

A type assertion must not substitute for runtime input validation when downstream consumers rely on the asserted type for correct behavior. Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>.has() provides no guarantee the value matches any literal member of the union type.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/31)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Claim disposition: Misaligned. The asserted consequence — that an invalid key flows through into `searchText`/`zoteroSortingFn` and produces `undefined`, causing silent incorrect sort/filter — does not occur. `searchKey` throws on any value not in `SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS` (fuzzy.ts:244-247): the `as` cast is applied only to a value already confirmed present by `.has()`, and a miss raises `Error("Unsupported Zotero search field: ...")` rather than returning an invalid key. The path fails loud, not open.
Remediation disposition: Misaligned. Replacing the narrowing with a Zod enum would not change runtime behavior at this site (both fail loud on an unknown key), and `SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS` is declared `new Set<ZoteroSearchKey>([...])` (fuzzy.ts:136), so the compiler already rejects any Set member that drifts from the `ZoteroSearchKey` union — the claimed silent Set/union drift is compile-guarded.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy permits `as` at internal sites where the value is immediately narrowed/guarded; the ban targets type erasure at uncontrolled external boundaries. `searchKey` is an internal narrowing over owned constants that crashes on mismatch, which is the prescribed fail-loud pattern.
Code/action taken: No code change.
Audit anchor: src/utils/fuzzy.ts:134-136, 244-247.

### [General Review][tier2] NON_PROOF_TEST
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 1a3482eed71f891e915b7621789f740a15cfb9d9436638d23cc0884afce6e0c0 -->

**Location:** `src/appCommands.test.ts:6-27`
**Violated invariant:** Every test must prove nontrivial owned behavior. A test that asserts createAppCommands output equals a hard-coded expected ID array proves only documentation-level correspondence at a single point in time, not command structural validity (non-null action, unique ids, known category strings).
**Proof:** `Read src/appCommands.test.ts lines 6-27. The test passes vi.fn stubs to createAppCommands and asserts the returned command IDs equal a literal array. No assertion checks action !== undefined, id uniqueness, category in known union, or name is non-empty.`
**Symptom:** The test asserts command IDs equal an expected array literal. Any change that reorders, duplicates, breaks, or nullifies command properties passes as long as the ID list in source order matches.
**Source:** Lines 17–26: expect(commandIds).toEqual(['reload-db', 'theme-dark', ...]) — the only structural check is array equality against a hard-coded literal.
**Consequence:** A command with duplicate id, null action, or invalid category would pass this test. The test provides false confidence that commands are structurally valid.
**Evidence:** `src/appCommands.test.ts:1-28` (file-read), `src/appCommands.ts:1-72` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body: Accepted, remediation to follow.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted policy-aligned defect; remediation to follow. (Thread remains OPEN. This is the canonical thread for this finding; the duplicate on comment 3448879655 is closed against this one.)

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: True, verified at HEAD. The single test (src/appCommands.test.ts:5-27) passes vi.fn() stubs and asserts only that the returned command IDs equal a hard-coded literal array (lines 17-26). It makes no assertion that command.action fires the intended closure, that ids are unique, that category is in the known union, or that name is non-empty.
  Policy basis: test-guidelines Proof-Only Assertion Policy and Banned Test Shapes [LA-SHAPE]/[LA-EXISTENCE] — an ID-array equality check is a shape/documentation-correspondence assertion that would still pass on a broken implementation (dead callback, miswired closure, duplicate id). It does not exclude a plausible broken implementation at the owned boundary.
  Audit anchor: src/appCommands.test.ts:5-27; src/appCommands.ts. No remediation detail per disposition-round firewall.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Accepted** (independent disposition subagent — disposition only, no remediation detail).

Claim disposition: Aligned, narrowed. There is a real unmet owned proof burden, but not the one stated in full. The grab-bag of `category in known union` / `name non-empty` / `id uniqueness` is largely type-system/internals (the `Command` type already constrains `category`, and `id` ordering/uniqueness is not itself a user-facing behavior). The genuinely unproven, user-facing behavior is **action wiring**: the test injects `setTheme: vi.fn()` etc. but never invokes any command's `action`, so it never proves that selecting a command runs the right dependency with the right argument. Production wires `theme-dark` to `() => setTheme('code-dark')`, `theme-light` to `setTheme('code-light')`, `theme-monokai` to `setTheme('monokai')` (src/appCommands.ts:28-45). An implementation that swapped those theme arguments, or pointed an action at the wrong dep, would still pass the current id-array assertion.

User story the test must prove (no remediation detail): **Selecting a command in the palette invokes the correct action** — e.g. choosing the dark-theme command actually calls `setTheme('code-dark')`, the light command `setTheme('code-light')`, the monokai command `setTheme('monokai')`, and the non-theme commands invoke their respective injected dependency.

Audit anchor: src/appCommands.test.ts:6-27 (only `commands.map(c => c.id)` is asserted; no `action` is ever called) vs src/appCommands.ts:28-45 (the action-to-argument wiring that is currently unproven).

Disposition: left OPEN — real unmet user-story proof burden.
Policy basis: test-guidelines "Proof-Only Assertion Policy" / Line Admission Gate — the id-array line does not exclude a plausibly-broken action-wiring implementation.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — createAppCommands' action wiring (command -> injected effect) is a real, owned, user-facing behavior the test left unproven. It asserted only commands.map(c => c.id) and invoked no action, so a swapped theme argument, a cross-wired dependency, or a dead no-op all passed. (The reviewer's other sub-items — category-in-union, name-non-empty, id-uniqueness — were dropped as type-system/internals, not part of the accepted burden.)

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): the test now invokes each command's action and asserts its effect at the dependency-injection boundary — each theme command applies its own distinct theme (wrong/swapped argument fails), and each non-theme command fires its own dependency while every other channel stays silent (cross-wiring and dead no-op fail). The id/order contract is retained as a smaller secondary fact, not the proof. Production factory (src/appCommands.ts) unchanged. Proof: red->green via an injected wrong-theme-argument defect and a cross-wired export defect (both red on the new tests, green on revert); the old id-only test stayed green under both.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): Spec honored — appCommands.ts has zero net change (the injected-dependency seam is the boundary); assertions sit where the call IS the behavior, guarded by 'no other dependency fired' so they are not proxy-only; no exact-string/name assertions. Declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 53685bf.

### [General Review][tier2] TYPE_ESCAPE_HATCH
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: db50d27c43cd9e96992f2f96e6c7f86aea7c9457dbe72917731045fb8142f727 -->

**Location:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:244-247`
**Violated invariant:** A type assertion must not substitute for runtime input validation when downstream consumers rely on the asserted type for correct behavior. Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>.has() provides no guarantee the value matches any literal member of the union type.
**Proof:** `Read src/utils/fuzzy.ts lines 244-247. The searchKey function asserts value as ZoteroSearchKey instead of validating against a Zod enum or type-narrowing check over the known union members. Check the ZoteroSearchKey type definition to confirm it is a string literal union.`
**Symptom:** Line 245: if (SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS.has(value as ZoteroSearchKey)) return value as ZoteroSearchKey — the as-cast coerces any string present in the Set<string> into the union type without narrowing.
**Source:** The searchKey function at fuzzy.ts:244-247 uses TypeScript as-cast (value as ZoteroSearchKey) instead of validated narrowing like a Zod enum parse (z.enum([...KEY_VALUES])) or a type guard function.
**Consequence:** If SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS (derived from PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS) drifts from the ZoteroSearchKey union — e.g., a key is added but the type definition is not updated — the cast passes at runtime and the value enters searchText and zoteroSortingFn as an invalid key, producing undefined instead of a string and causing silent incorrect sort/filter results.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:238-251` (file-read)

threads.finding_body:
### [General Review][tier2] DUAL_IMPORT_PATH
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: b46114d8a98c3b8ef4b76205ae3f981e5f9404dcde17f35d8d7fbea06668f9ab -->

**Location:** `package.json:21-33`
**Violated invariant:** A dependency must have a single declared import path within the project to guarantee singleton engine state. citeproc is both a direct dependency and a peer dependency of @citation-js/plugin-csl, creating a version-divergence path that would spawn two independent instances.
**Proof:** `Grep citeproc in package.json line 33 (direct dependency). Read src/utils/fuzzy.ts line 9 (import * as CSL from 'citeproc'). Check node_modules/@citation-js/plugin-csl/package.json peerDependencies.citeproc range. Compare resolution paths.`
**Symptom:** citeproc is imported directly in fuzzy.ts (import * as CSL from 'citeproc') and is also used internally by @citation-js/plugin-csl as a peer dependency. Both currently resolve to 2.4.63.
**Source:** package.json line 33 declares citeproc as a direct dependency (~2.4.63). package.json line 21 declares @citation-js/plugin-csl (~0.7.21) which declares citeproc as a peer dependency. fuzzy.ts:9 imports citeproc directly instead of through the plugin.
**Consequence:** If a future npm update resolves one path to a different version (e.g. plugin bumps its peer range while the direct dep stays pinned, or vice versa), two citeproc singleton instances coexist — each with independent processor engine, locale tables, and citation state. Callers then get non-deterministic formatting depending on which instance processes the request.
**Evidence:** `package.json:18-50` (file-read), `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:1-15` (file-read)

**Possible duplicate disposition required:** a prior PR thread has this category/path fingerprint. Compare invariant, source, consequence, and evidence before resolving; do not suppress this finding by similarity alone.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified: package.json:33 declares citeproc "^2.4.63" as a direct dependency, imported at src/utils/fuzzy.ts:1 (import * as CSL from "citeproc"); package.json:21 declares @citation-js/plugin-csl "^0.7.21", which carries citeproc as a peer dependency. Two declared resolution paths for the same engine can diverge on a future npm update and spawn two independent citeproc singletons.
Remediation disposition: Accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: anti-slop split-truth / brittleness (scattered truth, single-source-of-truth). Not contradicted by any maintainer direction in the PR ledger (the ledger addresses UI citation-string fabrication separately, not this singleton-divergence concern).
Audit anchor: package.json:21, package.json:33, src/utils/fuzzy.ts:1.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Rejected.**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: the two declared paths are factually present, but the alleged consequence is speculative future-state, not a concrete present defect, and the direct dependency is a required design choice. The direct dep is pinned to an exact `2.4.63` (no caret), which satisfies plugin-csl's peer range `^2.4.6`, and npm dedupes both to a single installed instance — there is no second engine instance now. The "two singleton instances" scenario is explicitly conditional on a hypothetical future divergent `npm update`. fuzzy.ts:1 imports citeproc directly to call `CSL.parseParticles` (fuzzy.ts:25), a citeproc API that @citation-js/plugin-csl does not re-export, so routing the import through the plugin is not even available — the direct dependency is necessary for this specific feature.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Verification Rule (suppress findings whose consequence only manifests in an imagined future scenario without a present observable mechanism); anti-slop design-choice gate (importing a specific named library API for a specific feature is a user/design decision, not slop).
- Audit anchor: package.json:21 (@citation-js/plugin-csl) and :33 (citeproc 2.4.63 exact-pinned); src/utils/fuzzy.ts:1,25 (`CSL.parseParticles` direct use); node_modules/@citation-js/plugin-csl peerDependencies.citeproc = ^2.4.6 (satisfied, single resolved instance).

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Qualify Zotero keys with their library**

When the local DB contains both personal and group libraries, this query still includes every non-feed library but publishes bare `items.key` as the app-wide item id. Zotero item keys are only unique within a library/group, not across all libraries (see https://groups.google.com/g/zotero-dev/c/Wx86Jf3AL9s), so a key collision gives duplicate TanStack row ids, ambiguous `items.find(...)` selection, and attachment opens that can target the first matching key. Include `libraryID` in the domain id or restrict the query to a single library.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Investigating.

Claim disposition: Plausible, pending DB facts. Verified in code that the top-level query joins `libraries l` and filters only `l.type != 'feed'` (zoteroRepository.ts:329, 341) — it does not restrict to a single library — and publishes bare `items.key` as the app-wide id (zoteroRepository.ts:297, 474). Zotero item keys are unique only within a library/group, so the collision (duplicate TanStack row ids, ambiguous `items.find`, wrong attachment target) is real *iff* the target database actually contains more than one non-feed library (e.g. a group library alongside the personal one).
Remediation disposition: deferred. The disposition depends on whether the owner's Zotero database has multiple libraries; this requires inspecting the real `libraries` table rather than the diff.
Policy basis: A cross-library id collision is an owned-boundary correctness defect, but the precondition (multiple libraries present) must be confirmed against real DB facts before remediation scope is fixed.
Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:297, 329, 341, 474.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Accepted (policy-aligned defect, to be remediated).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: true; defect still present in HEAD.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy domain-identity correctness. The item query includes every non-feed library (`l.type != 'feed'`, src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:341) while publishing bare `i.key AS id` (line 297) as the app-wide item id, and `libraryID` is not part of the domain id. Zotero item keys are unique only within a library/group, so across personal + group libraries a key collision yields duplicate TanStack row ids and ambiguous `items.find(...)` selection.
- Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:297 (bare key as id), :329/:341 (query spans all non-feed libraries), :473-476 (domain id has no libraryID).

This thread is left OPEN as an accepted policy-aligned defect for the remediation round. No fix is prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted with modified remediation.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim: real in principle; the DB has exactly one non-feed library so no collision exists now. Policy basis: fail-loud / no-silent-default / boundary-correctness; grounded in policy + current code.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent): Root concern: the single-user-library identity assumption must be explicit and loud. Code/action: new multiple_libraries contract error; load fails loud on >1 non-feed library. Proof: two-library fixture throws; single-library happy path unaffected.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): Spec honored — declared remediation matches the implementation in commit 7dc3d8e. Commit: 7dc3d8e.
