**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Derive the dev proxy port from config**

If `zotero-gui.config.json` changes `server.port`, `src/server/main.ts` listens on that configured port while Vite continues to proxy every `/api` request to `localhost:3001`. In that configured-port scenario the dev UI's startup, library, attachment, and add-item calls go to the wrong backend and fail; make the proxy read the same config or keep the API port non-configurable.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified: vite.config.ts:16 hardcodes the dev proxy target "http://localhost:3001", while the server binds config.server.port (src/server/main.ts:57, loaded from zotero-gui.config.json). If server.port is changed in config, the dev proxy still targets 3001 and all /api calls reach the wrong backend.
Remediation disposition: Accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: anti-slop split-truth — the configured port is a single source of truth that the dev proxy duplicates; resolve by deriving the proxy target from the same config or making the API port non-configurable. No maintainer direction contradicts this.
Audit anchor: vite.config.ts:15-16, src/server/main.ts:57.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Outdated (superseded by 09d3964).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: defect no longer present in HEAD.
- Policy basis: single-source-of-truth for the configured API port. The finding flagged Vite proxying `/api` to a hard-coded `localhost:3001` while `main.ts` listens on the configured `server.port`. Commit 09d3964 made vite.config.ts read the same config: `const apiPort = loadAppConfig(CONFIG_PATH).server.port` and proxy `/api` to `http://localhost:${apiPort}` (both the `apiPort` derivation and the removal of the `3001` literal land in 09d3964).
- Audit anchor: vite.config.ts:8 (apiPort from config) and vite.config.ts:18 (proxy target). Fixing commit: 09d3964.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Filter trashed child attachments**

When a child attachment is moved to Zotero Trash, its itemID is recorded in `deletedItems`, but this child-attachment query still returns it because it never anti-joins that table. In that scenario the UI continues to show/open a trashed file, and the “No PDF Attachment” view counts a trashed PDF as present; apply the same deleted-item exclusion used for top-level records before attaching children to parents.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. Verified in code: the top-level item query anti-joins/marks deleted rows via `LEFT JOIN deletedItems di` (zoteroRepository.ts:333, 326), but the child-attachment query (zoteroRepository.ts:373-389) has no `deletedItems` join or exclusion. A child attachment moved to Trash (recorded in `deletedItems`) is still returned and attached to its parent, so the UI can show/open a trashed file and the "No PDF Attachment" view counts a trashed PDF as present.
Remediation disposition: accepted, remediation to follow.
Policy basis: Owned boundary logic must apply the same deleted-item exclusion to child attachments as to top-level records.
Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:373-389 (no deletedItems exclusion) vs 333.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Accepted (policy-aligned defect, to be remediated).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: true; defect still present in HEAD.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy correctness — silently surfacing deleted data. The child-attachment query (src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:373-389) joins items but never anti-joins `deletedItems`, unlike the top-level item query which does exclude trashed rows via the `deletedItems` left join (lines 326, 333). A child attachment moved to Zotero Trash is still returned and attached to its parent, so the UI shows/opens a trashed file and the "No PDF Attachment" view miscounts a trashed PDF as present.
- Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:373-389 (child-attachment query, no deletedItems exclusion); contrast lines 326/333 (top-level query excludes deletedItems).

This thread is left OPEN as an accepted policy-aligned defect for the remediation round. No fix is prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted with modified remediation.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim: real (attachment queries did not exclude Zotero Trash). Policy basis: fail-loud / no-silent-default / boundary-correctness; grounded in policy + current code.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent): Root concern: trashed attachments must not surface or count as a present PDF. Code/action: attachment queries anti-join deletedItems. Proof: trashed-attachment fixture dropped and counted PDF-less.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): Spec honored — declared remediation matches the implementation in commit 7dc3d8e. Commit: 7dc3d8e.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Escape reserved DOI suffix characters**

For DOI suffixes containing `?` or `#` (the manifest accepts any non-whitespace suffix after the DOI slash), `encodeURI` leaves those characters unescaped, so `fetch` treats them as a URL query/fragment rather than part of the DOI path. Those valid DOI inputs resolve the wrong identifier or fail before import; escape reserved suffix characters while preserving DOI path slashes.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. The DOI manifest pattern (10\.\d{4,9}/\S+$) admits DOI suffixes containing reserved URL characters such as ? and #, but resolver-plugins/doi.mjs constructs the request with encodeURI, which does not escape ? or #. Verified live: https://doi.org/10.1000/abc?xyz sends abc as the path and ?xyz as a query string, so a DOI whose suffix legitimately contains those characters resolves the wrong identifier at the fetch boundary.
Remediation disposition: Deferred to the remediation round.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy (correctness at the owned URL-construction boundary; an input the gate admits must not be silently mis-encoded). This is a correctness defect, not a security/fallback concern.
Code/action taken: Accepted policy-aligned issue, remediation to follow. No fix detail recorded; thread remains open.
Audit anchor: resolver-plugins/doi.mjs:21 vs resolver-plugins.json doi pattern.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Outdated (superseded by 4a56c60).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: claim was true at the time; defect now fixed in HEAD.
- Policy basis: correct URL boundary encoding. The finding correctly noted `encodeURI` leaves reserved `?`/`#` in a DOI suffix unescaped, letting `fetch` treat them as query/fragment. Commit 4a56c60 replaced `fetch(\`https://doi.org/${encodeURI(doi)}\`)` with `doiRequestUrl(doi)`, which splits namespace/suffix and applies `encodeURIComponent` to the suffix while preserving the namespace slash.
- Audit anchor: `doiRequestUrl` at resolver-plugins/doi.mjs:14-21. Fixing commit: 4a56c60.

threads.finding_body:
### [General Review][tier1] INCONSISTENT_VALIDATION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: c474c2a342b89470b55d885fefc9ae45aac8fac7800bca9c62b6a115f865ab5f -->

**Location:** `src/utils/citation.ts:82-93`
**Violated invariant:** Authorless citation output must be rejected consistently at every user-facing boundary (resolver gate and formatted citation), not only at the resolver validation gate. The project explicitly enforces 'authorless citation is an error' in bibtexParser.ts:42-44 and resolverBibtexSerialization.test.ts:47, but the citation.ts code path silently drops non-author/editor creator types and produces a CSL record with no name fields, which Citation.js renders as a degenerate citation with empty placeholders instead of failing loudly.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'throw new Error' src/utils/bibtexParser.ts && echo '---' && grep -n 'throw' src/utils/citation.ts`
**Symptom:** toFormattedCitation() produces a citation string with empty author placeholder (e.g. '(, n.d.)') for items whose creators have only non-author/editor roles, instead of throwing an error as the project policy requires.
**Source:** cslNamesByRole() in citation.ts:82 only classifies 'author', 'editor', and 'serieseditor' as name-bearing roles; all other creator types (contributor, inventor, bookAuthor, etc.) are silently dropped, and itemToCsl() at line 127 does not check whether the resulting name lists are empty before constructing the CSL record.
**Consequence:** The citation-copy function silently produces bibliographically invalid output for items with non-standard creator roles. The resolver import gate (bibtexParser.ts) rejects authorless entries, but the display-level citation path does not enforce the same contract, creating a behavioral inconsistency in the 'authorless = error' policy.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/citation.ts:82-93` (file-read), `src/utils/bibtexParser.ts:42-44` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Disposition: ACCEPT** (policy-aligned defect; to be remediated in a later round — no fix details here).

Claim verified against the code. `cslNamesByRole` (src/utils/citation.ts:82-92) only classifies `author`/`editor`/`serieseditor`; all other creator roles are dropped. `itemToCsl` (src/utils/citation.ts:141-143) adds `author`/`editor` only when non-empty and never asserts that at least one name field is present, so an item whose creators carry only non-author/editor roles (or no creators) yields a CSL record with no name fields and does NOT throw. `toFormattedCitation`/`toBibTeX` then render a degenerate citation.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy "crash at the boundary / fail loudly" and anti-slop invariant consistency — the "authorless citation is an error" invariant is enforced at the resolver/import gate (src/utils/bibtexParser.ts:42-44) but not at the display citation boundary. The repo owner has stated authorless citations ARE an error. The display path must enforce the same contract.

Audit anchor: src/utils/citation.ts:82-93, 127-160; src/utils/bibtexParser.ts:42-44.

Thread left OPEN pending remediation.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted policy-aligned defect; remediation to follow. (Thread remains OPEN.)

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: True, verified at HEAD. The resolver import gate rejects authorless entries (src/utils/bibtexParser.ts:42-44 throws "BibTeX entry must contain at least one author."), but the display citation path does not enforce the same contract: cslNamesByRole classifies only author/editor/serieseditor and drops all other creator roles (src/utils/citation.ts:82-92), and itemToCsl (src/utils/citation.ts:127-160) constructs the CSL record without checking whether author and editor name lists are both empty. An item whose creators carry only non-author/editor roles yields a CSL record with no name fields, which the renderer turns into a degenerate citation rather than failing as the "authorless = error" policy requires.
  Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy — a contract enforced at one user-facing boundary must not be silently violated at another; this is the silent-degenerate-output category. The project explicitly owns "authorless citation is an error."
  Audit anchor: src/utils/citation.ts:82-92,127-160; src/utils/bibtexParser.ts:42-44. No remediation detail per disposition-round firewall.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — an authorless citation must be rejected at the citation boundary, consistent with the import gate (bibtexParser/parseBibTeXToMetadata) that already treats authorless metadata as an error. The display/copy path did not mirror that rule.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): itemToCsl — the single point both toBibTeX and toFormattedCitation consume — now throws when the item has no author AND no editor, so both copy entrypoints fail loud instead of rendering a nameless citation. An edited volume (editor, no author) remains valid and renders; no placeholder author is fabricated. Proof: user-story tests — translator-only and contributor-only items throw on both entrypoints; authored and editor-only items render. Proven red->green by reverting the throw.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): Spec honored — the throw lives at itemToCsl (the owned boundary), reads the same role classification the renderer uses, and is not weakened to a fallback. Declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: f644c4c.

### [General Review][tier2] SUPPRESSED_ERROR_CLASSIFICATION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: cd26f319188e8d46daf1e2c51dab5169f48782ba739d121364cb37a690c571f9 -->

**Location:** `src/columnModel.ts:77-83`
**Violated invariant:** A malformed localStorage entry must produce a structured, informative error failure (e.g. 'Stored column layout is malformed') rather than a raw SyntaxError from JSON.parse that obscures the actual failure domain. The project's 'crash at the boundary' policy requires loud failure with an informative message.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'JSON.parse' src/columnModel.ts && echo '---' && grep -n 'Error' src/columnModel.ts | head -10`
**Symptom:** If localStorage contains invalid JSON under the columns key, the ErrorBoundary displays 'Unexpected token < in JSON at position 0' instead of 'Stored column layout is malformed' or similar structured diagnostic.
**Source:** readColumnLayout() at columnModel.ts:82 calls JSON.parse(raw) without a try-catch, so any JSON parse error propagates as a raw SyntaxError before Zod validation runs.
**Consequence:** The error message in the ErrorBoundary is a generic JavaScript parser error rather than a project-specific diagnostic, making it harder for users to understand that their persisted column layout is corrupted.
**Evidence:** `src/columnModel.ts:77-83` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Disposition: ACCEPT** (policy-aligned defect; to be remediated in a later round — no fix details here).

Claim verified against the code. `readColumnLayout` (src/columnModel.ts:82) calls `JSON.parse(raw)` directly, before the Zod `StoredColumnLayoutSchema.parse` at line 83. A malformed (non-JSON) persisted value therefore surfaces as a raw `SyntaxError` rather than a structured, classified boundary error identifying a corrupted persisted column layout. The module's own documented contract (src/columnModel.ts:35-40, 73-75) is to fail loudly with a contract-surfacing error; the JSON-syntax path is the one boundary that escapes that classification.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy "crash at the boundary" — the boundary should emit a structured, classified failure for malformed persisted state. This is the permitted boundary-renderer case (structured internal error -> user-facing diagnostic), not a fallback.

Audit anchor: src/columnModel.ts:76-83.

Thread left OPEN pending remediation.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: Misaligned. The finding alleges readColumnLayout() "obscures the actual failure domain" by letting JSON.parse throw a raw SyntaxError. But the code already CRASHES LOUDLY at the boundary on malformed storage (columnModel.ts:82, JSON.parse(raw) with no swallow), which is exactly what the project's "crash at the boundary" policy requires. There is no silent failure, no fallback, no default-reset — the malformed input halts execution with a stack trace. The reviewer's actual complaint is the cosmetic quality of the thrown message ("Unexpected token ..." vs "Stored column layout is malformed"), not a correctness or safety defect.
  Remediation disposition: Misaligned and policy-prohibited. The suggested fix (wrap JSON.parse in try/catch to re-emit a friendlier message) requires a try/catch around normal execution and a stringly error — both hard-banned. test-guidelines Try/Catch Ban: "Do not write try/catch/except/rescue blocks in tests or owned runtime code." bespoke-software-policy Runtime Safety Evasion: try/except of any kind is a fallback construct and is "ALWAYS wrong." bespoke-software-policy also bans error remediation built on message strings; the schema (Zod) is the single source of truth and a Zod/JSON parse failure IS the structured signal.
  Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy "Foundational Principle: Crash at the Boundary" (the crash IS the feature) + Runtime Safety Evasion (try/except always wrong) + test-guidelines Try/Catch Ban. No security or correctness boundary is owned here that the raw crash fails to surface.
  Code/action taken or explicit non-change: No code change. The boundary already fails loud on malformed input.
  Audit anchor: src/columnModel.ts:76-83 (readColumnLayout: localStorage.getItem -> JSON.parse -> StoredColumnLayoutSchema.parse, all fail-loud).

### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: de012989ea1a0453af169a9534d8b82f74d3a2b9754961e72fb6efada9ad9af3 -->

**Location:** `src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts:85-106`
**Violated invariant:** Every diagnostic that writes side effects must provide a cleanup mechanism or be idempotent; timing assumptions must be poll-based rather than hard-coded delays.
**Proof:** `rg 'importBibTeXToZotero' src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts -A 20 | head -25`
**Pattern:** side-effect-without-cleanup + hard-coded-timing
**Why this matters:** Running the diagnostic multiple times accumulates unstoppable stale items in the user's Zotero library. Under I/O load or slow storage, the 1500ms window may be insufficient, causing a false diagnostic failure that the user cannot distinguish from a genuine Zotero connectivity problem.
**Evidence:** `src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts:85-106` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts runAddItemDiagnostic (85-106) writes a real, timestamped item into the live Zotero library on every run and never removes it, so repeated diagnostic runs accumulate diagnostic records in the user real library with no cleanup or identification-for-removal path. This is the owner-affecting half of the finding (durable side effects in real user data without a cleanup/idempotence mechanism).
Remediation disposition: Deferred to the remediation round.
Policy basis: test-guidelines Live Test Data Lifecycle (real-boundary side effects must be markable and cleanable); bespoke-software-policy (do not leave debris in the owner real data). Note: the secondary fixed-1500ms-delay concern fails loud today (it throws if the item is not visible), so the load-bearing accepted concern here is the missing cleanup, not the timing.
Code/action taken: Accepted policy-aligned issue, remediation to follow. No fix detail recorded; thread remains open.
Audit anchor: src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts:85-106 (runAddItemDiagnostic).

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Accepted (policy-aligned defect, to be remediated).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: true; defect still present in HEAD.
- Policy basis: anti-slop hard-coded-timing in a proof path. `runAddItemDiagnostic` writes a real item to the user's Zotero library and then waits a fixed `await delay(1500)` (src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts:94) before reloading it from the eventually-consistent DB. A hard-coded sleep racing an eventually-consistent boundary can produce a false diagnostic failure (item not yet visible) that is indistinguishable from a genuine connectivity problem. (Note: 09d3964 touched this file only for a CONFIG_PATH import refactor and did not alter this logic.)
- Audit anchor: src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts:82-103, specifically the `await delay(1500)` at line 94 before `reloadItem`.

This thread is left OPEN as an accepted policy-aligned defect for the remediation round. No fix is prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the add-item diagnostic uses a hard-coded 1500ms window (false-failure risk under I/O load) and writes a Zotero item with no cleanup (repeat runs accumulate stale items).

Status: BLOCKED / deferred. Remediation (poll-with-deadline visibility check + per-run identifiable item with cleanup) is a real code change, but verifying it end-to-end requires running the live Add-Item diagnostic, which performs a write/delete against the user's real Zotero library. That live write is not authorized in this session, so the fix cannot be proven at its boundary and will not be closed on an unverified change. Tracked as open until an authorized live-diagnostic run can prove the poll + cleanup behavior.

### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: d610f6bb40e1b419e2ac7719b58248825cd35a08aba635b287969cb7ef0a5463 -->

**Location:** `resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:73-73`
**Violated invariant:** Every extracted bibliographic year value must be provably a four-digit integer before it enters BibTeX output; Number.parseInt must not be the sole validation because it silently truncates non-digit suffixes and produces NaN for non-numeric prefixes.
**Proof:** `rg 'Number\.parseInt.*year' resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs`
**Pattern:** silent-degradation-via-incomplete-validation
**Why this matters:** NaN date-parts in CSL-JSON produce non-deterministic BibTeX output (Citation.js may emit @article{..., year = {NaN}} or silently drop the field). A user importing a zbMATH citation gets a corrupted or missing year with no error, degrading citation metadata quality without any diagnostic.
**Evidence:** `resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:73-73` (file-read), `resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:46-54` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Claim disposition: Aligned. resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:73 derives the year via Number.parseInt(text(document.year, ...), 10). The text() helper only asserts a non-empty string; it does not validate a four-digit year. Verified: Number.parseInt("2020abc") silently truncates to 2020, and Number.parseInt("abc") yields NaN, which Citation.js then emits as year = {NaN}. The sibling resolver isbn-lib.mjs:46-53 already enforces the correct contract (/\d{4}/ match with a fail-loud invariant); zbmath lacks the equivalent guard.
Remediation disposition: Deferred to the remediation round.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy (crash/fail-loud at the boundary; no silent degradation of metadata). parseInt is not a validating boundary.
Code/action taken: Accepted policy-aligned issue, remediation to follow. No fix detail recorded; thread remains open.
Audit anchor: resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:73 (year extraction in articleBibTeX).

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Outdated (superseded by 4a56c60).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: claim was true at the time; defect now fixed in HEAD.
- Policy basis: silent-degradation-via-incomplete-validation. The finding flagged `Number.parseInt(text(document.year,...))` as the sole year validation (NaN for "circa 2019", truncation for "2020a"). Commit 4a56c60 introduced a `year(document)` helper that extracts a four-digit year via `/\d{4}/` with a loud invariant before `Number.parseInt`, matching isbn-lib's `recordYear`.
- Audit anchor: `year()` at resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:41-46, used at line 92. Fixing commit: 4a56c60.

### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: ffeef364ff2ecc0196eb82177de963382f1b81e6599548e97464073bffa863b5 -->

**Location:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:173-175`
**Violated invariant:** Subprocess stderr output must not be forwarded to API error responses, as it may contain file paths, environment variables, or sensitive intermediate data from resolver scripts.
**Proof:** `rg 'exited with code.*stderr' src/server/resolverPlugins.ts`
**Pattern:** configuration-leak-via-subprocess-stderr
**Why this matters:** A resolver plugin that prints its configuration, the document URL it fetched, or internal processing details to stderr on failure will leak those details to any caller of the /api/items/from-source endpoint. This is a multi-step information leak: internal diagnostics become external API responses.
**Evidence:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:170-176` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Claim disposition: Misaligned. The finding frames forwarding subprocess stderr into the error (resolver-plugins/resolverPlugins.ts:173-174: `resolver ${plugin.id} exited with code ${code}: ${stderr}`) as an information-leak that must be stripped. That is a generic multi-tenant/enterprise information-disclosure concern. This is single-user bespoke software whose server exists for local-app interop on the user own machine; it owns no information-containment boundary against its own caller. The maintainer has already rejected the parallel network-exposure framing on this PR ("Wrong security model. Bespoke software => user likely WANTS other local apps to interact with it").
Remediation disposition: Misaligned and policy-violating. The proposed remediation (do not forward subprocess stderr) suppresses diagnostic signal at exactly the boundary where a resolver subprocess failed. Under bespoke-software-policy, discarding stderr diagnostics is itself a defect (the 2>/dev/null / signal-suppression prohibition); the failing-resolver stderr is the evidence the owner needs to diagnose the failure, and it is surfaced loudly rather than swallowed.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy (single-user, no owned network/information-containment boundary; fail loud and preserve diagnostic signal; do not suppress stderr). The claim conflicts with the stronger fail-loud / no-diagnostic-suppression policy.
Code/action taken: No code change.
Audit anchor: src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:171-176 (close handler error); error surfaces via src/server/server.ts:149-150 as upstream_boundary_failed, preserving the resolver diagnostic for the local owner.

### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 993fe23b0b7af100ac95cac54eb3ea169468045b8c33f35919c8b5c649df8146 -->

**Location:** `src/utils/citation.ts:2-3`
**Violated invariant:** Module-level side-effect imports must not cause library plugin registration at import time; consumers importing only types or unrelated exports should not trigger Cite plugin initialization.
**Proof:** `rg 'import.*@citation-js' src/utils/citation.ts`
**Pattern:** module-level-side-effect-registration
**Why this matters:** If the Cite constructor is called before the side-effect imports have run, format() calls silently produce empty output (no CSL plugin = no APA rendering). The coupling between module import order and runtime behavior is fragile: tree-shaking, dead-code elimination, or import reordering by a bundler could change the temporal registration order. The test file repeating the imports means the test can pass while production code fails (if the test's registration happens to run first).
**Evidence:** `src/utils/citation.ts:1-4` (file-read), `src/utils/citation.test.ts:1-4` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Disposition: REJECT.**

Claim disposition: Misaligned / false premise. The side-effect imports `import '@citation-js/plugin-bibtex'` and `import '@citation-js/plugin-csl'` (src/utils/citation.ts:2-3) are the OFFICIAL, documented Citation.js registration pattern: format plugins register themselves on the shared `Cite` plugin registry at module evaluation. They are imported in the SAME module as `Cite` (line 1), and `new Cite(...)` is only ever called from `toBibTeX`/`toFormattedCitation` (citation.ts:164-174), both defined in this module. ES module semantics guarantee all top-level statements (including the two registrations) execute before any export of the module is callable, so the "Cite constructor called before the plugins register" ordering hazard cannot occur.

The tree-shaking / dead-code-elimination concern is also false: bare side-effect imports (`import 'x'`) are specifically preserved by bundlers BECAUSE they carry side effects; this is standard, intended ESM behavior, not a fragile coupling. The duplicate imports in the test file are normal — each module that constructs a `Cite` registers the plugins it needs.

Policy basis: known-solution-first — this is the library's own contract/recipe, not bespoke slop; anti-slop design-choice gate — correct use of a declared dependency is not an implementation defect.

Audit anchor: src/utils/citation.ts:1-3, 164-174.

Resolving.

### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 8c7cfc6ca4125a21ba2b1923d944bf51dced9624024ab340a845f808810024dc -->

**Location:** `src/columnModel.test.tsx:101-101`
**Violated invariant:** Test assertions must match exactly the error or behavior they intend to prove; a disjunctive regex with a dead branch allows logic changes to silently pass the test.
**Proof:** `rg 'missing column: doi' src/columnModel.ts`
**Pattern:** disjunctive-dead-branch-assertion
**Why this matters:** A change that removes the length check and replaces it with a different validation strategy (e.g., iterating and collecting all missing columns) could break the error contract but still pass this test because the broad regex swallows both possible outputs. This is a classical proof-laundering pattern: the test appears to prove the error message but actually accepts any of several messages.
**Evidence:** `src/columnModel.test.tsx:95-102` (file-read), `src/columnModel.ts:98-110` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Disposition: ACCEPT** (policy-aligned defect; to be remediated in a later round — no fix details here).

Claim verified against the code. The test input (src/columnModel.test.tsx:97-102) removes `doi` from `columnOrder`, so `stored.columnOrder.length !== contractIds.size` is true at src/columnModel.ts:88 and the order-mismatch error ("...does not match the current column contract.") is thrown FIRST. The `missing column: ${id}` branch at src/columnModel.ts:91-94 is never reached for this input. The disjunctive regex `/missing column: doi|does not match the current column contract/` accepts either message, so the test passes on the line-88 path while naming the unreached `missing column` behavior.

Policy basis: test-guidelines banned-test-shapes [LA-STRING]/proof-laundering — a disjunctive assertion with a dead branch does not exclude a plausible broken implementation and lets a logic change pass silently while masking which path actually fired.

Audit anchor: src/columnModel.test.tsx:101; src/columnModel.ts:88-94.

Thread left OPEN pending remediation.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted policy-aligned defect; remediation to follow. (Thread remains OPEN.)

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: True, verified at HEAD. src/columnModel.test.tsx:95-102 stores a columnOrder with 'doi' filtered out and asserts toThrow(/missing column: doi|does not match the current column contract/). For that input, readColumnLayout reaches the length check first (src/columnModel.ts:88-90) because removing one id changes columnOrder.length, throwing "...does not match the current column contract." The "missing column: doi" alternative (columnModel.ts:91-94) is never reached for this fixture — a dead disjunctive branch. The assertion is also a string-message assertion.
  Policy basis: test-guidelines Banned Test Shapes [LA-STRING] (string error assertion) and the disjunctive-dead-branch proof-laundering pattern — a broad alternation accepts more than one message, so a logic change to the error contract can still pass. The line does not exclude a plausible broken implementation.
  Audit anchor: src/columnModel.test.tsx:95-102; src/columnModel.ts:88-94. No remediation detail per disposition-round firewall.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Rejected** (independent disposition subagent).

Claim disposition: Misaligned. The owned user story here is **"malformed/outdated persisted column state fails loud instead of silently resetting"**, and that story is proven. The assertion is `expect(() => readColumnLayout()).toThrow(/missing column: doi|does not match the current column contract/)`. The load-bearing proof is `.toThrow(...)` — an exception MUST be raised. A silent-reset implementation (the failure mode this test owns) returns `defaultColumnLayout()` and throws nothing, so it fails this test regardless of the regex. The two disjuncts are not a "dead branch": they are the two real fail-loud outputs of the same boundary. Dropping `doi` from a contract-sized order shortens `columnOrder`, which trips the length-cardinality guard at src/columnModel.ts:88-89 ("...does not match the current column contract."); the `missing column: doi` message at src/columnModel.ts:93 fires when cardinality is right but membership is wrong. Both are throws; both are fail-loud.

The message-string is internals, not the user story. Pinning one exact string would (a) couple the test to a wording with no user-facing contract and (b) move toward a banned exact-string assertion (test-guidelines: "NO STRING MATCHING" / banned exact-string shapes). The reviewer's proposed harder pin would pin different internals without raising the epistemic status of any owned behavior.

Policy basis: test-guidelines "Failure semantics" (owned) + banned exact-string-assertion shape; the asserted behavior (throws, does not silently reset) is owned and proven; the message text is not owned.
Audit anchor: src/columnModel.ts:88-94 (both throw branches) vs src/columnModel.test.tsx:95-102 (the `.toThrow` proof). No code change.

### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 97d4d661b56ab5848edda521b4365aeb592bed8b5a3c7991e990cc1a6d6c21c7 -->

**Location:** `src/utils/citation.test.ts:25-29`
**Violated invariant:** A serialization round-trip test must use independent code paths on each side to detect symmetric bugs; feeding output back through the same library with the same plugin set cannot detect bugs shared by both serialization and parsing.
**Proof:** `rg 'function reparse' src/utils/citation.test.ts -A 4`
**Pattern:** symmetric-round-trip-proof-laundering
**Why this matters:** If a future version of @citation-js/plugin-bibtex silently drops the 'page' field during serialization and the parser also ignores 'page' in the same version, the round-trip test passes even though users lose page number data in their BibTeX output. An independent validation path — such as raw regex matching against the BibTeX string for specific field patterns — would detect this.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/citation.test.ts:23-29` (file-read), `src/utils/citation.ts:64-70` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Disposition: ACCEPT** (policy-aligned defect; to be remediated in a later round — no fix details here).

Claim verified against the code. `reparse` (src/utils/citation.test.ts:25-29) feeds `toBibTeX` output back through `new Cite(bibtex).data` — the SAME Citation.js library and bibtex plugin that `toBibTeX` (src/utils/citation.ts:164-166) used to serialize. The field-preservation assertions (citation.test.ts:55-58, 80-90) therefore prove only that Citation.js parsing inverts Citation.js serialization, not that the repository-owned mapping emits each field correctly into the BibTeX text. A bug shared by both directions (e.g. both silently drop `page`) passes undetected.

Policy basis: test-guidelines banned-test-shapes [LA-ROUND-TRIP] — a round-trip assertion using a shared implementation on both sides cannot detect symmetric bugs at the owned boundary.

Audit anchor: src/utils/citation.test.ts:23-29, 55-58, 80-90.

Thread left OPEN pending remediation.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted policy-aligned defect; remediation to follow. (Thread remains OPEN.)

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: True, verified at HEAD. The reparse helper (src/utils/citation.test.ts:25-29) re-parses toBibTeX output via new Cite(bibtex).data — the SAME @citation-js library and plugin set used to serialize (toBibTeX -> new Cite(...).format('bibtex'), src/utils/citation.ts:164-166). A field that the @citation-js/plugin-bibtex serializer drops and the parser also ignores in the same version would survive this round trip undetected.
  Policy basis: test-guidelines Banned Test Shapes [LA-ROUND-TRIP] — "round-trip assertion with shared implementation"; a serialization proof must use an independent path on each side to detect symmetric bugs. As written, the symmetric round trip cannot exclude a shared serialize/parse defect.
  Audit anchor: src/utils/citation.test.ts:23-29; src/utils/citation.ts:164-166. No remediation detail per disposition-round firewall.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Rejected** (independent disposition subagent).

Claim disposition: Misaligned. The owned user story is **"copy BibTeX yields a correct entry for the item"** — concretely, a book serializes as `@book` (not `@article`) with the right title/authors, an article as `@article`, a conference paper as `@inproceedings`. That story is already proven by INDEPENDENT oracles in this same file, not by the symmetric round-trip:

- `entryType(bibtex)` matches the raw BibTeX string with its own regex `/^@(\\w+)\\{/` (src/utils/citation.test.ts:31-35) — a parser-independent path. Tests assert `@book` and `not @article` (line 52-53), `@article` (line 78), `@inproceedings` (line 101).
- The dateless tests assert against the raw string: `not.toMatch(/year\\s*=/)`, no `unknown`, no `N.D.` (lines 118-123) — again parser-independent.

What the repo OWNS is the `ItemType -> CSL type` mapping in itemToCsl (src/utils/citation.ts:16-52, 127-160). That mapping is proven by the independent entry-type oracle plus concrete field-value assertions. What the repo does NOT own is Citation.js's serialize/parse symmetry; a bug shared by `@citation-js/plugin-bibtex` serialize+parse is a dependency-boundary concern, not an owned user story (test-guidelines "Interlocking Rule": test our mapper, not whether the dependency is internally correct). `reparse` is a supplementary field-value check layered on top of the independent entry-type proof, not the sole proof of the user story.

Policy basis: test-guidelines "Interlocking Rule" + "Independent Oracles" — the owned mapping story is proven by the regex entry-type oracle and concrete fields; the shared-library round-trip critique targets a dependency boundary the repo does not own.
Audit anchor: src/utils/citation.test.ts:31-35 (independent regex oracle) and :52-53/:78/:101 (entry-type assertions) vs src/utils/citation.ts:16-52 (the owned mapping). No code change.

### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 3fb1f8b7f7d189f25b0f4a2cefae3dbf6bd751e78176e443bee18c6e27a43eb8 -->

**Location:** `resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:4-8`
**Violated invariant:** A shared utility function (invariant) must be defined in exactly one owned location and imported by all consumers, not independently defined in every plugin and server module, to prevent silent divergence when one copy is corrected and others are not.
**Proof:** `rg '^function invariant' resolver-plugins/ src/server/resolverPlugins.ts src/components/AddByIdentifierModal.tsx`
**Pattern:** identically-structured utility function independently defined in every file of a project, indicating each plugin was generated in isolation without extracting shared imports
**Why this matters:** A developer who fixes a bug in one invariant (e.g., adding stack trace preservation or structured error logging) must find and patch all six copies independently. The identical-but-isolated definitions create a latent divergence surface where a subset of call sites can silently behave differently after a partial update.
**Evidence:** `resolver-plugins/arxiv.mjs:1-5` (diff-snippet), `resolver-plugins/doi.mjs:1-5` (diff-snippet), `resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:4-8` (file-read), `resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:4-8` (file-read), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:78-82` (file-read), `src/components/AddByIdentifierModal.tsx:34-38` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Outdated (superseded by 09d3964).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: defect no longer present in HEAD.
- Policy basis: single-source-of-truth for a shared utility (the finding flagged `invariant` independently defined in every plugin/server module). Commit 09d3964 extracted `invariant` (and `text`) into resolver-plugins/utils.mjs as the single owned definition; the plugins now import it (`import { invariant, text } from './utils.mjs'`) and merely re-export for their own consumers.
- Audit anchor: sole definition at resolver-plugins/utils.mjs:1-5; imports at resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:3, zbmath-lib.mjs:3, arxiv.mjs:1, doi.mjs:1. Fixing commit: 09d3964.

threads.finding_body:
**CRITICAL:** Command-mode filtering is broken — `shouldFilter={false}` disables all cmdk query-to-item matching, so all commands are always shown regardless of user input; no manual replacement filter is applied to commands.

When `mode === 'commands'`, the component renders `commands.map(renderCommandItem)` at line 187-188 without any query-based filtering. Users typing in command mode cannot narrow down the command list.

```suggestion
  shouldFilter={mode === 'commands'}
```

This enables cmdk's built-in filtering for commands (matching on `command.name` via the `value` prop at line 107) while keeping the custom Fzf-based filtering for items.
---
Reply with `@kilocode-bot fix it` to have Kilo Code address this issue.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Outdated (superseded by 09d3964); also a duplicate of thread on comment 3448428489 (same command-mode filtering concern).

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):

### [General Review][tier1] NULL_UNSAFE
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 96ef58c79343cc3c6e28fbad87ab273a27f6acecf85186241d48cb1d3a486fba -->

**Location:** `src/schemas.ts:37-37`
**Violated invariant:** POLICY.NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT — a required domain field that fails preflight checks must not be allowed to pass through the Zod boundary as undefined only to be silently replaced with empty string by every downstream consumer
**Proof:** `grep -rn 'title.*optional\|title.*??' src/ --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' | grep -v node_modules | grep -v '.d.ts'`
**Symptom:** A Zotero DB item with a null title flows through the entire pipeline: RawItemRowSchema accepts it as null, queryLibrary maps null to undefined, ZoteroItemSchema accepts undefined via z.string().optional(), cellValue returns '' for undefined, and buildZoteroSearchDocuments maps undefined to ''.
**Source:** src/schemas.ts:37 declares `title: z.string().optional()` instead of `title: z.string().min(1)`. The zod validation gate is supposed to enforce domain invariants, but .optional() admits undefined, which every consumer then maps to empty string as a runtime default.
**Consequence:** A malformed Zotero DB row with a null title survives as visibly empty cells and an empty-string search index instead of failing loudly at startup with a structured ZoteroDatabaseContractError. This hides data quality issues from the user and makes title-less items undiagnosable without server-side SQL inspection.
**Evidence:** `src/schemas.ts:34-66` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:473-476` (file-read), `src/columnModel.ts:119-130` (file-read), `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:204-208` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
**Accepted (policy-aligned defect, to be remediated).**

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: true; defect still present in HEAD.
- Policy basis: POLICY.NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT (bespoke-software-policy "Crash at the Boundary" / anti-slop fallback-laundering). `ZoteroItemSchema.title` is declared `z.string().optional()` (src/schemas.ts:37). A null DB title is admitted (RawItemRowSchema accepts null at zoteroRepository.ts:27), mapped to undefined (zoteroRepository.ts:476), accepted by the optional Zod field, and then silently replaced with `''` by downstream consumers (e.g. the search index at src/utils/fuzzy.ts:207, `title: item.title ?? ''`). A validation boundary that admits a missing domain-required field and lets consumers substitute an empty-string default hides data-quality issues instead of failing loud.
- Audit anchor: src/schemas.ts:37 (optional title); src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:27,476 (null->undefined); src/utils/fuzzy.ts:207 (undefined->'').

This thread is left OPEN as an accepted policy-aligned defect for the remediation round. No fix is prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted with modified remediation.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim real (an absent title was silently defaulted to '' in the search projection — a runtime default masking a valid data state; title is legitimately optional). Policy basis: no silent runtime default.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent): Root concern: a titleless item must be representable without a fabricated '' title token. Code/action: ZoteroSearchDocument.title is optional; the projection carries item.title as-is; searchText omits an absent field instead of substituting ''. Proof: red-green test that a titleless item is searchable by another field and does not match an empty-title query.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): Spec honored — implementation matches the spec in commit 8c05edc. Commit: 8c05edc.

**Location:** `src/appCommands.test.ts:6-28`
**Violated invariant:** POLICY.NO_MOCK_PROOF — a test that proves only the shape of a command registration list (ID strings) does not prove any behavioral contract: the actions could be dead callbacks, miswired closures, or silently throwing functions and the test would still pass
**Proof:** `cat src/appCommands.test.ts`
**Symptom:** The single test in appCommands.test.ts creates createAppCommands with all-vi.fn() callbacks, then asserts the returned array of command objects has exactly the expected hardcoded ID strings in order. It proves nothing about whether calling command.action() triggers the intended side effect (reload, export, theme change, etc.).
**Source:** src/appCommands.test.ts:6-28 — the test was added to prove the command registration shape for the read-only UI migration but asserts only documentation-level correspondence between input deps and output IDs, not that the actions fire the correct App closures.
**Consequence:** A future refactor that swaps action closures (e.g., 'cols-reset' fires the wrong reset function) or introduces a dead callback would pass this test while breaking the user-visible command palette behavior.
**Evidence:** `src/appCommands.test.ts:6-28` (file-read)

Generated BibTeX must be well-formed — field values wrapped in braces must have braces escaped so the output is always parseable by the BibTeX parser.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Outdated (superseded by 4a56c60 / the citeName gate at HEAD).

Claim disposition: the BibTeX-well-formedness concern at this resolver boundary (field values wrapped in braces must not break the parser) is already discharged at HEAD (53685bf). Line 73 is now const cite = new Cite([ inside bookBibTeX; every author flows through citeName(name) at line 78, and citeName (resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:19-26) throws loudly if a name contains { or }. The documented rationale matches the finding exactly: Citation.js emits CSL name fields by wrapping the raw value in {...} without escaping interior braces, so a brace in a name would produce unbalanced BibTeX; the resolver rejects such input rather than emitting corrupt output. This is fail-loud at the owned boundary, which the no-fallback/no-sanitize policy prefers.

Policy basis: anti-slop / bespoke-software fail-loud-on-invalid-input. The brace-escaping proof burden is met by a rejecting invariant, not a silent sanitizer.

## ai-review/slop / Generated BibTeX must be well-formed — field values wrapped in braces must have braces escaped so the output is always parseable by the BibTeX parser.

Subprocess stderr output must not be forwarded to API error responses, as it may contain file paths, environment variables, or sensitive intermediate data from resolver scripts.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/47)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: misaligned. The finding frames forwarding subprocess stderr to the API error response as an information-disclosure risk ("file paths, environment variables, or sensitive intermediate data"). The cited line src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:175 at HEAD is if (bibtex.length === 0) {; the actual stderr forwarding is at line 171 (fail(new Error(... exited with code ${code}: ${stderr}))), and that error message does reach the HTTP body (server.ts:164 wraps it as ApiError upstream_boundary_failed 502, surfaced at server.ts:195-198). So the mechanism exists, but the security framing does not match what this software is.

Remediation disposition: misaligned. This is a single-user, pre-launch, bespoke local app. The resolver subprocess is the user own node resolver-plugins/*.mjs running on the user own machine, and the API consumer is the user own local UI. There is no untrusted-client boundary and no containment invariant owned here. Surfacing the operator own subprocess stderr to the operator own UI is exactly the fail-loud diagnostic behavior the policy prefers; suppressing it would convert a real upstream failure into an opaque one the user cannot debug.

Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Global Principle 7 (security/sandbox comments require semantic-contract classification: for single-user editor-like software, leak/escape framing is generic security framing unless the app owns a security boundary) and bespoke-software-policy (fail loudly; do not add enterprise hardening for unknown deployment targets). anti-slop: hiding the stderr would be a fail-open/error-suppression move, which is the opposite of what is wanted.

No code change made. Resolving on that basis.

### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 3fb1f8b7f7d189f25b0f4a2cefae3dbf6bd751e78176e443bee18c6e27a43eb8 -->

**Location:** `resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:19-26`
**Violated invariant:** A shared utility function must be defined in exactly one owned location and imported by all consumers, not independently defined in every plugin and server module, to prevent silent divergence when one copy is corrected and others are not.
**Proof:** `grep -rn 'function citeName' resolver-plugins/`
**Pattern:** Two resolver library files (isbn-lib.mjs and zbmath-lib.mjs) each define a private citeName(name) function with identical brace-checking logic (reject author names containing `{` or `}`). Only the error message text differs. The PR extracted invariant, text, and readRawStdin into utils.mjs but left citeName duplicated. Both serve Citation.js brace-escaping as the same downstream consumer, so a future fix to one copy's brace-checking semantics (e.g., which BibTeX-special characters to reject) would silently diverge from the other.
**Why this matters:** If the brace-rejection criterion needs to change (e.g., also reject $, \, % which are BibTeX-special but currently pass through both citeName functions), correcting only isbn-lib.mjs produces silent divergence until a malformed zbMATH author hits the validation gate. The two copies would emit different error messages for the same class of invalid data, eroding the predictability of the fail-loud boundary.
**Evidence:** `resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:19-26` (code), `resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:27-34` (code), `resolver-plugins/utils.mjs:1-19` (code)

**Possible duplicate disposition required:** a prior PR thread has this category/path fingerprint. Compare invariant, source, consequence, and evidence before resolving; do not suppress this finding by similarity alone.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: true at HEAD (53685bf). citeName is defined privately and identically in resolver-plugins/isbn-lib.mjs:19-26 and resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:27-34, with the same brace-validation rule (!value.includes("{") && !value.includes("}")), differing only in the error message text. The shared module resolver-plugins/utils.mjs exports invariant, text, and readRawStdin but NOT citeName, so this one piece of validation truth was left un-deduplicated even though the shared-import pattern is already established in both files.

Remediation disposition: deferred to the remediation round (no fix prescribed here).

Policy basis: anti-slop scattered-truth / brittleness blast-radius (the same concept defined in multiple places, so a future change to one copy of the brace-rejection criterion silently diverges from the other). Both copies serve Citation.js name-field brace handling as the same downstream consumer.

Root concern: the personal-name brace-validation rule is scattered across two resolver libs instead of living in the single owned location (utils.mjs), creating a silent-divergence blast radius at the fail-loud BibTeX boundary.

This thread is left OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the citeName BibTeX brace-rejection invariant was duplicated in isbn-lib.mjs and zbmath-lib.mjs; scattered fail-loud rules can diverge.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): citeName now has one owned definition in resolver-plugins/utils.mjs, imported by both plugins; local copies deleted; rejection invariant unchanged and still proven by the brace-rejection serialization tests.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: da9fec5

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Filter trashed child notes**

When a child note is moved to Zotero Trash, Zotero records that note item in `deletedItems`, but this query still returns every child note with a parent. In that scenario the UI continues to surface the trashed note in note counts, expanded rows, the inspector, and note search even though the corresponding attachment query below now excludes deleted child items; apply the same deleted-item exclusion here before attaching notes to their parents.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: true; defect present in HEAD (53685bf). The child-notes query (src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:406-412) returns every child note with a parentItemID and does NOT anti-join deletedItems, whereas both attachment queries DO (line 428 and line 447: AND a.itemID NOT IN (SELECT itemID FROM deletedItems)). Commit 7dc3d8e added the deletedItems anti-join to attachments only ("attachment queries anti-join deletedItems"); notes were not given the same exclusion. A child note moved to Zotero Trash is recorded in deletedItems but is still attached to its parent here, so it surfaces in note counts, expanded rows, the inspector, and note search while the trashed attachment beside it is correctly excluded.
- Remediation disposition: not specified here (role B does not prescribe fixes).
- Policy basis: this is a repository-owned boundary correctness defect (the queryLibrary mapper produces user-visible state that diverges from Zotero Trash semantics), not an enterprise/edge concern; it is asymmetric with the already-applied attachment trash exclusion, which is the owned contract.

Root concern: trashed child notes (recorded in deletedItems) are surfaced to the UI because the notes query lacks the deletedItems exclusion that the attachment queries already enforce.

Thread left OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the child-notes query lacked the deletedItems anti-join the attachment queries apply, so trashed child notes surfaced.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The notes query now applies the same NOT IN (SELECT itemID FROM deletedItems) anti-join, symmetric with attachments. Proven: a trashed child note is dropped while a non-trashed sibling is kept; live zotero-doctor loads the library through this path.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: e28b591.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Strip query before arXiv .pdf suffix**

This URL pattern accepts common arXiv PDF links with query strings, such as `https://arxiv.org/pdf/2401.01234.pdf?download=1`, but `resolver-plugins/arxiv.mjs` removes the `.pdf` suffix before it drops `?`/`#`. For those accepted inputs the normalized ID becomes `2401.01234.pdf`, so the resolver asks arXiv for `/bibtex/2401.01234.pdf` instead of `/bibtex/2401.01234` and the import fails. Either narrow this accepted URL shape or strip query/fragment before removing the PDF suffix.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted with modified remediation.

Claim disposition: true at HEAD (53685bf). The manifest arxiv-url pattern (resolver-plugins.json:53, ^https?://arxiv\.org/(?:abs|pdf)/[^\s]+$) accepts https://arxiv.org/pdf/2401.01234.pdf?download=1 (confirmed by running the pattern). resolver-plugins/arxiv.mjs applies .replace(/\.pdf$/i, "") (anchored to end-of-string) at line 9 BEFORE .split("?")[0] at line 10. For that accepted input the raw string ends in ?download=1, so the .pdf$ anchor does not match and the suffix survives; .split("?")[0] then yields 2401.01234.pdf. The resolver requests https://arxiv.org/bibtex/2401.01234.pdf and the import fails. Reproduced: the full normalization chain returns "2401.01234.pdf" for that input.

Remediation disposition: the underlying defect is real; the precise policy-compatible fix (narrow the accepted shape vs. reorder normalization) is for the remediation round. No fix prescribed here.

Policy basis: test-guidelines boundary-interpretation correctness. The accepted-input contract and the input-normalization order diverge at an owned boundary, so a contract-valid arXiv PDF URL with a query string deterministically fails to resolve.

Root concern: a URL shape the manifest advertises as accepted is mangled by the resolver normalization order, producing an identifier the upstream BibTeX endpoint cannot serve.

This thread is left OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true (accept-with-modified-remediation) — the manifest accepts arXiv PDF URLs with a query/fragment, but arxiv.mjs stripped .pdf before the query, leaving the suffix and a malformed id.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): arxiv normalization splits ?/# first, then strips the .pdf suffix; the extracted arxivIdFromInput is proven over every accepted URL form (bare, /abs, /pdf, /pdf/.pdf, /pdf/.pdf?download=1, #fragment, old-style).

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: da9fec5.

### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 32b865e5d03fe82870c2714b82df60b955a812c3ce304b6c13df2790ebaffc72 -->

**Location:** `src/App.tsx:198-207`
**Violated invariant:** Structured error responses from the server (with error kind and message) must be forwarded to the user, not replaced with a client-generated generic message that discards the server's classification.
**Proof:** `rg -A 10 'const openAttachment' src/App.tsx`
**Pattern:** error-suppression
**Why this matters:** The server carefully classifies errors into kinds (attachment_not_found, upstream_boundary_failed, attachment_path_missing) with descriptive messages. The client discards every structured detail, leaving the user with a generic HTTP status number that disappears in 3 seconds. Debugging a failed attachment open requires reading the server log.
**Evidence:** `src/App.tsx:198-207` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: true at HEAD (53685bf). The openAttachment handler moved from the cited 198-207 to App.tsx:138-147, but the defect persists: on a non-ok response it throws new Error(Attachment open failed with HTTP ${response.status}) and the .catch shows only that client-generated string. It never reads the structured JSON error body.

Root concern (user story): the server route POST /api/attachments/:id/open returns structured ApiError bodies the user needs to act on -- attachment_not_found (404), attachment_path_missing (400), upstream_boundary_failed (502) (src/server/server.ts:16-21,88,93,186). The client discards every structured kind/message and surfaces a bare HTTP status that vanishes with the toast, so a user cannot tell why the open failed without reading the server log.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy fail-loudly / diagnostic-signal-suppression (a structured upstream error must reach the owner, not be collapsed into a generic status); anti-slop error-laundering. ApiErrorResponseSchema (src/schemas.ts:83-88) already defines the structured kind+message contract the client is failing to forward.

No remediation prescribed here. Thread left OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — openAttachment discarded the server's structured error and showed a bare HTTP status.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): On failure the body is parsed via the existing ApiErrorResponseSchema and error.message is surfaced; a malformed body fails the parse (fail loud). Proven: the toast shows the server's message, not a status code.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 8d3d561

threads.finding_body:
### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: e4a0029141248cfba4463ee99330f55e1060d5a92d739d33f2d78045f551d7f7 -->

**Location:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.test.ts:169-190`
**Violated invariant:** Each independent failure mode of a resolver plugin process (timeout, output limit, empty output, invalid BibTeX, multiple entries) must have its own named test so that a failure in one mode does not hide failures or regressions in others.
**Proof:** `rg -n 'rejects timeout' src/server/resolverPlugins.test.ts`
**Pattern:** coarse-grained-test-grouping
**Why this matters:** When the test breaks, the developer must bisect the five failure modes manually because the single test name and single assertion framework cannot indicate which mode failed. The tight grouping also means that adding a sixth mode to the list is a low-friction decision that further dilutes diagnostic value.
**Evidence:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.test.ts:169-190` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: misaligned (test methodology/granularity critique, not an unmet proof burden). At HEAD (53685bf), src/server/resolverPlugins.test.ts:169-190 groups five resolver-process failure modes (timeout, stdout byte limit, empty output, invalid BibTeX, multiple entries) in one it() block. Each mode already has its OWN distinct substantive assertion against a real spawned subprocess: await expect(runResolverPlugin(...)).rejects.toThrow(/timed out/), /stdout byte limit/, /empty BibTeX/, /Invalid BibTeX/, /exactly one/ respectively, each with a representative fixture script. None of the five owned failure-semantics behaviors is unproven.

Remediation disposition: misaligned. The finding asks to split one well-targeted test into five separately-named it() blocks purely for failure-bisection ergonomics. Applying the user-story lens: the test is a proof burden for user-facing failure semantics, and all five are already proven at the real subprocess boundary. The request pins test methodology/naming, not behavior, and would push toward count-driven proliferation that test-guidelines explicitly disfavors ("prefer fewer tests, each tied to a distinct owned guarantee"). Splitting excludes no plausible broken implementation the current test admits.

Policy basis: test-guidelines Evidence Rule (more tests are not more proof; do not optimize for raw count or coarse-vs-fine grouping when each claim is already discriminating) and the user-story lens (reject findings that critique methodology rather than an unmet user-story proof burden). The five .rejects.toThrow assertions are proof-bearing failure-semantics checks, not policing assertions.

No code change made. Resolving on that basis.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Remove immutable from the live Zotero DB URI**

This default tells SQLite the database file is immutable. When the live Zotero DB is in WAL mode and Zotero has committed rows that are still only in `zotero.sqlite-wal`, a new `DatabaseSync('file://…?immutable=1')` reader does not read those frames, so `/api/library` can stay stale or even fail against an older main file right after Zotero writes despite opening a fresh connection. Use a normal read-only URI such as `mode=ro`, or read from an explicitly copied/checkpointed snapshot instead of the live WAL database.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: True; on-point and present at HEAD 53685bf. zotero-gui.config.json:6 sets databaseUri to file:///home/dzack/Zotero/zotero.sqlite?immutable=1. The immutable=1 URI flag tells SQLite to assume the main database file never changes and to skip locking and WAL handling. The live Zotero DB runs in WAL mode, so committed rows can exist only in zotero.sqlite-wal; an immutable reader never reads those WAL frames. /api/library can therefore return stale data (or read an older main-file state) right after Zotero writes, even on a freshly opened connection. This is a real data-correctness defect about a real config value, not a portability nit and not a mislocated scanner rule.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy fail-loud / read-correct-data-from-the-boundary -- the reader must observe the actual committed state of the live DB, and immutable=1 silently hides newer committed rows (a silent-wrong-output failure mode, the category this policy exists to catch). The Verification Rule is satisfied: stale/incorrect library data is a concrete problem for the owner on their actual machine right now.

Root concern: the live-DB read path silently observes a pre-WAL-checkpoint snapshot, so the library view can disagree with what Zotero has actually committed.

Status: OPEN -- accepted policy-aligned defect for the remediation round. No fix prescribed here.

Audit anchor: zotero-gui.config.json:6 (zotero.databaseUri, the ?immutable=1 flag).

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected (on investigation).

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): the finding's premise — that immutable=1 hides WAL-committed data — assumes the live DB is in WAL mode.

Investigation (remediation subagent, by reality): on this machine the live Zotero DB is in rollback-journal mode, NOT WAL — the header journal-mode bytes read 1/1, a zotero.sqlite-journal file is present, and there is no -wal/-shm. Probing the running DB read-only: immutable=1 opens and reads (committed pages live in the main file, so no staleness); mode=ro / readOnly:true / timeout all return SQLITE_BUSY (database is locked) because Zotero holds the file. Removing immutable=1 would make the app unable to read the library while Zotero is open — defeating its purpose — with no staleness benefit, since the DB is not WAL. No change made; the claim does not hold for this configuration.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Strip ZBMath AN prefixes case-insensitively**

The server accepts resolver inputs with case-insensitive patterns, so `AN:0139.24606` passes validation, but this branch only strips the lowercase `an:` prefix. For that accepted input the resolver searches ZBMath for `an:AN:0139.24606` and fails before import; normalize or strip the prefix case-insensitively before building the search query.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: true at HEAD (53685bf). The server matches manifest patterns case-insensitively (pluginAcceptsInput at src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:114 uses new RegExp(descriptor.pattern, "i")), so the zbmath-an input AN:0139.24606 passes validation and reaches the resolver. But parseZblNumber at resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs:16-17 only strips the lowercase literal prefix "an:" (input.startsWith("an:")). For input AN:0139.24606 it returns AN:0139.24606 unchanged; resolver-plugins/zbmath.mjs then builds search_string an:AN:0139.24606, which fails the nr_total_results === 1 invariant before import. Verified by reproducing the regex acceptance and the prefix-strip logic.

Remediation disposition: deferred to the remediation round (no fix prescribed here).

Policy basis: test-guidelines boundary-interpretation correctness / bespoke fail-loud-on-real-input. The accepted-input contract (case-insensitive) diverges from the normalization logic at an owned boundary, so a validated user input deterministically fails to resolve.

Root concern: the resolver accepts an input shape (case-insensitive AN prefix) that its own normalization cannot handle, so a contract-valid input never imports.

This thread is left OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the server accepts AN: case-insensitively but parseZblNumber stripped only lowercase an:, so AN:1234 built an:AN:... and never resolved.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): parseZblNumber strips /^an:/i in both bare and ?q= URL forms (URL form still asserts presence); AN: in any case yields byte-identical BibTeX to an:.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: da9fec5

## ai-review/slop / Generated BibTeX must be well-formed — field values wrapped in braces must have braces escaped so the output is always parseable by the BibTeX parser.

Every dependency declared in package.json must be provably imported or required at its declared boundary before a PR that introduces it is complete. A dependency with zero import sites is dead infrastructure: it wastes install time, increases lock-file surface area, expands the attack surface for supply-chain vulnerabilities, and misleads future maintainers about what the project actually depends on.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/64)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: False, and the scanner rule header is mislocated. The body alleges this dependency has zero import sites (dead infrastructure). The cited location package.json:38 at HEAD 53685bf is "fzf": "^0.5.2". That dependency IS imported and used at src/utils/fuzzy.ts:3 (import { Fzf } from "fzf"), and Fzf is constructed in the fuzzy-ranking paths of that module. So the dead-dependency claim is factually false for the cited line. The rule header on this thread (Generated BibTeX must be well-formed -- braces escaped) is a mislocated github-advanced-security BibTeX rule reposted onto a package.json dependency line where no BibTeX generation exists. No code change.

replies.author: dzackgarza

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Duplicate of src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:96 (thread PRRT_kwDOS-nFnM6LHi-N, comment 3449054713).

This security-tab finding ("A validated resource must carry its derived work product forward instead of discarding and recomputing it on every consumer invocation") is the generic-header restatement of the same compiled-regex concern: line 98 compiles the accepted-input pattern at load time and discards it, while pluginAcceptsInput (line 114) recompiles it per call. The canonical thread (resolverPlugins.ts:96) was Rejected as a micro-optimization with no correctness defect and no measured/reproduced user-visible problem (pr-feedback-triage Global Principle 6); the path is per-user-action, dominated by subprocess spawn and network fetch, not a hot loop. ## ai-review/slop / A single owned capability (fuzzy string matching) should use one dependency, not two overlapping libraries with different APIs and bundle costs.

A single owned capability (fuzzy string matching) should use one dependency, not two overlapping libraries with different APIs and bundle costs.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/65)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Outdated (superseded by 61f121a).

Claim disposition: no longer applicable at HEAD (53685bf). The finding alleges the owned fuzzy-matching capability uses "two overlapping libraries with different APIs and bundle costs." 61f121a consolidated fuzzy matching to a SINGLE engine: fuse.js was removed from package.json (the only fuzzy dependency now is fzf ^0.5.2), there are zero fuse imports anywhere under src/, and src/utils/fuzzy.ts:3 is the sole fuzzy-engine import (import { Fzf } from 'fzf'). The line cited (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:4) is now import { transliterate } from 'transliteration', which is citekey transliteration, not a second fuzzy-match library. Both ranking modes (rankDocuments, rankPaletteDocuments) run on Fzf.

Policy basis: anti-slop one-capability/one-dependency — the concern is satisfied; the second overlapping library is gone. No open burden remains.

Resolving as outdated.

threads.finding_body:
## ai-review/slop / Character transformation in citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail loud with an error that prevents silent data loss. The replacement-character fallback followed by silent r

Character transformation in citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail loud with an error that prevents silent data loss. The replacement-character fallback followed by silent removal at fuzzy.ts:77 violates the 'no silent data transformation' invariant.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/61)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Outdated (superseded by 8c05edc / 61f121a).

Claim disposition: no longer applicable at HEAD (53685bf). The finding targets a "replacement-character fallback followed by silent removal at fuzzy.ts:77": a U+FFFD sentinel emitted for untransliterable characters and then stripped via a U+FFFD-containing unsafe-character class. That mechanism no longer exists. Current bbtTransliterate (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:29-31) is transliterate(value, { unknown: '' }) wrapped in fold-to-ascii — an explicit, named omission at the transliteration step. There is no U+FFFD sentinel: BBT_UNSAFE_CITEKEY_CHARS (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:7) does not include �, and there is no replacement-character round-trip anywhere in the file. The cited "fuzzy.ts:77" is now bbtAuthorsAlpha (creator-initials logic), unrelated to transliteration.

On the deeper "succeed deterministically or fail loud, no silent data loss" framing: the surviving behavior is the documented, deterministic BBT-mirroring OUTCOME (untransliterable characters are omitted from a DERIVED citekey token), not a silent sentinel round-trip; the two lossy paths the finding attacked (surrogate-pair corruption of the surrounding prefix, and indistinguishable stripping of a literal U+FFFD in source data) were specifically eliminated by the unknown:'' approach (see the comment at src/utils/fuzzy.ts:11-27). The fallback-sentinel construct the finding flagged is gone.

Policy basis: anti-slop no-silent-data-transformation — the silent-sentinel-fallback artifact is removed and replaced by an explicit deterministic omission; no open burden remains for the cited mechanism.

Resolving as outdated.

threads.finding_body:
### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 70e71118e0115c52547a7db4179b6f6fb7e89fdd9205e25ee8aa030558943682 -->

**Location:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:96-99`
**Violated invariant:** A validated resource must carry its derived work product forward instead of discarding and recomputing it on every consumer invocation
**Proof:** `rg 'new RegExp' src/server/resolverPlugins.ts --line-number`
**Pattern:** compiled-regex validation discards the compiled regex; hot-path recompiles it on every call
**Why this matters:** Each resolver input check compiles the pattern regex from scratch instead of using a cached compiled regex, incurring O(n) regex compilation per invocation with no benefit
**Evidence:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:96-99` (file-read), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:113-115` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: factually true but the harm is purely a micro-optimization. At HEAD (53685bf), src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:98 compiles each accepted-input pattern once at load time (new RegExp(input.pattern)) as a fail-loud manifest-validation gate and discards the compiled value, and pluginAcceptsInput at line 114 recompiles the same patterns with the "i" flag on every call. The two compile sites are not redundant: line 98 validates the pattern at startup, while line 114 must add case-insensitivity, so it cannot reuse line 98 result as-is.

Remediation disposition: misaligned. The finding stated harm is "O(n) regex compilation per invocation with no benefit" — a performance claim, not a correctness defect (both compilations are behaviorally equivalent). The "hot path" characterization is wrong: pluginAcceptsInput is called at most twice per user-initiated import (server.ts:78 and resolverPlugins.ts:132), a path that immediately spawns a child process and performs network fetches — orders of magnitude slower than compiling about 6 trivial regexes. There is no measured or reproduced user-visible problem. Caching the compiled regex would ADD derived state to carry forward, not remove complexity.

Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Global Principle 6 (micro-optimizations are rejected by default; incorporate only when the change improves objective correctness, removes complexity, fixes a measured/reproduced user-visible problem, or is essentially zero-risk and semantics-preserving — none hold here). anti-slop: not a correctness or blast-radius defect.

No code change made. Resolving on that basis.
