`src/utils/fuzzy.ts:189` still describes advanced search as using "Fuse threshold + token all/any ranking", but this diff removes `fuse.js`, `fuzzyThreshold`, and all Fuse usage from advanced search. Update the comment to describe the current fzf token all/any behavior so the docs do not preserve the old implementation contract.
---
Reply with `@kilocode-bot fix it` to have Kilo Code address this issue.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted.

Claim disposition: true; live at HEAD (53685bf). The JSDoc at src/utils/fuzzy.ts:188-191 still describes advanced search's default fields as "Fuse threshold + token all/any ranking." 61f121a removed fuse.js entirely (no fuse in package.json; the only fuzzy dep is fzf ^0.5.2) and removed the fuzzyThreshold setting; both ranking modes now run on Fzf (rankDocuments and rankPaletteDocuments both construct new Fzf, src/utils/fuzzy.ts:281,304). So the comment documents a removed implementation contract — there is no Fuse and no threshold.

Policy basis: anti-slop brittleness / scattered-stale-truth + bespoke-software-policy comments-are-signal. A comment that names a removed mechanism is a misleading source of truth for the next reader/agent about how the default fields are ranked. This is the doc-staleness defect, not a behavioral bug; it is genuine but trivial.

Root concern (no fix prescribed): the prose contract for the default-searchable-fields / advanced-search ranking must describe the engine that actually runs (fzf token all/any), not the removed Fuse-threshold path.

Status: OPEN for the remediation round. No remediation prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS JSDoc referenced a removed Fuse threshold and claimed two engines, stale since the single-fzf consolidation.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): JSDoc rewritten to describe both modes on fzf (palette: whole-query subsequence; advanced: per-token AND/OR); no stale Fuse/threshold reference remains.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Hide citation actions for non-author creator roles**

When a supported Zotero type has only name-bearing roles other than author/editor, such as a patent with `inventor` or a film with `director`, this predicate returns true and the UI offers the BibTeX/APA copy actions. `itemToCsl()` then drops those roles in `cslNamesByRole()` and throws the new “no author or editor” error, so clicking the advertised citation action raises an uncaught UI error instead of being unavailable or handled. Make the affordance predicate match all throw conditions, or map the relevant Zotero creator roles into CSL names.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted.

Claim disposition: true; live at HEAD (53685bf). isCitable (src/utils/citation.ts:131-133) returns true purely from ITEM_TYPE_TO_CSL[itemType] !== null — a TYPE-only check. itemToCsl additionally throws when author.length === 0 && editor.length === 0 (src/utils/citation.ts:160-164), a CREATOR condition that isCitable does not see. So a citable-TYPE item whose only creator is a non-author/non-editor role (e.g. a patent with inventor, a film with director, a book with only a translator) passes isCitable, the UI advertises the copy action, and the render throws.

The throw is uncaught at the call sites: src/App.tsx:128-135 calls toFormattedCitation(selectedItem) synchronously after the isCitable gate, before any .then/.catch; src/components/InspectorPanel.tsx:90-95 gates copyBibtex on isCitable(item) and then calls toBibTeX(item) with no catch. In both, the advertised action raises an uncaught error rather than being unavailable or handled — exactly as the finding states.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy fail-loud-at-the-correct-boundary + anti-slop affordance/throw mismatch. The affordance predicate must match all throw conditions of the rendering path. Introduced by f644c4c (isCitable + the new creator-condition throw added together but not reconciled).

Root concern (no fix prescribed): the predicate the UI consults to decide whether to OFFER a citation action must be true on exactly the inputs for which the citation path succeeds; it must not be satisfiable by inputs that make the render throw.

Status: OPEN for the remediation round. No remediation prescribed here. (Shares root concern with the citation.ts:82 thread, which covers the role-drop site / misleading-message facet; both are accepted independently because each names a distinct true sub-claim.)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — isCitable gated on item TYPE only but itemToCsl also throws on the creator condition; a citable-type item with only a translator passed the affordance then threw uncaught.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): isCitable = hasCitableType && hasNameBearingCreator (the exact inverse of every itemToCsl throw condition), so the UI never offers a throwing copy action; direct-call throw unchanged. Proven: a translator-only citable-type item is isCitable=false and still throws on direct render.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: fc91461

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Encode DOI cells before opening doi.org**

When the DOI column is visible and a stored DOI suffix contains reserved URL characters such as `?` or `#`, interpolating the raw cell value into `https://doi.org/${cellVal}` makes the browser treat the suffix tail as a query/fragment instead of DOI path data. Those valid DOI values open the wrong identifier even though the resolver path now preserves them by encoding the suffix; the table click handler should use the same DOI URL construction instead of raw concatenation.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: true at HEAD (53685bf). src/components/LibraryTable.tsx:104 builds the DOI link by raw interpolation -- window.open(https://doi.org/${cellVal}) -- with no encoding of the cell value.

Root concern (user story): a stored DOI suffix can legitimately contain URL-reserved characters (e.g. ? or #). Interpolated raw, the browser treats the suffix tail as a query/fragment, so clicking a valid DOI cell opens the wrong identifier -- a user-visible correctness failure on real data, not speculative hardening. This is also scattered truth: the resolver path already preserves such DOIs by encoding the suffix (hardened in 4a56c60), but the table click handler re-derives the doi.org URL by string concatenation and diverges from that encoded construction.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy (behave correctly on real boundary data; do not silently send the user to the wrong resource); anti-slop scattered-truth/blast-radius (a single encoded DOI-URL construction should be the one source, not duplicated raw concatenation).

No remediation prescribed here. Thread left OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the DOI cell built the doi.org URL by raw interpolation, so a reserved char in the suffix opened the wrong identifier.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The DOI cell uses a frontend doiUrl util that percent-encodes the suffix and preserves the namespace slash (mirrors the resolver's rule without importing resolver-subprocess code into the browser bundle). Proven: a reserved char is %-encoded; a plain DOI is unchanged.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 8d3d561

## ai-review/general / A type assertion must not substitute for runtime input validation when downstream consumers rely on the asserted type for correct behavior. Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>.

POLICY.NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT — a required domain field that fails preflight checks must not be allowed to pass through the Zod boundary as undefined only to be silently replaced with empty string by every downstream consumer

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/67)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: false / mislocated at HEAD (53685bf). The header is the generic POLICY.NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT rule -- a required domain field that fails preflight must not pass through the Zod boundary as undefined only to be silently replaced with empty string by every downstream consumer -- anchored on src/schemas.ts:37, which is a blank line immediately after CollectionSchema closes (line 36). The finding names no specific field, and the mechanism it describes is not realized at this anchor:

- The only optional field on the schema this anchor terminates (CollectionSchema, lines 27-36) is key: z.string().optional(). That optionality is correct and intentional: the synthetic all / My Library root view has no real Zotero key (comment at schemas.ts:32-35).
- Crucially, key is NOT silently defaulted to empty string downstream. The import boundary fails LOUD: selectModalImportCollections throws Selected collection ... has no resolvable Zotero collection key when a selected real collection has an undefined/empty key (src/libraryViews.ts:64). That is the opposite of the silent-empty-string default the invariant prohibits.

So the specific NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT mechanism (undefined -> silently replaced with empty string by every consumer) does not exist for any field at this anchor. (The separate desire to make non-synthetic collection key compile-time-required is a distinct type-level concern living on the NULL_UNSAFE thread at zoteroRepository.ts:550, not this NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT one.) A generic policy header reposted on a blank line, whose stated mechanism is contradicted by the actual fail-loud boundary, is not an actionable repository-owned defect. Resolving.

POLICY.NO_MOCK_PROOF — a test that proves only the shape of a command registration list (ID strings) does not prove any behavioral contract: the actions could be dead callbacks, miswired closures, or silently throwing functions and the test would still pass

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:

Claim disposition: false against current code; was true against the prior id-only test. The finding (POLICY.NO_MOCK_PROOF) alleges the test proves only the shape of a command registration list (ID strings) and would still pass if the actions were dead callbacks, miswired closures, or silently throwing functions. That described the earlier appCommands.test.ts. Commit 53685bf rewrote the file to prove action wiring behaviorally:

- It drives createAppCommands with a distinct spy per injected dependency (the injected deps ARE the user-facing effects: reload, export, citation copy, theme, show/reset columns), invokes each commands action(), and asserts the right dependency fired exactly once with the right argument while every other dependency stayed silent (appCommands.test.ts:58-119). A dead no-op action fails (the expected spy is never called); a cross-wired action fails (the wrong spy fires); a swapped theme argument fails (toHaveBeenCalledWith(theme), lines 67, 85-87).
- The id/order list assertion is demoted to one separate test explicitly annotated as a smaller, separate fact ... not the proof of behavior (lines 121-136).

So the broken implementations the finding names (dead callback, miswired closure, silently throwing function) would NOT pass the current suite. These spies sit on the actual effect channels (the dependency boundary), so they assert real wiring, not an internals/call-count proxy. The finding targets a test that no longer exists. Resolving as outdated.

## ai-review/general / A request with a malformed JSON body sent to any POST endpoint must return HTTP 400 with kind invalid_request, not HTTP 500 with kind internal_error. The error middleware must distinguish client-level

A request with a malformed JSON body sent to any POST endpoint must return HTTP 400 with kind invalid_request, not HTTP 500 with kind internal_error. The error middleware must distinguish client-level parse errors from server bugs.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/72)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Outdated (superseded by commit 7dc3d8e).

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: already satisfied at HEAD (53685bf). The invariant ("a malformed JSON body must return HTTP 400 invalid_request, not HTTP 500 internal_error; the error middleware must distinguish client-level parse errors from server bugs") is implemented. express.json() rejects an unparseable body with an error carrying { type: "entity.parse.failed", status: 400 }; isBodyParseFailure (src/server/server.ts:112-115) classifies it by that STRUCTURAL identity, and classifyError (lines 117-125) maps it to { kind: "invalid_request", status: 400 } while genuine non-ApiError faults stay 500. Classification is by structure, never by message string (no exact-string matching). This was the 7dc3d8e change ("a malformed JSON body is classified by its structural identity ... into a 400 invalid_request instead of 500").
- Proof at the real boundary: src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:274-285 POSTs a literally malformed JSON body to the live express app and asserts kind invalid_request with status 400; the control at lines 287-295 asserts a genuine internal fault still classifies 500 internal_error. Both exercise the real middleware, not a helper branch.
- Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Loop and Convergence (finding already discharged by a prior commit -> Outdated). The reclassification uses a typed structural read, not a stringly error match, so it does not introduce an EXACT-STRING red flag.

No code change. Resolving.

## ai-review/general / Expensive side effects (localStorage read, JSON.parse, Zod schema validation, column key iteration) in readStoredColumns must not execute on every React component render. The lazy initializer form of 

When all search fields are disabled and a non-empty query exists, the user expects zero filtering (no fields to filter on = show everything) rather than zero results. An empty search-keys list should short-circuit to the full items list, not to an empty list.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/70)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Investigate before action.

Claim disposition: needs investigation. The code is as described: when a non-empty query exists but no search fields are enabled, both rankDocuments (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:279) and filterZoteroItems (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:326-327) short-circuit keys.length === 0 to return [] (empty result). The reviewer asserts the user EXPECTS the opposite — "no fields to filter on = show everything" — and wants an empty-keys short-circuit to the full items list.

Why not accepted as a defect: which semantics is correct for "non-empty query + all fields disabled" is an owner UX/product decision, not a repository-owned invariant that the current code violates. "All fields off => match nothing" (the current behavior) is a defensible reading: there is no field in which the query can match, so nothing matches. "All fields off => ignore the query and show all" is the reviewer's reading. Neither is dictated by any contract visible in the code, and the empty-search-keys path is reached only from a UI state the owner controls. Per anti-slop, the desired UX of an all-toggles-off state is user taste, not auto-fixable slop.

Missing evidence required before any change: the owner's intended behavior contract for the (non-empty query, zero enabled fields) state — specifically whether disabling all fields is meant to mean "filter on nothing" (current []) or "filtering inert, show full list." Until that contract is stated, the remediation direction is undetermined.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Verification Rule (no concrete owner-affecting defect is established without the intended-behavior contract) + anti-slop user-owned-decision separation.

Status: OPEN pending the owner's intended-UX contract for the empty-enabled-fields state. No remediation prescribed.

threads.finding_body:
### [General Review][tier1] TYPE_ESCAPE_HATCH
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: cd26f319188e8d46daf1e2c51dab5169f48782ba739d121364cb37a690c571f9 -->

**Location:** `src/columnModel.ts:177-177`
**Violated invariant:** Every column key from DEFAULT_COLUMNS must be a valid SortKey at compile time; the `columnId as SortKey` cast at line 177 removes this guarantee, allowing non-SortKey keys to compile and silently produce empty sort values.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'as SortKey' src/columnModel.ts && grep -n 'creators_compact' src/utils/fuzzy.ts | head -3`
**Symptom:** A new column added to DEFAULT_COLUMNS with a key not listed in SortKey compiles without error but sorts all items to the empty string for that column, producing a non-reproducible ordering.
**Source:** zoteroSortingFn at columnModel.ts:177 casts columnId (a generic string from TanStack) as SortKey via `columnId as SortKey`. There is no runtime guard that columnId is actually in the SortKey union before the cast.
**Consequence:** Column sorting degrades silently when new columns lack an explicit SortKey entry. No compile error or test failure announces the gap.
**Evidence:** `src/columnModel.ts:170-184` (file-read), `src/librarySelectors.ts:1-5` (file-read)

**Possible duplicate disposition required:** a prior PR thread has this category/path fingerprint. Compare invariant, source, consequence, and evidence before resolving; do not suppress this finding by similarity alone.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: false at HEAD (53685bf). The finding asserts that columnId as SortKey at src/columnModel.ts:177 removes a compile-time guarantee and allows a new DEFAULT_COLUMNS key that is not a valid SortKey to compile and silently sort to empty string. That scenario is unrepresentable in the current types:

- SortKey = keyof ZoteroItem | creators_compact (src/librarySelectors.ts:4).
- ColumnDefinition.key = keyof ZoteroItem | creators_compact (src/types.ts:152).

These two unions are identical, so every DEFAULT_COLUMNS entry key already IS a SortKey -- there is no key you can add to DEFAULT_COLUMNS that compiles but is not a SortKey. The cast is not substituting for runtime validation of untrusted input; it recovers the type that @tanstack/react-table erased. TanStack types sortingFn(columnId: string) generically, so columnId arrives typed as string even though the only column ids that reach zoteroSortingFn are those built from ZOTERO_COLUMNS = DEFAULT_COLUMNS.map(buildColumnDef) (columnModel.ts:204-217, the sole column source; useLibraryTable.ts:48). The id round-trips internally: column.key (a SortKey) -> ColumnDef.id (string, erased by the library) -> back to SortKey here.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy explicit carve-out -- as is acceptable where the erased type is immediately narrowed and the value is an internal implementation detail, NOT a module/SDK/API boundary accepting uncontrolled external data. This is precisely that case (cargo-cult flagging of every as is called out as wrong). There is no external boundary here and no runtime-validation obligation to substitute for. Resolving as a false finding.

threads.finding_body:
### [General Review][tier1] NULL_UNSAFE
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 4f1d748eea4ae8e062d84795d0ce81514136faef57b31f9ad82c04626c6884e6 -->

**Location:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:550-561`
**Violated invariant:** Every non-synthetic collection from the repository must carry a Zotero collection key at the import boundary, but both the runtime schema (CollectionSchema) and the type interface (Collection) declare key as optional, so a future schema change that drops key would not be caught at compile time.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'key' src/server/zoteroRepository.ts | tail -5 && grep -n 'key' src/schemas.ts | grep Collection && grep -n 'key' src/types.ts | grep Collection`
**Symptom:** If queryLibrary ever stops populating the key field (e.g., SQL column rename), selectModalImportCollections at libraryViews.ts:64 throws a generic error instead of a typed contract violation, and the error surfaces at runtime rather than during schema validation.
**Source:** CollectionSchema at schemas.ts:35 declares key as z.string().optional(); Collection interface at types.ts:147 has key?: string. The contract that real (non-synthetic) collections must carry a populated key is an unwritten convention enforced only by a runtime throw at libraryViews.ts:64.
**Consequence:** The repository-to-import-module contract for collection keys has no type-level enforcement. A future regression that drops the key value is invisible until a user triggers the import path.
**Evidence:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:550-562` (file-read), `src/libraryViews.ts:58-68` (file-read), `src/schemas.ts:27-36` (file-read), `src/types.ts:140-148` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: true at HEAD (53685bf). Every non-synthetic collection produced by queryLibrary carries a real Zotero key (src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:552-561 maps row.key from RawCollectionRowSchema, where key is z.string() and non-optional at the SQL row boundary, line 98). But the published contract types weaken that: CollectionSchema.key is z.string().optional() (src/schemas.ts:35) and the Collection interface declares key?: string (src/types.ts:147). The "real collections must carry a populated key" contract is therefore enforced only by a runtime throw at the import boundary (src/libraryViews.ts:64-66 in selectModalImportCollections), with no compile-time guarantee. A future regression that drops key would pass type-checking and only fail at runtime when a user triggers import.
- Remediation disposition: not specified here (role B does not prescribe fixes).
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy "Every external boundary ... assert the expected shape" and Crash at the Boundary — the optionality, needed only for the single synthetic { id: "all" } collection, leaks across all real collections and removes type-level enforcement of an owned repository-to-import contract.

Root concern: the repository->import contract that real collections carry a Zotero key has no compile-time enforcement; key optionality intended only for the synthetic My Library view weakens the type for all real collections.

Thread left OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — Collection.key was optional only for the synthetic view, weakening the type for every real collection.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): Collection is a discriminated union (library-root | real with key:string); schema is z.discriminatedUnion with key:z.string().min(1) on real, so a keyless real collection is unrepresentable; the prior runtime keyless guard's burden transferred to the schema, proven by a schema-rejection test.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: e28b591

threads.finding_body:
### [General Review][tier1] DUPLICATED_CONTRACT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: fdd78005c939ee63b03abe37baff8db1e4506d6c34b267642e9d8c206bf63da7 -->

**Location:** `src/libraryViews.ts:11-17`
**Violated invariant:** Adding a new view sentinel requires coordinated changes in at least three independent locations (LIBRARY_VIEW_SENTINELS, selectItemsForCollection switch, getCollectionName if-else chains); adding a sentinel to only some locations produces incorrect UI behavior without any compiler error.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'duplicates\|no-pdf\|no-extraction\|nonstandard-citekey' src/libraryViews.ts src/librarySelectors.ts src/App.tsx`
**Symptom:** A new sentinel added to LIBRARY_VIEW_SENTINELS and selectItemsForCollection but omitted from getCollectionName silently shows 'My Library' as its collection name instead of the expected label.
**Source:** LIBRARY_VIEW_SENTINELS (libraryViews.ts:11-17) is the canonical definition, but selectItemsForCollection (librarySelectors.ts:116-126) independently enumerates each sentinel in a switch statement, and getCollectionName (App.tsx:226-233) independently enumerates each sentinel with if-else branches. The default branch in selectItemsForCollection at least throws on unknown ids, but getCollectionName has no guard and falls through to 'My Library'.
**Consequence:** A new view sentinel added incompletely produces a misleading collection name in the sidebar header. The bug is silent — no compiler error, no test failure unless a test explicitly checks the new sentinel's name.
**Evidence:** `src/libraryViews.ts:11-17` (file-read), `src/librarySelectors.ts:111-135` (file-read), `src/App.tsx:226-233` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: true at HEAD (53685bf). The view-sentinel contract is scattered across three independently-maintained sites: the canonical list LIBRARY_VIEW_SENTINELS (src/libraryViews.ts:11-17); the selectItemsForCollection switch (src/librarySelectors.ts:116-135); and getCollectionName (src/App.tsx:226-233).

Root concern: adding a sentinel requires synchronized edits in all three, but only one site is guarded. selectItemsForCollection has a default branch that throws on an unknown id (librarySelectors.ts:128). getCollectionName has NO guard: it enumerates duplicates/no-pdf/no-extraction/nonstandard-citekey then collections.find, falling through to My Library (App.tsx:231-232). A sentinel added to the canonical list and the selector but omitted from getCollectionName silently mislabels its sidebar header as My Library with no compile error and no test failure -- the high-blast-radius / scattered-truth failure mode.

Policy basis: anti-slop Brittleness / scattered-truth-and-blast-radius (the same concept defined in three places so a change to one breaks distant consumers); the canonical set exists precisely to be the single source, yet two consumers re-enumerate it by hand and one of them fails open to a wrong label.

No remediation prescribed here. Thread left OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the view-sentinel contract was scattered and getCollectionName silently labelled an unknown id as 'My Library'.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): LIBRARY_VIEW_SENTINEL_NAMES is the single id->name map (id list derived from its keys); selectActiveViewName resolves a name and THROWS on an unknown id; App.getCollectionName delegates to it. Proven: unknown id throws; each sentinel resolves to its own name.

### [General Review][tier2] SILENT_IGNORE
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: c474c2a342b89470b55d885fefc9ae45aac8fac7800bca9c62b6a115f865ab5f -->

**Location:** `src/utils/citation.ts:82-92`
**Violated invariant:** Creator roles beyond author, editor, and serieseditor must either be mapped to a CSL name field or explicitly excluded with a diagnostic; silent dropping of known Zotero roles (translator, inventor, contributor, etc.) produces a misleading error downstream.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'creatorType' src/types.ts && grep -n 'cslNamesByRole' src/utils/citation.ts && echo 'Roles handled:' && sed -n '82,92p' src/utils/citation.ts`
**Symptom:** An item with translator or inventor as its only creator triggers 'Item has no author or editor' (citation.ts:160-163) even though it has creators — the misleading error masks the real issue (unhandled Zotero creator role).
**Source:** cslNamesByRole at citation.ts:82-92 only recognizes 'author', 'editor', and 'serieseditor'. All other Creator.creatorType values (including 'translator', 'inventor', 'contributor', 'bookAuthor', 'attorneyAgent') are silently dropped from both author and editor arrays.
**Consequence:** Users cannot distinguish 'item has no creators at all' from 'item has creators with an unsupported role'. The error message is actively misleading, as it claims 'no author or editor' when the item may have creators of other Zotero roles.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/citation.ts:78-92` (file-read), `src/utils/citation.ts:155-165` (file-read)

**Possible duplicate disposition required:** a prior PR thread has this category/path fingerprint. Compare invariant, source, consequence, and evidence before resolving; do not suppress this finding by similarity alone.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted.

Claim disposition: true; live at HEAD (53685bf). cslNamesByRole (src/utils/citation.ts:82-92) recognizes only author/editor/serieseditor and silently drops every other Zotero creatorType (translator, inventor, contributor, bookAuthor, director, attorneyAgent, ...). For a citable-TYPE item whose only name-bearing creator is one of these dropped roles, itemToCsl then hits the "no author or editor" throw (src/utils/citation.ts:160-164), so the user sees "has no author or editor" even though the item DOES have creators. The error misclassifies "unsupported role" as "no creators."

Policy basis: anti-slop dead/misleading control flow + bespoke-software-policy fail-LOUD-with-accurate-signal. This is the silent-failure class the policy exists to catch: the drop is invisible and the downstream error is actively misleading. f644c4c added the creator-condition throw without making the role coverage / the citability affordance match it, so the inconsistency is real and introduced by that commit. Note isCitable (src/utils/citation.ts:131-133) gates the UI on TYPE only, so this throw is reachable from a UI-advertised action.

Root concern (no fix prescribed): the set of creator roles that yield a name-bearing CSL entry must be consistent with the condition under which itemToCsl throws and with the affordance predicate the UI trusts; a known Zotero role must not be dropped in a way that produces a misleading "no creators" failure.

Status: OPEN for the remediation round. No remediation prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — cslNamesByRole treated only author/editor as name-bearing and silently dropped other roles, so the authorless throw gave a misleading message for an item that visibly has creators.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The throw now distinguishes 'no creators' from 'creators present but none name-bearing'; both isCitable and itemToCsl consult the same hasCitableType/hasNameBearingCreator predicates so predicate and throw share one source. No placeholder author.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: fc91461.

### [General Review][tier2] REDUNDANT_VALIDATION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 31a3297f671fd19b1814e17f63b4b34c0be6cbc6c68e86b65b84b72874a66f71 -->

**Location:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:230-230`
**Violated invariant:** Item types should be validated exactly once against a definitive contract. Calling parseItemType during preflight (line 230) and then LibraryPayloadSchema.parse (line 564) on the same data duplicates the same validation without providing a distinct fault domain.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'parseItemType\|LibraryPayloadSchema.parse' src/server/zoteroRepository.ts`
**Symptom:** Each library load validates every observed item type twice — once in assertObservedItemTypes via parseItemType (line 230) and again when LibraryPayloadSchema.parse validates every item's itemType field against ItemTypeSchema (line 564).
**Source:** assertObservedItemTypes at zoteroRepository.ts:216-232 runs parseItemType on every distinct item type from the DB. Then the full pipeline at line 564 passes all items through LibraryPayloadSchema.parse, which validates the same itemType field via its own ItemTypeSchema enum. Both enforce the same 'must be in ITEM_TYPES' contract.
**Consequence:** Unnecessary double computation on every library load. If the two validations ever diverge (one allows a type the other rejects), the behavior is inconsistent — the preflight would throw before any item is returned, while the schema parse would throw inside a later-boundary parse.
**Evidence:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:216-232` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:564-565` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: false. The finding alleges parseItemType during preflight (assertObservedItemTypes, src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:214-232) duplicates LibraryPayloadSchema.parse (line 564) "without providing a distinct fault domain". The distinct-fault-domain premise is false at HEAD: the preflight runs parseItemType over DISTINCT top-level item types and throws the typed ZoteroDatabaseContractError kind "unsupported_item_type" BEFORE any rows are mapped, whereas a row-shape failure inside the single queryLibrary run surfaces as the distinct kind "invalid_rows" (loadValidatedLibrary at lines 322-332). Those are two different, deliberately separated typed fault domains, and the structural one is load-bearing: an owned route test asserts the surfaced kind is exactly "unsupported_item_type" (src/server/libraryApi.test.ts:114-120), which a schema-only parse would not produce.
- Remediation disposition: misaligned. Collapsing to a single validation would erase the typed structural error kind the contract preflight exists to provide.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Verification Rule (no concrete owner problem now: per-library-load distinct-type validation is negligible cost on a once-per-load path) and Crash at the Boundary (the typed unsupported_item_type kind is the boundary signal, not redundant). The residual double-compute is a micro-optimization on a non-hot path; anti-slop rejects micro-optimizations by default.

No code change. Resolving.

### [General Review][tier2] DEAD_CODE
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 7d007c9da5f0c18d2ff909d2082c3631b4fbdecbb7479109691efbadaedefd72 -->

**Location:** `package.json:41-41`
**Violated invariant:** Every declared runtime dependency must be imported by at least one owned source file, or it should be removed to avoid misleading maintainers about the app's actual dependency surface.
**Proof:** `grep -rn 'from.*motion' src/ 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; grep -rn 'from.*@radix-ui/react-select' src/ 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; grep -rn 'from.*@radix-ui/react-tabs' src/ 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; grep -rn 'autoprefixer' src/ 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; grep -rn 'esbuild' src/ 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; echo 'vite in both dependencies and devDependencies:' grep -c 'vite' package.json`
**Symptom:** The package.json declares 'motion' (line 41), '@radix-ui/react-select' (line 26), '@radix-ui/react-tabs' (line 27), 'autoprefixer' (line 57), and 'esbuild' (line 58), but none is imported by any file under src/. Additionally, 'vite' appears in both dependencies (line 45) and devDependencies (line 63).
**Source:** package.json dependency declarations that are not consumed by owned source code.
**Consequence:** Unnecessary install-time overhead and misleading documentation of what the app actually depends on. The dual vite listing is confusing but harmless since both resolve to the same version.
**Evidence:** `package.json:18-65` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: False for the cited line. This tier2 DEAD_CODE finding (package.json:41) claims "motion" is declared but not imported by any file under src/. At HEAD 53685bf, package.json:41 is "motion": "^12.23.24", and motion IS imported and used: src/components/AdvancedSearchModal.tsx:2 (import { AnimatePresence, motion } from "motion/react") with motion.div used at lines 70 and 233. So the dead-code claim is false for the cited dependency.

Policy basis: anti-slop Dependency Inversion Rule -- "unused" is not a quality signal and dead-dependency claims must be verified by actual import sites (rg). The verification shows a real import site, so the DEAD_CODE pattern does not hold for line 41. (The same comment body also lists @radix-ui/react-select, @radix-ui/react-tabs, autoprefixer, esbuild, and a dual vite listing; those are on other lines, are not this thread's cited line:41 subject, and the comment itself concedes the dual vite listing is "harmless." This disposition addresses only the line:41 motion claim that anchors this thread.) No code change.

### [General Review][tier2] DUPLICATED_LOGIC
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 9b97b48fc906e0bd0f3c4d406544f86fcaadf1bf7a60caf2c3ba06d5ae89accd -->

**Location:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:281-285`
**Violated invariant:** Two code paths that construct identical Fzf instances from the same document type should share a factory or builder rather than independently duplicating the configuration.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'new Fzf' src/utils/fuzzy.ts`
**Symptom:** rankDocuments (line 281-285) and rankPaletteDocuments (line 304-308) each construct a new Fzf instance with identical configuration (selector document key projection, casing 'case-insensitive' or configurable, normalize true) from the same ZoteroSearchDocument type.
**Source:** Both functions accept ZoteroSearchDocument[] and independently configure Fzf with the same selector function (closure over searchText with the caller's keys), the same casing behavior (or configurable vs hardcoded), and the same normalize flag.
**Consequence:** Any future change to Fzf configuration (e.g., adding a custom scoring function, changing normalize behavior) must be applied in two places. The divergence potential increases with each Fzf configuration option added.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:271-311` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: factually accurate but design-hostile. Both rankDocuments (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:281-285) and rankPaletteDocuments (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:304-308) do construct a new Fzf with a searchText selector and normalize:true. But the two are deliberately distinct ranking modes, not duplicated logic:

- rankPaletteDocuments: hardcoded casing 'case-insensitive', then a single direct fzf.find(query) subsequence ranking (palette UX, e.g. 'agtf' -> "Algebraic Geometry and Theta Functions").
- rankDocuments: configurable casing (settings.matchCase ? 'case-sensitive' : 'case-insensitive'), and instead of a single find it splits the query into whitespace tokens and computes per-token match sets, then applies token-AND vs token-OR (settings.matchType) — none of which exists in the palette path.

The file documents this as intentional (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:194-198: "The two modes use different engines [paths] because their UX is intentionally different (subsequence palette vs. configurable-threshold advanced search)"). Extracting a shared Fzf factory would couple two intentionally-divergent behaviors (configurable vs fixed casing, token-split-AND/OR vs whole-query subsequence) behind one builder.

Policy basis: anti-slop OVER-ABSTRACTION / "factory wired for exactly two options that never diverge" red flag, and the design-choice gate (deliberate behavioral constraint: two distinct search modes are a user-owned design decision). The shared ~3-line Fzf config is not a real concept worth a shared builder; the divergent surrounding logic is the point. This is generic "extract a factory" review slop, rejected by default per anti-slop micro-optimization rules.

No code change. Resolving as rejected.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Index all creator names for author search**

When a user searches by a creator name that is not present in the compact display string—such as a first name or a third author hidden behind “et al.”—the top-bar search, command palette, and Advanced Search “Creators” target no longer find the item. The only UI-exposed creator search key is `creators_compact` (from the default columns/search keys), but this projection stores only `formatCreatorsCompact(...)` while the full `creators` projection is not selectable, so common author searches regress for multi-author or first-name queries. Populate the searchable creator key with all creator names or wire the full creators projection into the UI.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted.

Claim disposition: true; live at HEAD (53685bf). The only UI-exposed creator search key is creators_compact: it is the single creator member of PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:200-206), and isDefaultSearchField (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:213-215) seeds the advanced-search default toggles from that same set. creators_compact is projected as formatCreatorsCompact(item.creators) (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:221), which collapses to "Lastname", "A & B", or "Lastname et al." (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:340-345) — it drops every first name and every author past the second. The richer creators projection (formatCreatorsFull, src/utils/fuzzy.ts:222) exists on ZoteroSearchDocument but is NOT in PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS and is not a UI default field, so a search for a first name or a 3rd+ author yields no match in the top-bar search, palette, or advanced-search "Creators" default.

Policy basis: test-guidelines/anti-slop owned-behavior correctness — this is a real user-facing search-recall regression for multi-author / first-name queries, not speculative edge-case hardening; the boundary the repo owns (which creator tokens are searchable) silently excludes data users reasonably search by.

Root concern (no fix prescribed): the default-searchable creator surface must index the creator names a user can reasonably query (including non-leading authors and given names), not only the compact display abbreviation.

Status: OPEN for the remediation round. No remediation prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — only the compact creator display form was indexed, so a user could not find an item by a non-leading co-author or a first name.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The searchable creator value comes from creatorsSearchText (every creator's first and last name) under the existing creators_compact key; PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS unchanged; display/sort still read formatCreatorsCompact. Proven: find by 3rd-author surname, by first name, by 4th author via palette scope; compact display unchanged.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: ea5d7d2

Claim disposition: false / mislocated. The cited line schemas.ts:37 is icon: z.string().optional() inside LibraryRootCollectionSchema, a genuinely-optional UI field. The first sentence of the finding ("Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>") is the fuzzy.ts ZoteroSearchKey finding header attached to an unrelated schema line; there is no as-cast in src/schemas.ts. The second clause (POLICY.NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT: a required domain field passing the Zod boundary as undefined then silently defaulted to empty string) does not match this file: the only import-boundary-critical field, RealCollectionSchema.key, is z.string().min(1) (required, src/schemas.ts:47), not optional; a keyless real collection cannot parse and can never reach the import boundary (comment 27-31). ## ai-review/general / The module-level preflight in main.ts must not execute the full queryLibrary pipeline (SQL queries, row parsing, domain mapping) only to discard the result, because the same pipeline is re-executed on

The module-level preflight in main.ts must not execute the full queryLibrary pipeline (SQL queries, row parsing, domain mapping) only to discard the result, because the same pipeline is re-executed on every /api/library request via deps.loadLibrary().

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/85)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: true. At HEAD (8d3d561) src/server/main.ts line 44 calls loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri(config.zotero.databaseUri) at module scope and discards the return value. loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri runs the full validated pipeline (assertStructuralContract plus the expensive queryLibrary: SQL scans plus per-row Zod parsing). The same function is then wired as loadLibrary (line 47) and re-executed on every /api/library request. The startup preflight only needs the structural-contract check, not the discarded queryLibrary output, so every server start runs the full library query pipeline twice (startup plus first request) and throws away the startup result.
  Remediation disposition: underspecified here by design (no fix prescribed).
  Policy basis: anti-slop workflow-waste / discarded-result module-scope side effect; a startup fail-fast should run the minimum validation needed to prove the dependency is available, not the full data pipeline whose result is discarded.

Root concern (for remediation round): the startup preflight must prove database availability via the structural-contract check only, without executing (and discarding) the full library query pipeline that is already re-run per request.

Left OPEN for the remediation round. No fix prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the startup preflight ran the full library query pipeline and discarded the result, and the same pipeline re-ran per /api/library request (boot materialized the whole library only to throw it away).

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): Startup now calls assertDatabaseContractFromUri (structural-only contract check via try/finally close); the per-request path still runs the real query. Proven: startup runs the structural check but NOT the full data query (query-preparation count) and rejects a contract-violating DB; live zotero-doctor validates the DB through this path.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 183a0bc

## ai-review/general / A request with a malformed JSON body sent to any POST endpoint must return HTTP 400 with kind invalid_request, not HTTP 500 with kind internal_error. The error middleware must distinguish client-level

Every non-synthetic collection from the repository must carry a Zotero collection key at the import boundary, but both the runtime schema (CollectionSchema) and the type interface (Collection) declare key as optional, so a future schema change that drops key would not be caught at compile time.

replies.author: dzackgarza

Outdated (superseded by commit e28b591).

  Claim disposition: false at HEAD. The finding alleged that both the runtime schema (CollectionSchema) and the type interface (Collection) declare key as optional, so a dropped key would not be caught at compile time. That is no longer the case. At HEAD (8d3d561), src/schemas.ts:49-52 defines CollectionSchema as z.discriminatedUnion("kind", [LibraryRootCollectionSchema, RealCollectionSchema]). RealCollectionSchema (schemas.ts:39-47) declares key: z.string().min(1) - required and non-empty. LibraryRootCollectionSchema (schemas.ts:32-37) correctly has no key (the synthetic My Library root is not a real Zotero collection). The producer (zoteroRepository.ts:551-565) builds the synthetic root as { kind: "library-root", ... } (no key) and every real collection as { kind: "real", ..., key: row.key }. A keyless real collection cannot parse and can never reach the import boundary. The optional-key premise is false at HEAD.

## ai-review/general / Expensive side effects (localStorage read, JSON.parse, Zod schema validation, column key iteration) in readStoredColumns must not execute on every React component render. The lazy initializer form of 

BibTeX input must be validated exactly once before being sent to the Zotero write boundary. The current code validates the same bibtex string twice — once in runResolverPlugin (line 180) and again in importBibTeXToZotero (line 198) — with no mutation between the calls.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/86)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: true. At HEAD (8d3d561) src/server/resolverPlugins.ts validates the same BibTeX string twice with no mutation between: runResolverPlugin calls parseBibTeXToMetadata(bibtex) at line 180 (inside the child 'close' handler), then the sole caller chain resolveSourceToZotero (lines 224-225) passes that exact return value to importBibTeXToZotero, which calls parseBibTeXToMetadata(bibtex) again at line 198. There is no code path that reaches importBibTeXToZotero without going through runResolverPlugin first, so the line-198 validation is dead on every production request and validates an already-validated, unchanged string rather than an independently-sourced value.
  Remediation disposition: underspecified here by design (no fix prescribed).
  Policy basis: anti-slop dead-control-flow / JUST-IN-CASE code (a branch that never independently executes is review burden and gives a false sense of defense); single source of validation truth at the Zotero write boundary.

Root concern (for remediation round): BibTeX must be validated exactly once on the path to the Zotero write boundary; the duplicate validation that can never independently fire on a distinct code path must be eliminated so the validation point is unambiguous and not dead defense.

Left OPEN for the remediation round. No fix prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza

Rejected (on investigation).

Investigation (remediation subagent, by call graph): the premise is false. importBibTeXToZotero has a SECOND caller — runAddItemDiagnostic in src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts — which passes a BibTeX string directly with NO prior runResolverPlugin validation. The line-227 parse is therefore the sole import-boundary guard for that path; removing it would expose an unvalidated boundary (a banned removed-guard pattern). Both parses are kept. No change made.

Claim disposition: false against HEAD. The finding alleges getStandardCitekey (CSL.parseParticles + transliteration + fold-to-ascii per creator) runs on every render for every active item via nonstandardCitekeyItems inside selectVisibleLibraryItems, doing O(creators*items) work on every state change EVEN WHEN the nonstandard-citekey view is not selected. That premise is false: selectVisibleLibraryItems (src/librarySelectors.ts:189-201) calls selectItemsForCollection, which switches on collectionId (lines 120-138); nonstandardCitekeyItems is invoked ONLY in the case nonstandard-citekey branch (lines 129-130). When any other view is selected, nonstandardCitekeyItems and getStandardCitekey are never called. The work is gated behind the switch case, so the "even when not selected" cost the finding describes does not occur.
Remediation disposition: misaligned. This is a micro-optimization (memoize/precompute citekeys) for a cost path that, per the gating above, only runs when the user explicitly opens the nonstandard-citekey diagnostic view; no measured user-visible problem is shown.
Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Global Principle #6 (micro-optimizations rejected by default absent a measured/reproduced user-visible problem); the factual premise (unconditional per-render computation) is contradicted by the switch gating.
Audit anchor: src/librarySelectors.ts:120-138 (switch), 129-130 (nonstandard-citekey-only call), 196 (single selectItemsForCollection invocation).

## ai-review/general / Expensive side effects (localStorage read, JSON.parse, Zod schema validation, column key iteration) in readStoredColumns must not execute on every React component render. The lazy initializer form of 

An item with undefined title is excluded from the duplicate-count map (count phase skips it via early return), but in the filter phase it is treated deterministically as not-a-duplicate (returns false). If a duplicate pair consists of one titled item and one title-less item, the title-less item is invisible to the duplicate detector.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/83)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: false; the premise misframes the duplicate-detection contract. The finding asserts a duplicate pair can consist of one titled item and one title-less item, making the title-less item invisible. But duplicateItems (src/librarySelectors.ts:58-78) implements TITLE-based duplication: two items are duplicates iff their normalized titles are equal. A title-less item (item.title === undefined) has no title to compare, so by the definition of this detector it can never be the equal-title twin of a titled item; their title states differ (one present, one absent). The count phase skip (line 65) and the filter-phase false (line 71) are the same intended semantic, stated explicitly in the code comment at 62-63: a title-less item cannot be a title-duplicate. This is a deliberate design choice about what duplicate means, not an asymmetric bug. Fail-loud is preserved: a titled item missing from the count map throws (line 73-74).
Remediation disposition: misaligned. Treating a titled item and a title-less item as a duplicate pair would require redefining duplication on some other key, which is a product redesign, not a defect fix.
Policy basis: anti-slop design-choice gate (the duplicate predicate is a user-owned behavioral definition); bespoke-software-policy (fail-loud retained on the only case that can violate the invariant).
Audit anchor: src/librarySelectors.ts:58-78, esp. comment 62-63 and throw 73-74.

### [General Review][tier1] CYCLE_DOUBLE_EXECUTION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 4d8d69b87c321dcde4b1b2c38754d7c96af28f59dabef90d814c77d60013b665 -->

**Location:** `src/server/main.ts:44-44`
**Violated invariant:** The module-level preflight in main.ts must not execute the full queryLibrary pipeline (SQL queries, row parsing, domain mapping) only to discard the result, because the same pipeline is re-executed on every /api/library request via deps.loadLibrary().
**Proof:** `grep -n 'loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri' src/server/main.ts src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts src/server/zoteroRepository.ts`
**Symptom:** main.ts line 44 calls loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri() at module scope, which runs assertStructuralContract AND queryLibrary. The queryLibrary result is discarded. Then every /api/library request (server.ts:136-138) calls deps.loadLibrary() which runs the same full pipeline again.
**Source:** main.ts:44 calls loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri() which delegates to loadValidatedLibrary() (zoteroRepository.ts:322-332), which runs both assertStructuralContract (cheap) and queryLibrary (expensive). The startup preflight does not need queryLibrary output — it only needs the structural contract check. The preflight should call assertStructuralContract directly instead.
**Consequence:** Every server start executes the full library query pipeline twice (startup + first request). For large Zotero libraries this doubles startup latency and wastes the queryLibrary execution at module scope since its result is discarded. This is a regression of the previously resolved CYCLE_DOUBLE_EXECUTION finding: the earlier form was assertZoteroDatabaseContract running queryLibrary internally; the current form is main.ts's module-level call to `loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri` which wraps the same double execution.
**Evidence:** `src/server/main.ts:43-57` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:298-332` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts:1-12` (file-read), `src/server/server.ts:135-139` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Duplicate of thread PRRT_kwDOS-nFnM6LHwTP (src/server/main.ts:44, advanced-security #85).

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): same CYCLE_DOUBLE_EXECUTION finding as the canonical thread. Confirmed against HEAD (8d3d561): main.ts:44 calls loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri at module scope and discards the result, while the same pipeline is re-run per /api/library request via the loadLibrary wiring (line 47). Dispositioned and tracked under the canonical thread; closed here as a duplicate per pr-feedback-triage Loop and Convergence.

### [General Review][tier2] REDUNDANT_VALIDATION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: d4def40c0ac7aa7f1919bd27a7b2598655c082552bae24e928ad20eebf5fd6c8 -->

**Location:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:180-198`
**Violated invariant:** BibTeX input must be validated exactly once before being sent to the Zotero write boundary. The current code validates the same bibtex string twice — once in runResolverPlugin (line 180) and again in importBibTeXToZotero (line 198) — with no mutation between the calls.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'parseBibTeXToMetadata' src/server/resolverPlugins.ts`
**Symptom:** runResolverPlugin calls parseBibTeXToMetadata(bibtex) at line 180 after the resolver process produces BibTeX output. Then resolveSourceToZotero (line 224-225) calls runResolverPlugin, takes its return value, and immediately calls importBibTeXToZotero, which calls parseBibTeXToMetadata(bibtex) AGAIN at line 198 on the same unchanged bibtex string.
**Source:** Both runResolverPlugin (line 180) and importBibTeXToZotero (line 198) independently validate the BibTeX string. The only call path to importBibTeXToZotero is through resolveSourceToZotero (line 216-226) which always goes through runResolverPlugin first. There is no code path that calls importBibTeXToZotero without prior runResolverPlugin validation.
**Consequence:** The validation in importBibTeXToZotero is dead code on every production request. If the BibTeX validation logic in runResolverPlugin is ever bypassed, the duplicate in importBibTeXToZotero provides a false sense of defense — it validates the same string that was already checked, not an independently sourced value. Dead validation also masks whether the second check actually guards a distinct code path (it does not).
**Evidence:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:125-226` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Duplicate of thread PRRT_kwDOS-nFnM6LHwTT (src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:198, advanced-security #86).

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): same finding as the canonical thread — parseBibTeXToMetadata is called both in runResolverPlugin (line 180) and in importBibTeXToZotero (line 198) on the same unchanged string, and the only path to importBibTeXToZotero is through runResolverPlugin first, so the second call is dead validation. This REDUNDANT_VALIDATION report (line 180) is the same redundant-double-validation defect described in the canonical thread. Confirmed against HEAD (8d3d561): lines 180 and 198 both call parseBibTeXToMetadata; resolveSourceToZotero (224-225) is the sole caller chain. Dispositioned and tracked under the canonical thread; closed here as a duplicate per pr-feedback-triage Loop and Convergence.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Strip URL queries before encoding DOI suffixes**

When the accepted input is a DOI URL with ordinary URL parameters, such as `https://doi.org/10.1234/foo?utm=1`, this only removes the DOI origin and leaves `?utm=1` in the DOI string. Because `doiRequestUrl()` now percent-encodes the suffix, the resolver requests `https://doi.org/10.1234/foo%3Futm%3D1` instead of resolving the DOI path `10.1234/foo`, so common copied DOI URLs with tracking/query or fragment text fail or resolve the wrong identifier. URL-form inputs need their query/fragment stripped before preserving reserved characters for bare DOI inputs.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: true. At HEAD (8d3d561) resolver-plugins/doi.mjs readStdin (lines 26-28) strips only the doi.org origin prefix and trims; it does NOT strip a URL query (?...) or fragment (#...). The DOI manifest accepted-input pattern ^(?:https?://(?:dx\.)?doi\.org/)?10\.\d{4,9}/\S+$ matches a URL-form DOI with a trailing query (\S+ accepts ?utm=1), so https://doi.org/10.1234/foo?utm=1 reaches the resolver as 10.1234/foo?utm=1. doiRequestUrl then percent-encodes the suffix via encodeURIComponent, producing https://doi.org/10.1234/foo%3Futm%3D1 and requesting the wrong identifier. The da9fec5 query/fragment stripping was added to the arXiv plugin only; doi.mjs was hardened for percent-encoding but never gained query/fragment stripping.
  Remediation disposition: underspecified here by design (no fix prescribed).
  Policy basis: anti-slop Brittleness/coupling-to-volatile-input plus bespoke-software happy-path-correctness for the documented copy-a-DOI-URL workflow; the percent-encoding hardening that motivated this code makes the missing query/fragment strip load-bearing.

Root concern (for remediation round): URL-form DOI inputs must have their query and fragment removed before the suffix is preserved/encoded, so a copied DOI URL with tracking parameters resolves the bare DOI path rather than an encoded query-bearing suffix.

Left OPEN for the remediation round. No fix prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — a DOI URL input carrying a query/fragment was not stripped before the suffix was percent-encoded, so the query became part of the encoded identifier and resolved the wrong DOI (da9fec5 added query-stripping for arXiv, not DOI).

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): doiFromInput strips query then fragment then the doi.org/dx.doi.org prefix before doiRequestUrl encodes the suffix (mirrors arxivIdFromInput; fetch guarded by import.meta.main). Proven: every accepted DOI input form, with/without ?query/#fragment, yields the correct request URL.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 98fb6ac

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Parse accepted ZBMath URL schemes case-insensitively**

The server accepts resolver inputs with the manifest regex compiled using the `i` flag, so a pasted URL like `HTTPS://zbmath.org/?q=AN:0787.14001` passes validation. This branch checks the scheme with case-sensitive `startsWith`, falls through to the bare-number path, and returns the whole URL; `zbmath.mjs` then searches upstream for `an:HTTPS://...` rather than the ZBMath number, so accepted URL inputs with uppercase schemes fail before import.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: true. At HEAD (8d3d561) resolver-plugins/zbmath-lib.mjs parseZblNumber (line 15) detects URL inputs with case-sensitive input.startsWith('http://') || input.startsWith('https://'). The zbmath-url manifest pattern ^https?://(?:www\.)?zbmath\.org/\?q=(?:an:|an%3A)\d{4}\.\d{5}$ is matched with the i flag in pluginAcceptsInput, so HTTPS://zbmath.org/?q=AN:0787.14001 is accepted. The uppercase scheme fails both case-sensitive startsWith checks, falls through to the bare-number branch at line 23, and returns the whole URL with only a leading /^an:/i strip applied (which does not match), so the upstream search receives an:HTTPS://... and never resolves. da9fec5 fixed the AN: prefix case-sensitivity (line 12 AN_PREFIX, /^an:/i) but did NOT fix the URL scheme case-sensitivity; the two are independent.
  Remediation disposition: underspecified here by design (no fix prescribed).
  Policy basis: anti-slop coupling-to-volatile-input / case-mismatch between the case-insensitive accept gate and the case-sensitive parse branch; bespoke-software happy-path-correctness for accepted inputs.

Root concern (for remediation round): every input the manifest gate accepts case-insensitively must be parsed correctly; the URL-vs-bare-number branch must recognize the scheme case-insensitively so accepted uppercase-scheme URLs are stripped to the bare zbMATH number rather than passed through whole.

Left OPEN for the remediation round. No fix prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — parseZblNumber detected URLs with a case-sensitive startsWith, but the manifest accepts URLs case-insensitively, so an uppercase-scheme URL fell through and was sent upstream as a number.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The scheme check is now /^https?:///i. Proven: an uppercase/mixed-scheme zbMATH URL yields the same number as the lowercase form.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 98fb6ac

## ai-review/slop / Generated BibTeX must be well-formed — field values wrapped in braces must have braces escaped so the output is always parseable by the BibTeX parser.

Server-side configuration must not be exposed to the client bundle; only the keyboardShortcuts field is needed by the frontend, but the entire config (database URI, storage dir, import endpoint, resolver paths, server port) is inlined via static JSON import.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/91)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: factually true but not a repository-owned defect. keyboardShortcuts.ts:1 default-imports the whole zotero-gui.config.json and only reads .keyboardShortcuts (line 9), so the bundler inlines the sibling keys (databaseUri, storageDir, importEndpoint, resolver paths, port) into the client bundle. The mechanical fact is correct.
- Remediation disposition: misaligned. The framing is "server-side configuration must not be exposed to the client bundle" / "information-disclosure" — an enterprise security-boundary concern.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Out of Scope -> Portability ("Hardcoded /home/dzack/ paths or any absolute local paths ... are intentional") and Out of Scope -> Enterprise Patterns ("Missing security hardening ... compliance features"); pr-feedback-triage Global Principle #7 (security/info-disclosure comments require semantic-contract classification: this app owns Zotero library/editor behavior, not a containment or secret-confidentiality invariant). This is a single-user app served on 127.0.0.1; the contents are the OWNER's own local paths and the owner's own loopback write-plugin endpoint, visible only in the owner's own browser on the owner's own machine. There is no secret here and no untrusted client. Per the Verification Rule, this causes no concrete problem for the owner RIGHT NOW on their actual machine. The values in zotero-gui.config.json (file:///home/dzack/..., http://127.0.0.1:23119/write) are exactly the absolute-local-path / loopback-infra class the policy declares out of scope. Accepting an info-disclosure hardening here would import a misaligned security boundary the product does not own.
- Audit anchor: src/keyboardShortcuts.ts:1,9; zotero-gui.config.json:5-16 (all "leaked" values are owner-local paths / loopback endpoint).

## ai-review/slop / Character transformation in citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail loud with an error that prevents silent data loss. The replacement-character fallback followed by silent r

Structured error responses from the server (with error kind and message) must be forwarded to the user, not replaced with a client-generated generic message that discards the server's classification.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/87)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
- Claim disposition: false against HEAD (8d3d561). The finding alleges structured server error responses are replaced with a client-generated generic message that discards the server's classification. The current code does the opposite: openAttachment (src/App.tsx:140-153) parses the failure body with ApiErrorResponseSchema.parse(await response.json()) and rethrows new Error(payload.error.message) (lines 148-149), so the server's own message is surfaced to the user via showToast, not a fabricated generic string. The cited line 214 is unrelated UI copy ("Start Zotero, then reload the library.") inside the zotero_unavailable branch; the library-load failure path renders libraryLoadError.message verbatim (line 219) keyed on the structured kind (line 206). No client-side generic-message substitution exists on either error path.
- Remediation disposition: n/a.
- Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy "Crash at the Boundary" is satisfied (ApiErrorResponseSchema is a strictObject parse with kind/message min(1); a malformed error body throws rather than defaulting). The finding's premise (discarded server classification) is contradicted by current code, so accepting it would be acting on a stale pre-8d3d561 observation. Superseded by 8d3d561 (openAttachment parses ApiErrorResponseSchema and surfaces error.message).
- Audit anchor: src/App.tsx:139-153 (openAttachment) and src/App.tsx:206,219 (library-load error surfacing).

### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 94b44f375628a37d3cbd9d88946d58c7542b3dce649f4d8a2c07d699a6939755 -->

**Location:** `src/keyboardShortcuts.ts:1-1`
**Violated invariant:** Server-side configuration must not be exposed to the client bundle; only the keyboardShortcuts field is needed by the frontend, but the entire config (database URI, storage dir, import endpoint, resolver paths, server port) is inlined via static JSON import.
**Proof:** `grep -rn 'import appConfig from' src/keyboardShortcuts.ts && grep -rn 'KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS' src/components/CommandPaletteHost.tsx && echo '--- all config keys reachable in client bundle ---' && node -e "const c=require('zotero-gui.config.json');console.log(Object.keys(c))"`
**Pattern:** bridge-burning information-disclosure via default-import of server config JSON into client-bundled module, leaking backup-local infrastructure details that no client code uses
**Why this matters:** Every user's local file paths (Zotero database, storage directory, resolver CWD), internal write-plugin URL (http://127.0.0.1:23119/write), and resolver timeouts are visible in the client JavaScript bundle at /assets/index-*.js. Removing this data later requires a breaking bundle change or adding a server endpoint, meaning the leak is persistent across rebuilds.
**Evidence:** `src/keyboardShortcuts.ts:1-9` (file-read), `zotero-gui.config.json:1-32` (file-read), `src/components/CommandPaletteHost.tsx:1-3` (file-read)

### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 49ab02c67c03263c7f396befd22756eb45f15ce9aeddc78710a4271763f33ea7 -->

**Location:** `src/server/main.ts:44-44`
**Violated invariant:** A startup fail-fast should run the minimum validation needed to prove the dependency is available; the full library query (SQL scans + Zod parsing of every row) must not execute at module scope with the result discarded.
**Proof:** `rg 'loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri' src/server/main.ts && rg 'function loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri' src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts -A 10`
**Pattern:** workflow waste — module-scope side effect that runs the full expensive pipeline (dozens of SQL queries, per-row Zod parsing) but discards the result, duplicating the exact same work performed when the client hits /api/library
**Why this matters:** Startup latency is inflated by a redundant full library scan. For a library with thousands of items, attachments, notes, tags, and creators, the redundant query adds measurable delay before app.listen() is called — and the result is thrown away. Each reload (client /api/library call) re-executes the same pipeline because the module-scope result was never cached.
**Evidence:** `src/server/main.ts:43-48` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroDatabase.ts:5-12` (file-read), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:322-332` (file-read)

## ai-review/general / A request with a malformed JSON body sent to any POST endpoint must return HTTP 400 with kind invalid_request, not HTTP 500 with kind internal_error. The error middleware must distinguish client-level

## ai-review/general / Route-level tests must exercise the production code path (loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri -> assertZoteroDatabaseContract -> queryLibrary), not bypass the contract preflight by passing queryLibrary(db) dir

Two code paths that construct identical Fzf instances from the same document type should share a factory or builder rather than independently duplicating the configuration.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/93)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: false. The finding alleges two code paths construct identical Fzf instances and should share a factory. They are NOT identical: rankDocuments (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:282-308) takes casing from settings.matchCase, splits the query into whitespace tokens, runs fzf.find per token, and combines token results with AND/OR (settings.matchType). rankPaletteDocuments (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:310-322) hard-codes case-insensitive, runs the WHOLE query as a single fzf subsequence find, and returns fzf native ranking order. The Fzf options differ (casing source) and the find/combine logic differs entirely. These are two deliberately distinct, explicitly-documented ranking modes (see the PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS JSDoc at src/utils/fuzzy.ts:180-202). Extracting a shared factory would couple two intentionally-divergent modes; the shared surface they DO own (key list, document projection, searchText selector) is already centralized in PALETTE_SEARCH_KEYS / SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS / searchText.
Remediation disposition: misaligned. A common-builder refactor would force the two modes through one configuration and reintroduce coupling the split was designed to avoid.
Policy basis: anti-slop design-choice gate (two distinct named ranking behaviors are user-owned design, not duplication slop); the only genuinely-shared concern (the key contract) is already single-sourced.
Audit anchor: src/utils/fuzzy.ts:292-307 (token-AND/OR Fzf) vs 314-321 (single-subsequence Fzf); shared key/projection truth at 203-209 and 273-280.

threads.finding_body:
## ai-review/general / Expensive side effects (localStorage read, JSON.parse, Zod schema validation, column key iteration) in readStoredColumns must not execute on every React component render. The lazy initializer form of 

When all search fields are disabled and a non-empty query exists, the user expects zero filtering (no fields to filter on = show everything) rather than zero results. An empty search-keys list should short-circuit to the full items list, not to an empty list.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/94)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: false as a defect; this is an owner UX/product taste preference, not a correctness violation. The finding wants "all search fields disabled + non-empty query" to short-circuit to the FULL items list ("no fields to filter on = show everything"). Current code does the opposite by design: rankDocuments returns [] when keys.length === 0 (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:290), and filterZoteroItems returns [] when enabledSearchKeys is empty (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:338). "Zero enabled fields + a query the user typed = nothing to match against = no matches" is an internally consistent semantic. The opposing "show everything" reading is equally defensible but is a different product decision, not the one the owner chose. There is no repository-owned proof burden left unmet and no fail-loud invariant violated either way.
Remediation disposition: misaligned. Changing the empty-keys branch to return the full list would substitute the reviewer UX taste for the owner UX taste.
Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Global Principle (reviewer comments are claims, not commands; do not import a different UX semantic absent a correctness defect) and the anti-slop design-choice gate (behavioral constraint = user-owned decision, not slop).
Audit anchor: src/utils/fuzzy.ts:288-290 (empty-keys branch in rankDocuments) and 335-338 (filterZoteroItems).
