## ai-review/general / A type assertion must not substitute for runtime input validation when downstream consumers rely on the asserted type for correct behavior. Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>.

A type assertion must not substitute for runtime input validation when downstream consumers rely on the asserted type for correct behavior. Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>.has() provides no guarantee the value matches any literal member of the union type.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/95)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: false against HEAD (8d3d561). The finding alleges a type assertion substitutes for runtime validation (casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after a Set.has check provides no guarantee). The substantive cast lives in searchKey (src/utils/fuzzy.ts:268-271): if (SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS.has(value as ZoteroSearchKey)) return value as ZoteroSearchKey; throw new Error(...). The membership check is a real runtime gate that THROWS on any non-member; the asserted-type return is only reached after that gate passes. The inner cast exists solely to satisfy the Set<ZoteroSearchKey> parameter type, not to launder validation. This is fail-loud, not cast-as-validation. (The cited line 247 is accessDate: item.accessDate ?? in buildZoteroSearchDocuments, unrelated to the cast; the scanner header is mislocated.)
Remediation disposition: n/a.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Crash at the Boundary is satisfied (unknown key throws). reviewing-llm-code TYPE-ESCAPE: a guarded cast preceded by a fail-loud runtime membership check is not the validation-evasion the catalog targets.
Audit anchor: src/utils/fuzzy.ts:268-271 (searchKey throws on non-member).

threads.finding_body:
### [General Review][tier1] INCORRECT_ERROR_CLASSIFICATION
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 5af8734b18c2aab145197ee1b90525babd56ab976c1d2c79a5560111123f069e -->

**Location:** `src/server/server.ts:156-165`
**Violated invariant:** Resolver execution failures (timeout, process crash, empty output, invalid BibTeX) must not be classified as 'upstream_boundary_failed' because they are local executor failures, not upstream Zotero write-plugin failures. The error kind 'upstream_boundary_failed' must identify Zotero connectivity/write-plugin errors only; resolver execution failures must produce a distinct kind so monitoring and diagnostics can distinguish the two semantically different failure domains.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'upstream_boundary_failed' src/server/server.ts && grep -n 'throw new Error' src/server/resolverPlugins.ts | head -10`
**Symptom:** Any resolver execution failure (timeout, process crash, empty output, invalid BibTeX) returned from the /api/items/from-source endpoint produces HTTP 502 with kind 'upstream_boundary_failed', the same error kind and status code that a Zotero write-plugin connectivity failure would produce. A user or monitoring system cannot distinguish "the resolver plugin timed out" from "Zotero is unreachable".
**Source:** The .catch() handler at src/server/server.ts:163-165 wraps ALL errors from resolveSourceToZotero into a single ApiError('upstream_boundary_failed', 502, ...). resolveSourceToZotero (src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:216) composes two phases: runResolverPlugin (local process execution with timeout/crash/output failures) and importBibTeXToZotero (HTTP communication with the Zotero write plugin). The catch handler conflates errors from both phases under one error kind, so a local resolver timeout and an upstream Zotero connectivity failure are indistinguishable at the API boundary.
**Consequence:** 1) A resolver that times out or crashes returns 502 'upstream_boundary_failed', misleading the user into believing Zotero is down when the resolver process itself failed. 2) Monitoring and alerting cannot discriminate local resolver failures from Zotero write-plugin failures, making it impossible to page on upstream Zotero issues without also paging on resolver plugin bugs. 3) The test suite (src/server/serverErrors.test.ts) tests upstream_boundary_failed only for Zotero write-boundary failures (line 298-305) and has no test covering resolver execution failures through the route, so the misclassification is not surfaced by tests.
**Evidence:** `src/server/server.ts:150-178` (diff-snippet), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:125-189` (file-read)

**Possible duplicate disposition required:** a prior PR thread has this category/path fingerprint. Compare invariant, source, consequence, and evidence before resolving; do not suppress this finding by similarity alone.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: true at HEAD (8d3d561). Remediation disposition: underspecified (no fix detail recorded; remediation round owns it).

Root concern: The .catch() at src/server/server.ts:163-165 collapses every error from resolveSourceToZotero into ApiError(upstream_boundary_failed, 502). resolveSourceToZotero (src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:216-226) composes two semantically distinct phases: runResolverPlugin (local process: timeout 150, stdout/stderr byte-limit 158/164, spawn error 167, non-zero exit 171, empty BibTeX 176, invalid BibTeX 180-183) and importBibTeXToZotero (the genuine upstream HTTP failure to the Zotero write plugin, 210-211). All resolver-phase errors are bare new Error(message) with no type identity, so the server boundary cannot discriminate a local executor failure from a real Zotero connectivity failure; both surface as upstream_boundary_failed 502. A local resolver timeout is reported to the user and to monitoring as Zotero being unreachable.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy (an error crossing a system boundary must preserve its original type identification so the downstream observer can discriminate failure modes); test-guidelines (failure semantics are repository-owned behavior — ApiErrorKind is the owned structured-response contract); pr-feedback-triage Global Principle 7 (semantic-contract classification, not enterprise hardening). This is the canonical thread for the INCORRECT_ERROR_CLASSIFICATION finding; the duplicate at server.ts:165 (codescan 96) is disposed against this thread.

Left OPEN for the remediation round. No fix prescribed.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: true. At HEAD (8d3d561) the .catch() at src/server/server.ts:163-165 wraps EVERY error from resolveSourceToZotero into a single ApiError(upstream_boundary_failed, 502). resolveSourceToZotero (resolverPlugins.ts:216-226) composes two distinct failure domains: runResolverPlugin (local executor: timeout at line 150, nonzero exit 171, empty BibTeX 176, stdout/stderr byte-limit 158/164, invalid-BibTeX parse 180-183) and importBibTeXToZotero (genuine upstream Zotero write-plugin HTTP failure, 210-211). All collapse to one kind, so a local resolver timeout is indistinguishable at the API boundary from Zotero being unreachable. serverErrors.test.ts proves upstream_boundary_failed only for the Zotero write-boundary path (line 298-305); no test exercises a resolver-execution failure through the route, so the conflation is currently unproven and unguarded.
  Remediation disposition: aligned in concern (resolver-executor failures must carry a kind distinct from upstream Zotero failures). No fix prescribed here.
  Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Diagnostic Signal Suppression / preserve original error-type identification at boundaries; the error kind upstream_boundary_failed must identify Zotero connectivity/write-plugin errors only.

Duplicate note: the resolved fingerprint-twin thread (server.ts:109-114, same scanner fingerprint) raised a DIFFERENT invariant (malformed-JSON body -> 400 invalid_request), which is already remediated via isBodyParseFailure (server.ts:112-124). This resolver-vs-upstream conflation is a separate, live claim and is NOT suppressed by that similarity.

Root concern: local resolver-executor failures and genuine upstream Zotero write-plugin failures are collapsed into one error kind, erasing the distinction needed by the user and by monitoring.

Status: OPEN, accepted for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the import route collapsed ALL resolveSourceToZotero errors into one upstream_boundary_failed kind, so a local resolver-execution fault was indistinguishable from a genuine upstream Zotero write failure.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): Resolver faults raise a typed ResolverExecutionError and Zotero write faults a ZoteroImportError; the route classifies by instanceof (never message) into resolver_execution_failed vs upstream_boundary_failed; unknown errors propagate to internal_error. Proven: a resolver-execution fault surfaces resolver_execution_failed while an upstream write fault surfaces upstream_boundary_failed.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 183a0bc

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Allow editor-only BibTeX entries through the import gate**

When a resolver returns a valid edited-volume/proceedings BibTeX record with `editor` but no `author`, this validation rejects it before Zotero can import it. The rest of the app already treats editors as name-bearing/citable creators, so the add-by-identifier path fails for editor-only records that the read/citation path can otherwise represent; accept `editor` as satisfying the name-bearing requirement here.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: true. At HEAD (8d3d561) src/utils/bibtexParser.ts parseBibTeXToMetadata (lines 42-44) rejects any entry lacking author/AUTHOR with "BibTeX entry must contain at least one author." It does not accept an editor tag as satisfying the name-bearing requirement. The premise that the rest of the app treats editors as name-bearing/citable is verified: src/utils/citation.ts:107-114 (itemIsCitable: author.length > 0 || editor.length > 0) and 181-202 (comment "edited volume (editor present, no author) IS valid; only the truly nameless ...", and record.editor assignment) explicitly treat an edited volume with editor and no author as a valid citable item. So the import gate is stricter than the read/citation contract: a valid edited-volume/proceedings record returned by a resolver (editor, no author) is rejected before Zotero can import it, even though the read path can represent it.
  Remediation disposition: underspecified here by design (no fix prescribed).
  Policy basis: internal-contract consistency (the import gate must not contradict the citation path's name-bearing definition); test-guidelines owned-boundary correctness for the add-by-identifier path.

Root concern (for remediation round): the BibTeX import gate's name-bearing requirement must match the application's citation contract, under which an editor is a name-bearing creator; an editor-only edited-volume/proceedings record must satisfy the gate rather than being rejected for missing author.

Left OPEN for the remediation round. No fix prescribed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — the BibTeX import gate required an author, but the citation contract (citation.ts hasNameBearingCreator) treats an edited volume (editor, no author) as valid and citable, so a citable record could not be imported.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The import gate now accepts at least one name-bearing creator (author OR editor), matching the citation contract; it still rejects a record with neither. Proven: an editor-only edited volume is accepted, a no-author-no-editor record is still rejected.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 98fb6ac

### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 85427ed5aeb8cda09ed50fdabad9577391a051c2f7482c160dca22d80343248f -->

**Location:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:322-332`
**Violated invariant:** An error crossing a system boundary must preserve its original type identification so the downstream observer can discriminate different failure modes.
**Proof:** `sed -n '322,332p' src/server/zoteroRepository.ts`
**Pattern:** error_reclassification
**Why this matters:** server.ts classifies errors by kind for structured API responses. With this reclassification, a SQL data-type violation, a logic error, and a third-party library crash all produce the same response. Automated triage keying on error.kind cannot distinguish them.
**Evidence:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:326-330` (code_snippet), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:488-488` (code_snippet), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:335-335` (code_snippet)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: true. At HEAD (8d3d561) loadValidatedLibrary (zoteroRepository.ts:322-332) re-throws ZoteroDatabaseContractError unchanged (line 327-329, preserving its typed kind) but wraps every OTHER error into ZoteroDatabaseContractError(invalid_rows, message) at line 330. The original error object is discarded: only error.message is carried forward, with no cause chain and no retained type. A SQLite data-type violation, a third-party library crash, and a Zod row-shape parse failure all surface as the same kind invalid_rows, so a downstream observer (server.ts classifies by kind) cannot discriminate these distinct failure domains.
  Remediation disposition: aligned in concern (the original error type/identity must be preserved across this boundary, e.g. via an attached cause, so distinct failure modes remain discriminable). No fix prescribed here.
  Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Diagnostic Signal Suppression (errors crossing a boundary must preserve original type identification; collapsing distinct upstream error types into one synthetic kind erases diagnostic signal). Note: this is not a fallback - the code does fail loud (re-throws). The defect is error-type-identity erasure, not silent continuation.

Root concern: heterogeneous failures (SQL type error, library crash, Zod row-shape mismatch) collapse to a single invalid_rows kind with the original error type discarded, defeating downstream discrimination.

Status: OPEN, accepted for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — loadValidatedLibrary re-threw typed contract errors but flattened every other error into invalid_rows, erasing the original error type and cause.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): It now keeps invalid_rows only for a Zod row-shape error (with the ZodError retained as cause) and propagates every other domain as-is, preserving type/cause. Proven: a Zod row-shape failure surfaces invalid_rows with cause instanceof ZodError.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 183a0bc

threads.finding_body:
### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 85427ed5aeb8cda09ed50fdabad9577391a051c2f7482c160dca22d80343248f -->

**Location:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:164-171`
**Violated invariant:** A date that fails semantic validation must not be silently converted to a simpler form that omits the failure signal.
**Proof:** `sed -n '164,171p' src/server/zoteroRepository.ts`
**Pattern:** silent_year_coercion
**Why this matters:** Citation.js accepts any string and formats it. If cleanDate extracts '0000', Citation.js emits 'year = {0000}' in the BibTeX, which is syntactically valid but semantically wrong.
**Evidence:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:164-171` (code_snippet), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:526-526` (code_snippet)

**Possible duplicate disposition required:** a prior PR thread has this category/path fingerprint. Compare invariant, source, consequence, and evidence before resolving; do not suppress this finding by similarity alone.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: false. cleanDate (zoteroRepository.ts:164-171) runs on the READ path only (queryLibrary maps the result into item.date at line 526). It is not a write-boundary validator. Zotero stores partial dates in its own multipart date field as "YYYY-00-00 YYYY" (the SQL-date part uses 00 for unknown month/day, followed by the user-entered text). Extracting the year from that encoding is the CORRECT interpretation of Zotero own stored format, not a coercion that omits a failure signal: there is no semantic-validation step here that the value "fails", and nothing is silently accepted at a write boundary. The function preserves the raw value when it does not match the known Zotero year-only patterns (returns raw), so it does not fabricate or default a value, and does not fail open.
  Remediation disposition: n/a (no defect).
  Policy basis: anti-slop design-choice gate. cleanDate integrates with a specific external tool (Zotero) data format with a deliberate, narrow behavioral constraint (mapping Zotero YYYY-00-00 partial dates to a display year). Per anti-slop, that is a user/design-owned integration premise, not slop. The hypothetical "0000" concern requires the upstream Zotero DB to literally store 0000-00-00; even then the value is preserved rather than substituted, so no diagnostic signal is suppressed at any owned boundary.
  Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:164-171 (cleanDate), call site at line 526 (read path).

No code change. Closed as Rejected.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve reserved characters in bare DOI suffixes**

When the user enters a bare DOI whose suffix contains `?` or `#` (the manifest accepts this via `\S+`, and the resolver comment says these are valid suffix characters), these splits truncate the actual identifier before `doiRequestUrl` can percent-encode it. For example, `10.1234/foo?bar` is resolved as `10.1234/foo`, so the resolver can import the wrong record; strip query/fragment only for URL-form inputs.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: true. At resolver-plugins/doi.mjs:33-41 (HEAD 183a0bc), doiFromInput strips ?query then #fragment UNCONDITIONALLY for every input before stripping the doi.org/dx.doi.org URL prefix. The function's own header comment (and the manifest's \S+ acceptance) establishes that a DOI suffix is opaque registrant-assigned text that may legitimately contain URI-reserved characters including ? and #. For a bare-DOI input such as 10.1234/foo?bar, the split('?')[0]/split('#')[0] truncate the real identifier to 10.1234/foo before doiRequestUrl can percent-encode it, so the resolver can request and import the wrong record. The ?/# stripping is only sound for the URL-form input it was added for (where ?utm_source / #section are non-identifier decoration); applying it to the bare-DOI form discards real suffix data.

Remediation disposition: the reviewer gestures at "strip query/fragment only for URL-form inputs" — recorded only as the root concern, not as an authoritative fix.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy "crash/operate on EXACTLY the right data" and anti-slop brittleness (coupling correctness to a transform that is valid for one input shape but silently corrupts another). The defect is a silent wrong-record import, not a crash.

Root concern (ACCEPT, no fix detail): doiFromInput must not strip characters that are valid, identifier-bearing suffix content for the bare-DOI input form; ?/# removal is only justified for the URL-form input where they are non-identifier URL decoration.

Leaving this thread OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — a regression from 98fb6ac: doiFromInput stripped ?query/#fragment unconditionally, but the DOI/Handle syntax permits ? and # in a bare DOI's opaque suffix, so a bare DOI like 10.1234/foo?bar was truncated to 10.1234/foo and resolved a different record.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): doiFromInput now branches on a case-insensitive URL scheme: a URL-form input is parsed with the URL constructor so .pathname excludes the query/fragment, while a bare DOI passes through intact (trimmed only). Proven: a bare DOI with ?/# is preserved and travels as percent-encoded path data (foo%3Fbar), while a URL input still strips its query/fragment. The test that had encoded the truncation as spec was corrected to assert preservation.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 3338800

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Wrap write-endpoint fetch failures as upstream errors**

If the Zotero write API is unreachable at this point (for example Zotero quits after the startup check, or the route is called directly), `fetchImpl` rejects before a `Response` exists. That rejection is not converted to `ZoteroImportError`, so the route's classifier falls through to `internal_error` 500 instead of the upstream/Zotero failure kind used for HTTP failures from the same boundary.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written.

Claim disposition: true, confirmed against current code. At src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:228-241 (HEAD 183a0bc), importBibTeXToZotero does await fetchImpl(importEndpoint, ...) and only wraps the !response.ok case (a real HTTP Response) as ZoteroImportError. If the write endpoint is unreachable (Zotero quit after the startup preflight, or the route is invoked directly), fetchImpl REJECTS with a raw network error (TypeError) before any Response exists, so no ZoteroImportError is constructed. The server classifier at src/server/server.ts:166-178 catches the error from resolveSourceToZotero: it converts ResolverExecutionError to resolver_execution_failed 502 and ZoteroImportError to upstream_boundary_failed 502, then re-throws anything else. The raw fetch rejection is neither type, so it propagates to classifyError (server.ts:120-127), which falls through to internal_error 500. An HTTP failure from the SAME write boundary surfaces as upstream_boundary_failed 502, so an unreachable-Zotero failure is misclassified as a server-internal fault, losing the upstream-boundary signal the typed-error design exists to preserve.

Remediation disposition: the reviewer's "wrap write-endpoint fetch failures as upstream errors" is recorded only as the root concern, not as an authoritative fix.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy fail-loudly-at-the-correct-boundary and the PR's own typed-error invariant (errors crossing the system boundary must preserve type identity so the observer can discriminate failure modes). This is a real diagnostic-signal/classification defect, not a hypothetical path: it is the exact transport-vs-HTTP asymmetry the typed errors were introduced to handle.

Root concern (ACCEPT, no fix detail): a transport-level failure of the Zotero write-endpoint fetch belongs to the same upstream/Zotero fault domain as a non-ok HTTP response from that endpoint, and must carry that domain type across the boundary rather than degrading to internal_error.

Leaving this thread OPEN for the remediation round.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Claim true — a gap from 183a0bc: importBibTeXToZotero wrapped only !response.ok as ZoteroImportError, so a transport-level rejection of the write-endpoint fetch (Zotero unreachable: ECONNREFUSED/DNS/timeout) escaped the boundary's instanceof classification and surfaced as internal_error (500) instead of upstream_boundary_failed (502) — same fault domain as an HTTP failure.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The write-endpoint fetch now has a .catch (scoped to the fetch call only) that wraps a transport rejection as ZoteroImportError with the original error as cause; server.ts's existing instanceof classification then returns upstream_boundary_failed. A local parse/schema fault is never mislabeled upstream. Proven by a route-level test using a genuine ECONNREFUSED (bound-then-closed ephemeral port, no mock): the failure surfaces as upstream_boundary_failed, not internal_error.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 3338800

threads.finding_body:
## ai-review/slop / Character transformation in citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail loud with an error that prevents silent data loss. The replacement-character fallback followed by silent r

Structured error responses from the server (with error kind and message) must be forwarded to the user, not replaced with a client-generated generic message that discards the server's classification.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/103)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected (mislocated scanner-rule header + claim false on current code).

Claim disposition: false. The rule HEADER reposted here ("Character transformation in citekey computation must either succeed deterministically or fail loud ... replacement-character fallback") does not apply to src/App.tsx:207. There is NO citekey/character-transformation code in App.tsx; citekey logic lives entirely in src/librarySelectors.ts (string compare, no transformation), and there is no bbtTransliterate / transliterate / U+FFFD sentinel anywhere under src/ (verified: rg for citekey|transliterate|FFFD finds none in App.tsx). The prior U+FFFD-sentinel concern was already removed in an earlier commit; the header is stale and misplaced on an unrelated line.

### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: c4d87244824672e5d59d9ced1a93e868ff26247c8d64b6b4fbd9e4776b3ae1ea -->

**Location:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:205-227`
**Violated invariant:** Every validation boundary must be necessary — redundant parsing on the production path is dead validation that masks whether the second check actually guards a distinct code path.
**Proof:** `rg 'parseBibTeXToMetadata' src/server/resolverPlugins.ts`
**Pattern:** REDUNDANT_VALIDATION
**Why this matters:** The second parse provides no actual safety margin because no code path between the two calls modifies the BibTeX string. If `parseBibTeXToMetadata` has a bug, both parses share it. A future change that adds a third validation step looks like defense-in-depth but continues the pattern of dead validation boundaries, making the pipeline harder to reason about without increasing correctness.
**Evidence:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:205-206` (file-read), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:227-227` (file-read), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:253-254` (file-read)

**Possible duplicate disposition required:** a prior PR thread has this category/path fingerprint. Compare invariant, source, consequence, and evidence before resolving; do not suppress this finding by similarity alone.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected (claim false on investigation; the two parses guard distinct, independently-reachable boundaries).

Claim disposition: false. The finding asserts the parseBibTeXToMetadata call at resolverPlugins.ts:227 is redundant with the parse at :206 because "no code path between the two calls modifies the BibTeX string." That reasoning only holds for the resolveSourceToZotero path (runResolverPlugin -> importBibTeXToZotero). It is false for the OTHER caller of importBibTeXToZotero.

importBibTeXToZotero has two production consumers (verified by grep):
  1. resolveSourceToZotero (resolverPlugins.ts:253-254), which first runs runResolverPlugin -> parseBibTeXToMetadata at :206;
  2. runAddItemDiagnostic (src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts:81-102), which builds a raw BibTeX template string in-place (lines 84-89) and calls importBibTeXToZotero(bibtex, ...) at liveDiagnostics.ts:91 WITHOUT ever calling runResolverPlugin or parseBibTeXToMetadata beforehand.

For caller (2) the parse at resolverPlugins.ts:227 is the SOLE import-boundary validation -- it is the only guard ensuring malformed BibTeX cannot be written to Zotero through the diagnostic path. Removing it would leave that boundary unvalidated. The two parses therefore guard two distinct, independently-reachable entrypoints; this is not dead/redundant validation.

The fingerprint's own "possible duplicate disposition required" note was checked: this is the same category/path as a prior round's finding, which was likewise REJECTED-on-investigation for the same reason (second caller does not pre-validate). The current code still has both callers, so the prior disposition is independently re-confirmed on the merits, not merely deferred to.

Remediation disposition: n/a (no defect; both validation boundaries are necessary).

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy crash-at-the-boundary -- importBibTeXToZotero is an import boundary that must validate its input regardless of which caller supplies it; anti-slop Cross-Inventory/multiple-data-points -- the redundancy claim was triangulated across both callers and fails.

No code change. Resolving as Rejected.

## ai-review/slop / An error crossing a system boundary must preserve its original type identification so the downstream observer can discriminate different failure modes.

A date that fails semantic validation must not be silently converted to a simpler form that omits the failure signal.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected (mislocated header + body misdescribes the cited code).

Claim disposition: false. src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:165-172 (HEAD 183a0bc) is cleanDate, a READ-PATH display normalizer for Zotero's own partial-date encoding. Line 171 is the return raw fallthrough. The function: returns undefined for empty input (:166); for Zotero's "YYYY-00-00  YYYY" zero-padded encoding returns the captured year (:167-170); otherwise returns the raw string UNCHANGED (:171). There is no validation that "fails" and no failure signal to omit -- non-matching input is passed through verbatim (raw is preserved), and the matching cases extract the real year from Zotero's documented partial-date format.

The reposted HEADER ("an error crossing a system boundary must preserve its original type identification") is the github-advanced-security generic-rule repost; cleanDate handles no errors and crosses no error boundary. The finding BODY ("a date that fails semantic validation must not be silently converted to a simpler form that omits the failure signal") mischaracterizes the function: cleanDate is not a write/validation boundary, it is display normalization of values Zotero itself produced, and it preserves the raw value on no-match rather than discarding anything.

This matches the prior round's REJECTED disposition for cleanDate (read-path display normalization, no write boundary, preserves raw on no-match, not error-type erasure), and the current code is unchanged in substance, so the rejection is independently re-confirmed on the merits.

Remediation disposition: n/a (no defect; no error-type or failure signal is erased).

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Verification Rule -- no concrete defect exists at this boundary now; pr-feedback-triage mislocated-header rule.

No code change. Resolving as Rejected.

  const db = new DatabaseSync(databaseUri);
  try { assertZoteroDatabaseContract(db); } finally { db.close(); }
Line 332 is the closing brace. This try/finally has NO catch -- it does not catch, transform, wrap, or flatten any error. The cited invariant ("an error crossing a system boundary must preserve its original type identification so the downstream observer can discriminate different failure modes") is therefore SATISFIED here, not breached.

The genuinely error-classifying sibling, loadValidatedLibrary (:348-361), also preserves type identity: it re-throws ZoteroDatabaseContractError unchanged (:353-354), wraps ONLY a Zod ZodError into the one real 'invalid_rows' kind while retaining the original ZodError as cause (:356-357), and propagates every other domain (SQLite runtime faults, third-party crashes) AS-IS (:359). No failure mode is collapsed; discrimination is preserved.

### [General Review][tier1] SIDE_EFFECT_IMPORT_VULNERABLE
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: e4396ab7afc8fb7148d58245a5325ba7ac13795dd62cd8a67e354f6452c96bbd -->

**Location:** `src/utils/citation.ts:2-3`
**Violated invariant:** Bare side-effect imports `import '@citation-js/plugin-bibtex'` and `import '@citation-js/plugin-csl'` must survive Vite's production tree-shaking so Citation.js can register its BibTeX and CSL output plugins at runtime. The vite.config.ts has no optimizeDeps.include or ssr.noExternal entries for these packages, leaving their survival to Rollup's default bare-import treatment, which drops them if their package.json declares `"sideEffects": false`.
**Proof:** `python3 -c "import json; p=json.load(open('node_modules/@citation-js/plugin-bibtex/package.json')); print('sideEffects:', p.get('sideEffects', '<absent>'))" && python3 -c "import json; p=json.load(open('node_modules/@citation-js/plugin-csl/package.json')); print('sideEffects:', p.get('sideEffects', '<absent>'))"`
**Symptom:** When `@citation-js/plugin-bibtex` or `@citation-js/plugin-csl` declare `"sideEffects": false` (common for ESM packages), Rollup's production tree-shaker may eliminate these bare imports. At runtime, `new Cite(...).format('bibtex')` throws a confusing plugin-not-registered error. The issue does not surface in development (vite dev, vitest) where module resolution is less aggressive.
**Source:** src/utils/citation.ts imports Citation.js plugins as bare side-effect imports. vite.config.ts has no explicit `optimizeDeps.include`, `ssr.noExternal`, or `rollupOptions` to prevent tree-shaking of these packages.
**Consequence:** A `vite build` production artifact could silently drop Citation.js plugin registrations, breaking all BibTeX export and APA citation formatting in the deployed app with no build-time error.
**Evidence:** `src/utils/citation.ts:1-4` (file-read), `vite.config.ts:1-22` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Investigate before action.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: Needs investigation. The threat model is structurally plausible — citation.ts:2-3 are bare side-effect imports (import @citation-js/plugin-bibtex, import @citation-js/plugin-csl) that register Citation.js output plugins purely by side effect, and citation.ts is client-bundled (consumed by InspectorPanel.tsx and App.tsx, so it goes through vite build / Rollup). vite.config.ts at HEAD has no optimizeDeps.include, ssr.noExternal, or rollupOptions for these packages. BUT the finding s own proof premise is conditional on the packages declaring "sideEffects": false, and that premise is currently FALSE: both @citation-js/plugin-bibtex and @citation-js/plugin-csl declare sideEffects = <absent> in their installed package.json. With sideEffects absent, Rollup defaults to treating the module as having side effects and preserves the bare import, so the concrete drop does not occur at the currently installed versions.
  Remediation disposition: underspecified. Whether a build-survival guard is warranted given the false premise, vs. a diagnostic that pins the assumption against version drift (the deps are ^0.7.21; a future minor that adds sideEffects: false would silently reactivate the risk), is a first-principles call for the remediation stage.
  Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Investigate-before-action — the comment points to a possible inconsistency whose triggering condition is not currently met; gather the build/version evidence before changing code. Note an existing diagnostic script (scripts/verify-vite-optimized-deps.mjs) may already bear on this.

Root concern: runtime plugin registration via bare side-effect imports depends on Rollup not tree-shaking them, a property currently held only because the upstream packages do not declare sideEffects: false — an assumption that is unpinned against dependency drift.

Left OPEN; no fix proposed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Rejected (on investigation).

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): the concern was that Vite/Rollup production tree-shaking could drop the bare side-effect imports (@citation-js/plugin-bibtex, @citation-js/plugin-csl) that register Citation.js plugins, silently breaking BibTeX/citation.

Investigation: none of @citation-js/plugin-bibtex, plugin-csl, or core declare sideEffects:false, so Rollup keeps the bare imports by default; vite.config.ts adds no treeshake/moduleSideEffects override; and the production just build bundle retains the live plugin registration tables (the plugin-dependent toBibTeX/toFormattedCitation tests pass). The registration is retained with no current breakage, so there is no present defect. No speculative pin added.

Having `vite` in both `dependencies` and `devDependencies` creates an ambiguous declaration: npm, yarn, and pnpm each resolve this differently, and `npm install --production` installs a build tool as a runtime dependency.

### [General Review][tier2] GUARDED_TYPE_CAST_AT_BOUNDARY
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: a3c7d6fe19eebddb298bcbf88b1459f7e17af6e91b07fdd036bee540d693d47c -->

**Location:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:174-178`
**Violated invariant:** A runtime-validated string value cast with `as ItemType` must match the TypeScript ItemType union exactly. The guard `ITEM_TYPES.includes(value)` and the `as ItemType` cast are independent code paths that can drift: adding a type to ITEM_TYPES without adding it to the TypeScript union (or vice versa) makes the type annotation incorrect without a build error.
**Proof:** `grep -rnE ' as (ItemType|ZoteroSearchKey|ResolverCommand) ' src/ --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' | grep -v node_modules | grep -v '.test.'`
**Symptom:** Three locations use `as` to bridge from runtime-validated strings to union types: `parseItemType` casts `as ItemType` (zoteroRepository.ts:176) after checking ITEM_TYPES; `searchKey` casts `as ZoteroSearchKey` (fuzzy.ts:269) after checking SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS; `ResolverCommandSchema` uses `.transform(value => value as ResolverCommand)` (resolverPlugins.ts:76) after a Zod schema ensures array shape. Each guard is correct today, but the `as` keyword suppresses TypeScript's ability to verify the value truly belongs to the target union.
**Source:** Pattern repeats at three domain boundaries: zoteroRepository.ts (ItemType), fuzzy.ts (ZoteroSearchKey), resolverPlugins.ts (ResolverCommand). Each is individually guarded but the cast is an independent statement that can diverge from the guard.
**Consequence:** A future code change that adds a member to ITEM_TYPES, SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS, or the Zod transform chain without simultaneously updating the corresponding TypeScript union type would silently accept values outside the declared type, making the type annotation a lie and hiding the mismatch from the type checker.
**Evidence:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:174-178` (file-read), `src/utils/fuzzy.ts:268-270` (file-read), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:76-76` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: false against current code. parseItemType (src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:174-179) gates the cast behind a runtime membership check: (ITEM_TYPES as readonly string[]).includes(value) at line 175, then returns value as ItemType at line 176, otherwise throws a typed ZoteroDatabaseContractError(unsupported_item_type) at line 178. This is a guarded narrowing of a runtime-validated value, not cast-as-validation. ITEM_TYPES is the single runtime source of truth that the ItemType union is derived from in src/types.ts, so the alleged guard/union drift would surface as a compile error, not a silent lie. Per bespoke-software-policy this is the sanctioned Crash-at-the-Boundary pattern (runtime assertion that fails loud on mismatch), not an unchecked as-any. No code change.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Crash at the Boundary (runtime assertion that crashes on mismatch is the correct pattern; as ItemType here is preceded by a fail-loud membership check).

The only real per-sentinel site is the switch in selectItemsForCollection (librarySelectors.ts:120, a DIFFERENT file from the threaded one), which is deliberate fail-loud data-aware dispatch: each sentinel maps to genuinely distinct selection logic (duplicateItems vs itemsWithoutPdf vs itemsWithoutExtraction vs tree-filter) with a throwing default. Every non-synthetic collection from the repository must carry a Zotero collection key at the import boundary, but both the runtime schema (CollectionSchema) and the type interface (Collection) declare key as optional, so a future schema change that drops key would not be caught at compile time.

replies.author: dzackgarza

Claim disposition: false against current code. The finding claims every non-synthetic collection must carry a Zotero collection key at the import boundary, but both CollectionSchema and the Collection type declare key as optional, so a dropped key would not be caught at compile time. Current code makes key REQUIRED on real collections. src/schemas.ts:46 declares RealCollectionSchema.key = z.string().min(1) (required, non-empty); the type RealCollection at src/types.ts:169 declares key: string (required). CollectionSchema (src/schemas.ts:49-52) is a discriminatedUnion on kind: the synthetic library-root variant has no key (it is not a real Zotero collection, by design), and the real variant requires a non-empty key. The mapper (src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:583-593) carries row.key from RawCollectionRowSchema.key = z.string() into every real collection, and the final LibraryPayloadSchema.parse (line 596) would fail loud if a real collection lost its key. The optional-key state the finding describes does not exist. Line 561 here is unrelated (issn mapping). No code change.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Crash at the Boundary; the real-collection key contract is already required and enforced by Zod (introduced e28b591), so the claim is factually false.

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Verify the created item before reporting add success**

When the write endpoint returns before Zotero has flushed the new row to SQLite (the surrounding comment says this race is possible), this route still returns 200 using only the write-boundary metadata. The frontend then immediately reloads the library and can show a success toast while the created item is absent, and a write-plugin success for an item that never becomes visible is never caught. Wait/retry for the returned key in `loadLibrary()` before reporting success, or return a pending state that makes the UI poll instead of declaring completion.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected.

Claim disposition: the underlying concern (the write endpoint reports add success from write-boundary metadata before Zotero has flushed the new row to SQLite, so the UI could show success for an item not yet visible) is a deliberate, documented design decision, and the proposed remediation conflicts with stronger project policy. The route (src/server/server.ts:180-189) intentionally derives success solely from the authoritative write-boundary result, which returns key/id/title under the strict ZoteroImportResultSchema (src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:86-101, item_key/item_id/titles all required non-empty). The comment at lines 180-184 states the rationale explicitly: the library snapshot is eventually consistent, so re-reading loadLibrary() here would race the DB flush and FALSELY report a successful write as not-visible. The Codex remediation (wait/retry for the returned key in loadLibrary before reporting success, or return a pending state with UI polling) would introduce exactly that race-prone read-after-write fallback the design rejects, plus a polling/retry control loop that is enterprise-pattern hardening inappropriate for this single-user app. The write boundary is the authoritative success oracle. No code change.
Policy basis: anti-slop Design Choices Are Not Slop and bespoke-software-policy (no fallback/retry hardening; the deliberate, commented design that trusts the authoritative write boundary over an eventually-consistent re-read is a user-owned decision, not a defect).

threads.finding_body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Allow nullable attachment metadata rows**

For an attachment with no title itemData row or a NULL `contentType`, the LEFT JOIN attachment queries produce `NULL` values; the previous mapper tolerated this with a title/path fallback and empty MIME. Requiring strings here turns one such attachment into an `invalid_rows` failure and makes `/api/library` fail for the whole library instead of showing the rest of the data.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Investigate before action. (Left OPEN — no remediation stamp.)

Claim disposition: needs investigation. AttachmentRowSchema (src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:76-83) and StandaloneAttachmentRowSchema (lines 85-92) require contentType: z.string() and title: z.string() (both non-nullable). The attachment SQL (lines 444-461 and 463-480) sources contentType from a LEFT-JOIN column (a.contentType) and title from MAX(CASE WHEN f.fieldName='title'...), both of which can produce SQL NULL when the attachment has no title itemData row or a NULL contentType. If such a row exists in the owner's real Zotero DB, the Zod parse throws and loadValidatedLibrary classifies it as invalid_rows, failing the whole /api/library load. The reachability of a NULL-contentType / no-title attachment in a real Zotero library is the open question (Zotero linked-URL / link-mode attachments can legitimately carry NULL contentType). The current fixtures (libraryApi.test.ts) never insert such a row, so this state is unproven either way.

Remediation disposition: the Codex-proposed remediation is MISALIGNED and must not be applied as written. Restoring a title/path fallback and an empty-MIME default is fabrication of domain data and directly conflicts with commit 11712f2 (stop fabricating domain data at the Zotero row boundary) and bespoke-software-policy (fallbacks are always slop; no runtime defaults in required-value paths). Whatever the investigation concludes, the resolution must be honest: either prove no such row exists in the owned DB and keep the strict schema (fail loud), or prove NULL contentType is a legitimate Zotero state and make the schema/mapper carry it through truthfully as nullable/undefined (NOT substitute an invented empty MIME or synthetic title).

Root concern (no fix detail): determine whether a real Zotero attachment with NULL contentType or no title itemData row is a reachable state in the owner's library; if so, the strict non-nullable AttachmentRowSchema turns one such row into a whole-library /api/library failure.
Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Crash at the Boundary + no-fabrication (11712f2); the claim is plausibly true but requires real-DB evidence, and the proposed fallback remediation is banned.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Investigate->confirmed real (live DB): Zotero declares itemAttachments.contentType nullable and the attachment title is a derived aggregate that is NULL with no title row; one such real attachment failed the whole /api/library load.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): Attachment row contentType/title are now nullable; NULL is carried through as an absent value (undefined), never a fabricated default; Attachment.title/mimeType made optional in the type+schema. Proven: an attachment with NULL contentType / no title row is accepted and mapped without fabrication, and the library load does not fail.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 389b492

threads.finding_body:
### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: cfb799fe31b1f3b1c65500caf8930adfa450bdbaac89a5eb5eba49a4db81a58a -->

**Location:** `src/schemas.ts:85-85`
**Violated invariant:** The API response boundary schema must enforce all domain invariants; optional inTrash allows a mapping regression to silently drop trash status
**Proof:** `rg 'inTrash' src/schemas.ts && echo '---' && rg 'inTrash' src/server/zoteroRepository.ts | head -3`
**Pattern:** Boundary-schema invariant mismatch: required domain field declared optional
**Why this matters:** Under 'no silent data loss,' the boundary schema must require fields that the domain guarantees. Optional inTrash converts a mapping regression into silently incorrect data rather than a loud schema failure. A refactoring that accidentally drops inTrash from the item mapping would deploy without error, and users would see trashed items mixed into the default library view.

**Location:** `src/App.tsx:75-78`
**Violated invariant:** State lifecycles must be correct: each call to showToast must produce exactly one visible toast that lasts the intended duration
**Proof:** `grep -c 'setTimeout' src/App.tsx && echo '; no clearTimeout or useRef found'`
**Pattern:** Racing toast timer: uncanceled previous timers cause premature toast dismissal
**Why this matters:** User-visible notifications are a correctness concern. If a user triggers a database reload and immediately triggers another action, the feedback message for the second action may disappear after only a fraction of its intended duration, causing the user to miss critical feedback about the second operation's outcome.
**Evidence:** `src/App.tsx:75-78` (file-read)

**Possible duplicate disposition required:** a prior PR thread has this category/path fingerprint. Compare invariant, source, consequence, and evidence before resolving; do not suppress this finding by similarity alone.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted as written.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: True. Verified against current code at HEAD 3338800. App.tsx:75-78 defines showToast(msg) as: setToast(msg); setTimeout(() => setToast(null), 3000); with no stored timer id, no clearTimeout, and no toast-timer useRef. Confirmed by search: the only setTimeout in App.tsx is line 77, the only useRef is paletteHostRef (unrelated). When showToast is called twice in quick succession the first timer is never canceled and fires while the second toast is still showing, dismissing the second toast early. Both reloadFromDb and handleAddResolvedItem call showToast, so two-in-quick-succession is a normal workflow here, not a contrived edge case. Not outdated: no cancellation guard exists at HEAD.
  Remediation disposition: aligned in direction (each showToast must own exactly one timer; a superseding toast must cancel the prior timer).
  Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Principle 5 — a race that produces stale user-visible state is a correctness bug; a small mounted/sequencing/cancellation guard that prevents old async work from clobbering newer user-visible state is aligned. Small blast radius, preserves intended behavior.

Root concern: an uncanceled prior toast-dismissal timer can prematurely dismiss a newer, more important toast, causing the user to miss operation feedback.

This is left OPEN for independent remediation; no fix is proposed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accept: showToast scheduled an uncancelable dismissal setTimeout, so two toasts in quick succession let the first timer dismiss the second early.

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): A toastTimerRef holds the pending timeout; showToast clears the previous timer before scheduling, the timer self-nulls on fire, and an unmount effect clears it. Proven: a second toast shown before the first timer fires stays visible for its own full duration.

Verification (orchestrator-stamped): declared remediation matches the committed code. Commit: 0f653ae

**Location:** `resolver-plugins/doi.mjs:45-60`
**Violated invariant:** Every plugin boundary must validate its own input shape independently; trusting the caller's manifest pattern matching removes defense-in-depth against misrouted inputs
**Proof:** `node -e "const m=require('./resolver-plugins/doi.mjs');try{console.log(m.doiFromInput('https://evil.org/10.1000/xyz123'))}catch(e){console.log('caught:',e.message)}"`
**Pattern:** Manifest-trust validation gap at plugin boundary: no hostname check before extracting path as DOI
**Why this matters:** If the manifest URL pattern is ever loosened or a similar URL matches the pattern incorrectly, the plugin silently extracts a DOI from any HTTPS URL's pathname. The extracted identifier is then resolved against doi.org, which may return a BibTeX record for a completely different work. This is a validation-evasion pattern where the plugin assumes its input is already sanitized by the caller.
**Evidence:** `resolver-plugins/doi.mjs:45-60` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Investigate before action.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent):
  Claim disposition: Needs investigation, but the validation gap is real at HEAD. doi.mjs:45 doiFromInput, for any input matching URL_SCHEME (/^https?:\/\//i), does new URL(trimmed) then doi = url.pathname.replace(/^\//, "") (lines 48-54). It never checks url.host against doi.org / dx.doi.org. So doiFromInput("https://evil.org/10.1000/xyz123") yields 10.1000/xyz123, which is then resolved against doi.org. Notably the function s own doc comment (lines 36-37) claims "the doi.org/dx.doi.org leading-slash + host is removed" — but the code removes only the leading slash, not the host; the comment describes a check that does not exist. doi.mjs also runs standalone (import.meta.main, line 62) reading raw stdin, so on that path doiFromInput is the only validation and the manifest pattern gate (pluginAcceptsInput, resolverPlugins.ts:155) does not apply.
  Remediation disposition: underspecified. Whether to assert the host belongs to the doi.org family in the URL branch (bringing the code in line with its own comment and with crash-at-the-boundary), vs. treating the manifest pattern as sufficient for the spawned path, is a first-principles remediation call.
  Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Crash at the Boundary — each boundary should assert its expected input shape rather than trust an upstream gate; a comment that misdescribes the code is also a laundering/accuracy concern.

Root concern: the URL branch of doiFromInput extracts a DOI from the pathname of ANY https URL without verifying the host, so a non-doi.org URL can be turned into a DOI and resolved as a different work; the standalone-executable path has no other guard.

Left OPEN; no fix proposed here.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Accepted.

Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Investigate->confirmed real: doiFromInput's URL branch took url.pathname as the DOI for ANY host, contradicting its own comment; a non-doi.org URL silently yielded a bogus DOI (live on the ungated stdin path).

Remediation (independent remediation subagent / first-principles spec): The URL branch validates url.hostname against an exact {doi.org, dx.doi.org} set and fails loud on any other host; bare-DOI and query/fragment handling unchanged. Proven: doi.org/dx.doi.org URLs resolve, a non-doi.org host throws, bare DOIs (incl. ?/# suffix) stay intact.

## ai-review/slop / An error crossing a system boundary must preserve its original type identification so the downstream observer can discriminate different failure modes.

A date that fails semantic validation must not be silently converted to a simpler form that omits the failure signal.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/133)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected. (Re-flag of the previously-rejected cleanDate read-path normalization; rationale re-verified.)

Claim disposition: false. The body concern is "A date that fails semantic validation must not be silently converted to a simpler form that omits the failure signal." cleanDate (zoteroRepository.ts:170-177) is not a validation boundary and there is no failure signal to omit: it normalizes Zotero's own stored date-sentinel convention on the READ path. Zotero stores year-only dates as the literal "YYYY-00-00 YYYY" / "YYYY-00-00" form; cleanDate extracts the year from that known storage shape (zeroMatch / partialZero) and otherwise passes the value through verbatim. It reads existing library data Zotero wrote, not untrusted input crossing a write boundary, so there is no semantic-validation step here that could "fail" — the 00-00 month/day is a deliberate Zotero encoding, not a validation error. The header ("error crossing a system boundary must preserve its original type") also does not apply to a pure string-normalization read helper.

Remediation disposition: misaligned. Treating a real Zotero storage convention as a validation failure to surface would manufacture spurious failures on normal library data.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy (read-path normalization of a known upstream storage convention is not a fail-loud boundary; no concrete defect now) + Verification Rule. No code change made. Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:170-177 (cleanDate) and :562 (read-path use in item mapping).

### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 414a801a10b0bf1b66d0d860249959e2a0b9a02fd0b3346efd05ca77047134b8 -->

**Location:** `src/columnModel.ts:177-178`
**Violated invariant:** A value crossing from an untyped framework boundary (TanStack columnId: string) into a typed owned domain (SortKey union) must be validated at the boundary, not cast with a bare TypeScript `as` assertion that evaporates at runtime.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'as SortKey' src/columnModel.ts && tsc --noEmit --strict src/columnModel.ts 2>&1 | head -20`
**Pattern:** type-assertion-bypass: A TanStack `columnId: string` is asserted to `SortKey` with an unguarded `as` cast. The `sortableValue` function that receives this value branches on specific `SortKey` members; an unrecognized key from persistence or a new column definition passes the cast silently and returns `undefined`, producing `NaN` subtraction in the comparator.
**Why this matters:** A persistent column layout from a future version, or a programmatic column registration that bypasses the `SortKey` union, silently breaks sorting: every comparison returns `0` (no sort), hiding the fault behind normal-looking but unsorted data.
**Evidence:** `src/columnModel.ts:172-186` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected. (Re-flag of the previously-rejected as-cast family; rationale re-verified against current code.)

Claim disposition: false in its consequential premise. zoteroSortingFn casts columnId as SortKey at columnModel.ts:177-178. The reviewer's stated failure path — "a persistent column layout from a future version, or a programmatic column registration that bypasses the SortKey union, silently breaks sorting" — is precluded by the current code, not left to chance. (1) Column ids are single-sourced: ZOTERO_COLUMNS is built by buildColumnDef (columnModel.ts:204-217) with id: column.key where column.key is typed SortKey via ColumnDefinition; TanStack does not invent columnIds, it carries exactly these. (2) Persisted layouts cannot inject a foreign id: readColumnLayout (columnModel.ts:76-120) validates the stored columnOrder/visibility/sizing against DEFAULT_COLUMN_IDS and throws loudly on any unknown id (lines 88-110) and on count mismatch — it does NOT silently reset. So every columnId reaching sortableValue is a real SortKey member, and the "unrecognized key returns undefined -> NaN/0-sort" path cannot be reached on a contract-valid layout.

Remediation disposition: misaligned. Adding a runtime membership re-check at the cast site would guard a state the persistence boundary already proves impossible (duplicate validation), and sortableValue itself is total over SortKey (no NaN-producing branch; falsy -> '' at librarySelectors.ts:184).

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy Verification Rule (no concrete defect on a contract-valid layout right now; the unknown-id condition is structurally precluded by the fail-loud persistence contract) + anti-slop (the proposed boundary re-validation would be redundant validation behind an existing single-source-of-truth contract). No code change made. Audit anchor: columnModel.ts:76-120 (fail-loud persistence contract), columnModel.ts:204-217 (single-source column ids), librarySelectors.ts:167-185 (total sortableValue).

threads.finding_body:
### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 91fdc2d6f3c73f11e9636f3954cf3f1181e0c8625841faab3c68524b2d20c76d -->

**Location:** `src/App.tsx:120-134`
**Violated invariant:** A user-facing data export must not bundle internal rendering metadata (column visibility, order, sizing) with domain data (Zotero items, collections) in the same serialized artifact, because the column layout is an implementation detail of the table component, not a property of the Zotero library.
**Proof:** `grep -A 15 'exportDatabaseJson' src/App.tsx | grep -E '(columnVisibility|columnOrder|columnSizing|columns:)'`
**Pattern:** domain-contamination: The `exportDatabaseJson` handler serializes TanStack table state (`{ columnVisibility, columnOrder, columnSizing }`) alongside `{ items, collections }` into a single downloadable JSON. Column layout is a rendering concern; bundling it with domain data creates an artifact where column widths and hidden-state flags appear to be Zotero metadata.
**Why this matters:** The exported artifact is not a clean Zotero data snapshot — it is a snapshot of one browser's rendering state welded onto the library data. An import/restore function would need to strip or ignore the `columns` key, and any downstream consumer that reads this file must know the column keys are internal TanStack identifiers, not Zotero field names.
**Evidence:** `src/App.tsx:120-134` (file-read)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected. GENUINELY NEW (tier2 SLOP SUSPECT, first raised against the exportDatabaseJson handler as committed); judged freshly against App.tsx:120-134.

Claim disposition: factually accurate but design-hostile. The description is true to the code: exportDatabaseJson (App.tsx:120-134) serializes { items, collections, columns } where columns = { columnVisibility, columnOrder, columnSizing } from the TanStack table state. But this is a user-requested feature, not an implementation defect. The handler is the "Database backup exported to JSON" action (showToast at :133); what a user-facing backup artifact contains is a product/scope decision. Bundling the user's current column layout into a manual backup so a restore can reproduce the same view is a coherent product choice, not domain contamination introduced by an agent.

Remediation disposition: misaligned. anti-slop's design-choice gate is single-pass: this matches the "implements a specific named feature a user would request" signal (export/backup), so the feature scope is a premise, not a review target. Splitting rendering state out of the backup, or defining a separate artifact, is exactly the kind of "make it more standard" refactor the policy bars for user-owned design decisions. There is no proof-loop failure, no fail-open branch, no boundary-validation evasion, and no fallback here.

Policy basis: anti-slop "Design Choices Are Not Slop" single-gate test (named user-requested feature = premise) + bespoke-software-policy Verification Rule (no concrete defect for the owner right now; the export round-trips its own format). No code change made. Audit anchor: src/App.tsx:120-134.

## ai-review/general / A request with a malformed JSON body sent to any POST endpoint must return HTTP 400 with kind invalid_request, not HTTP 500 with kind internal_error. The error middleware must distinguish client-level

Every non-synthetic collection from the repository must carry a Zotero collection key at the import boundary, but both the runtime schema (CollectionSchema) and the type interface (Collection) declare key as optional, so a future schema change that drops key would not be caught at compile time.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/142)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected; scanner header outdated.

Claim disposition: false on both the header and the body. (1) Header "malformed JSON body must return 400 invalid_request, not 500" is already addressed (commit 7dc3d8e): classifyError detects the express.json structural identity { type: 'entity.parse.failed', status: 400 } and returns 400 invalid_request (server.ts:146-157), proven by serverErrors.test.ts "classifies a malformed JSON request body as a 400 invalid_request, not a 500" (lines 318-329). (2) Body claim "both the runtime schema (CollectionSchema) and the type interface (Collection) declare key as optional" is factually wrong. Collection is a discriminated union: the real variant requires key non-optionally in BOTH surfaces — RealCollectionSchema.key is z.string().min(1) (schemas.ts:51) and RealCollection.key is key: string (types.ts:166-172). A keyless real collection is unrepresentable: it fails the Zod parse at runtime and is a compile-time error in the type. The synthetic library-root variant intentionally has no key (it is not a real collection and never reaches the import boundary). So a future schema change dropping key WOULD be caught — at compile time on the type and at runtime by the schema.

Remediation disposition: misaligned (no optional-key drift exists; the discriminated union already makes a keyless real collection impossible).

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy crash-at-the-boundary (required key enforced by schema + type) + pr-feedback-triage Loop and Convergence (Outdated header against 7dc3d8e). No code change made. Audit anchor: src/schemas.ts:44-57, src/types.ts:160-176, src/server/server.ts:146-157, src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:318-329.

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected (verified against current code; the route test wires the production load path).

Claim disposition: false. The invariant "Route-level tests must exercise the production code path ... not bypass the contract preflight by passing queryLibrary(db) directly" is satisfied. The /api/library route test wires loadLibrary: () => loadValidatedLibrary(db) (libraryApi.test.ts:531) — the production path that runs the structural preflight (assertStructuralContract) and then queryLibrary exactly once. It does NOT inject queryLibrary(db) directly to bypass the preflight. Every contract-failure test routes through loadValidatedLibrary as well (expectContractFailure at libraryApi.test.ts:84-95 calls loadValidatedLibrary; uses at lines 86, 100, 112, 208, 226, 255, 287, 345, 364, 391, 426). The cited line 180 is inside a contract test ("fails before serving when representative rows do not match owned row schemas", lines 172-187) that asserts invalid_rows through loadValidatedLibrary via expectContractFailure — i.e. the production path, not a bypass.

Remediation disposition: misaligned (no bypass exists to remediate).

Policy basis: test-guidelines Red-Green Evidence / boundary proof (the route boundary is exercised through the real production load path) + pr-feedback-triage Loop and Convergence. No code change made. Audit anchor: src/server/libraryApi.test.ts:527-568 (route test wires loadValidatedLibrary) and :84-95 (contract tests route through loadValidatedLibrary).

## ai-review/general / A type assertion must not substitute for runtime input validation when downstream consumers rely on the asserted type for correct behavior. Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>.

POLICY.NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT — a required domain field that fails preflight checks must not be allowed to pass through the Zod boundary as undefined only to be silently replaced with empty string by every downstream consumer

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/144)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected. MISLOCATED scanner header; underlying pattern re-verified and previously-rejected rationale holds.

Claim disposition: false at this location. The header references "Casting value as ZoteroSearchKey after checking Set<string>" and the body asserts a NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT concern (a required field passing the Zod boundary as undefined, then replaced with empty string downstream). Neither matches schemas.ts:37, which is the LibraryRootCollectionSchema definition (a discriminated-union member; no cast, no default). The ZoteroSearchKey cast actually lives in src/utils/fuzzy.ts:269, and it is fail-loud-guarded: function searchKey(value) { if (SEARCH_DOCUMENT_KEYS.has(value as ZoteroSearchKey)) return value as ZoteroSearchKey; throw new Error(...); } (fuzzy.ts:268-271) — the cast is inside the membership-checked branch and an unknown key throws, exactly the previously-rejected guarded-cast pattern. The NO_RUNTIME_DEFAULT body concern also does not apply to schemas.ts:37: RealCollectionSchema.key is z.string().min(1) (required, schemas.ts:51) and inTrash is z.boolean() (required, schemas.ts:95) — no required field is admitted as undefined-then-defaulted at this boundary.

Remediation disposition: misaligned (no real target at the cited line; the actual cast is already a fail-loud guard).

Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Global Principle 10 (mislocated/garbled scanner header is not a finding) + bespoke-software-policy crash-at-the-boundary (the real searchKey cast is membership-guarded and throws on miss). No code change made. Audit anchor: src/schemas.ts:37-52 (no cast/default here), src/utils/fuzzy.ts:268-271 (guarded ZoteroSearchKey cast).

## ai-review/general / A package name must appear in at most one dependency category. Having `vite` in both `dependencies` and `devDependencies` creates an ambiguous declaration: npm, yarn, and pnpm each resolve this differ

A package name must appear in at most one dependency category. Having `vite` in both `dependencies` and `devDependencies` creates an ambiguous declaration: npm, yarn, and pnpm each resolve this differently, and `npm install --production` installs a build tool as a runtime dependency.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/138)

replies.author: dzackgarza

Claim disposition: false against current package.json. The concern "vite appears in both dependencies and devDependencies" no longer holds: vite is declared exactly once, in devDependencies ("vite": "^6.2.3", package.json:62). It is not present in the dependencies block (lines 18-46, which ends at "zod": "^4.4.3" on line 45). So the ambiguous dual-category declaration the reviewer describes does not exist, and npm install --production no longer pulls vite as a runtime dependency.

Policy basis: pr-feedback-triage Loop and Convergence — already-addressed finding re-raised on a new push is disposed Outdated against the superseding commit. Audit anchor: package.json:18-46 (dependencies, no vite) and :47-64 (devDependencies, vite present once), commit 0f653ae.

## ai-review/general / Route-level tests must exercise the production code path (loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri -> assertZoteroDatabaseContract -> queryLibrary), not bypass the contract preflight by passing queryLibrary(db) dir

Item types should be validated exactly once against a definitive contract. Calling parseItemType during preflight (line 230) and then LibraryPayloadSchema.parse (line 564) on the same data duplicates the same validation without providing a distinct fault domain.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/141)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected; scanner header mislocated.

Claim disposition: false in its consequential premise. The body alleges item-type validation is duplicated "without providing a distinct fault domain" between the preflight (assertObservedItemTypes, which calls parseItemType at zoteroRepository.ts:236 over a DISTINCT typeName query) and the final LibraryPayloadSchema.parse. The two are NOT the same validation and DO serve distinct fault domains: (1) assertObservedItemTypes is a cheap structural preflight that runs a SELECT DISTINCT typeName and validates each distinct observed type once, failing loud EARLY with the specific typed kind ZoteroDatabaseContractError('unsupported_item_type', ...) before the full pipeline runs; (2) the final LibraryPayloadSchema.parse (zoteroRepository.ts:603) validates the materialized payload shape via ItemTypeSchema = z.enum(ITEM_TYPES) and would surface a bad type only as a generic ZodError, wrapped as 'invalid_rows'. The preflight exists precisely to give an early, distinct, named failure domain (unsupported_item_type) that the final shape-parse cannot provide. Removing it would lose that distinct fault domain — the opposite of the reviewer's stated invariant. The scanner header ("Route-level tests must exercise the production code path") is mislocated to zoteroRepository.ts:230.

Remediation disposition: misaligned. Collapsing to a single validation would erase the early, specifically-typed unsupported_item_type contract failure.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy crash-at-the-boundary (early, specifically-typed structural rejection is a distinct and intended fault domain, not redundant validation) + pr-feedback-triage Global Principle 10 (mislocated header). No code change made. Audit anchor: src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:220-238 (typed-early preflight), :555 (row mapping), :603 (final shape parse), and the typed kind at :134-153.

threads.finding_body:
## ai-review/general / A runtime-validated string value cast with `as ItemType` must match the TypeScript ItemType union exactly. The guard `ITEM_TYPES.includes(value)` and the `as ItemType` cast are independent code paths 

A runtime-validated string value cast with `as ItemType` must match the TypeScript ItemType union exactly. The guard `ITEM_TYPES.includes(value)` and the `as ItemType` cast are independent code paths that can drift: adding a type to ITEM_TYPES without adding it to the TypeScript union (or vice versa) makes the type annotation incorrect without a build error.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/137)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Rejected. (Re-flag of the previously-rejected as-cast family; rationale re-verified against current code.)

Claim disposition: false. The reviewer's premise — "adding a type to ITEM_TYPES without adding it to the TypeScript union (or vice versa) makes the type annotation incorrect without a build error" — is factually wrong for this codebase. There is no separate hand-maintained union to drift: ItemType is DERIVED from the array, export type ItemType = typeof ITEM_TYPES[number] (types.ts:39). Adding a member to ITEM_TYPES automatically extends ItemType; the two cannot diverge. Separately, the cast at zoteroRepository.ts:181 (return value as ItemType) is not validation-by-cast: it sits inside parseItemType (lines 179-184) guarded by a fail-loud membership check — if (typeof value === 'string' && (ITEM_TYPES as readonly string[]).includes(value)) return value as ItemType; — and otherwise throws ZoteroDatabaseContractError('unsupported_item_type', ...). The cast only executes on a value already proven to be a member at runtime.

Remediation disposition: misaligned. Replacing the cast with anything else would be churn: the membership check is the runtime validation, the union is single-sourced from the same array, and an unsupported type already crashes the load loudly.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy crash-at-the-boundary (the boundary already asserts membership and crashes on mismatch; this is the correct pattern, not as-any validation evasion) + Verification Rule (no drift possible, no concrete defect now). No code change made. Audit anchor: types.ts:1-39 (ITEM_TYPES + derived union), zoteroRepository.ts:179-184 (guarded parseItemType).
