### [General Review][tier1] SQL NOT IN subquery NULL sensitivity — entire library vanishes on DB corruption
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 283075c6373bbe678e1c6c8786dfd87208e615b8de6e089939c363aba9b0a04a -->

**Location:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:409-415`
**Violated invariant:** All non-child items must be queryable regardless of NULL presence in subquery result sets. x NOT IN (subquery) is UNKNOWN (not TRUE) when subquery contains NULL, filtering out every row.
**Proof:** `sqlite3 :memory: 'CREATE TABLE t(x); INSERT INTO t VALUES (1),(2),(NULL); SELECT 1 WHERE 3 NOT IN (SELECT x FROM t);'`
**Symptom:** Four SQL queries in zoteroRepository.ts use `WHERE i.itemID NOT IN (SELECT itemID FROM ...)`. If a corrupted Zotero DB or future schema change produces a NULL `itemID` in any subquery result set, SQL semantics dictate that `NOT IN (subquery_with_null)` returns FALSE for every row, making the query return zero rows. The entire library silently disappears.
**Source:** Lines 409-415 (items query), 444-445 (notes query), 462 (attachments query), 482 (standalone attachments query).
**Consequence:** A single NULL `itemID` in `itemAttachments`, `itemNotes`, or `itemAnnotations` makes the entire top-level item list empty. The app renders 'My Library (0 items)', no error is surfaced, and the user sees an empty library with no diagnostic.
**Evidence:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:409-415` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:444-445` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:462-462` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:482-482` (source)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Investigate before action. GENUINELY NEW (first raised against the just-committed anti-join SQL).

Claim disposition: needs investigation. The SQL kernel is true in general: x NOT IN (SELECT y ...) evaluates to UNKNOWN (not TRUE) for every row when the subquery result set contains a NULL, so a NULL in the subquery would empty the result. The triggering premise, however, is contested by the owned schema: the four cited subqueries select itemID from itemAttachments, itemNotes, itemAnnotations (items-query anti-join at zoteroRepository.ts:409-415) and deletedItems (notes/attachments anti-joins at :445, :463, :482). In both the real Zotero schema and the test fixture (libraryApi.test.ts:31-34) each of those itemID columns is declared INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, which in SQLite is a rowid alias that can never hold NULL (an inserted NULL is auto-assigned the rowid). So under the contract the repository asserts, a NULL itemID cannot appear in these subquery result sets, and the empty-library symptom cannot occur on a contract-valid DB.

Remediation disposition: underspecified. The residual question the orchestrator must resolve: the structural preflight (assertRequiredTablesAndColumns) validates column presence but does not explicitly assert the rowid-alias / NOT NULL invariant these anti-joins silently depend on. Whether to (a) reject on the rowid-alias guarantee, or (b) make that invariant explicit at the boundary, is a judgment call I leave open. No fix detail is proposed here.

Policy basis: bespoke-software-policy crash-at-the-boundary / verification rule (a defect must cause a concrete problem on a contract-valid DB right now). Root concern: the anti-join correctness rests on an itemID-never-NULL invariant that is guaranteed by SQLite rowid-alias semantics but is not asserted by the explicit DB contract. Left OPEN for orchestrator decision.

### [General Review][tier1] Double Zod parse of library payload on attachment-open
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 737ce9ca4f76b6578d1ab951045529b205b6ac479de495bdf679ee816abc4ad1 -->

**Location:** `src/server/server.ts:229-229`
**Violated invariant:** A validated LibraryPayload must not be parsed through the same Zod schema a second time — the return type of loadValidatedLibrary is already LibraryPayload.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'LibraryPayloadSchema.parse' src/server/server.ts src/server/zoteroRepository.ts`
**Symptom:** The POST /api/attachments/:id/open handler parses the entire library payload through Zod twice: once inside `deps.loadLibrary()` (which calls `loadValidatedLibrary` -> `LibraryPayloadSchema.parse` at zoteroRepository.ts:603) and again via the explicit `LibraryPayloadSchema.parse(deps.loadLibrary())` at server.ts:229.
**Source:** src/server/server.ts:229 — `const attachment = requireAttachmentWithPath(LibraryPayloadSchema.parse(deps.loadLibrary()), attachmentId);`
**Consequence:** Every attachment-open request validates the full library payload twice. For a 50k-item library with 5 field values each, ~250k Zod field validations run twice per request.
**Evidence:** `src/server/server.ts:229-229` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:603-603` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:353-366` (source)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written. GENUINELY NEW (first raised against the just-committed loadValidatedLibrary return-type contract).

Claim disposition: true. deps.loadLibrary() is typed AppDeps.loadLibrary(): LibraryPayload (server.ts:57) and in production resolves to loadValidatedLibrary -> queryLibrary, which already terminates in LibraryPayloadSchema.parse(...) (zoteroRepository.ts:603). The attachment-open handler then re-parses that already-validated value through the SAME schema: server.ts:229 requireAttachmentWithPath(LibraryPayloadSchema.parse(deps.loadLibrary()), attachmentId). The same redundant re-parse is present on the GET /api/library handler at server.ts:171 (res.json(LibraryPayloadSchema.parse(deps.loadLibrary()))). A value whose static type is already the parsed LibraryPayload is run through the full Zod validation a second time on every request to these routes.

Remediation disposition: aligned in direction (remove the redundant second parse), but I propose no fix detail — that is the remediation subagent's responsibility, including whether both sites (server.ts:171 and :229) are in scope.

Policy basis: anti-slop redundant-validation / dead-validation on the production path; the return-type contract of loadValidatedLibrary is the single source of truth for shape, so a second parse adds no epistemic value and runs full-payload validation twice per request. Root concern: a value that is statically and at runtime already a parsed LibraryPayload is re-validated through the identical schema at the route boundary. Accepted; remediation to be specced independently.

### [General Review][tier1] Regex pattern recompiled on every from-source POST
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: c77dafebbed4707c504e0959db3bef4f5529cb9f2260d3593091c03a408854d5 -->

**Location:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:137-137`
**Violated invariant:** Regex patterns that are fixed at configuration load time must be compiled once, not on every HTTP request.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'new RegExp' src/server/resolverPlugins.ts`
**Symptom:** `pluginAcceptsInput` compiles `new RegExp(descriptor.pattern, 'i')` on every invocation. With 4 plugins and ~5 patterns each, every POST /api/items/from-source request compiles 20 regex objects from scratch.
**Source:** src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:137 — `return plugin.acceptedInputs.some(descriptor => new RegExp(descriptor.pattern, 'i').test(input.trim()));`
**Consequence:** Unnecessary regex compilation on every request. Patterns are validated at load time (line 121) but the compiled objects are discarded. If a pattern is invalid, the error surfaces inside a per-request path instead of failing loud at startup.
**Evidence:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:121-121` (source), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:137-137` (source), `src/server/server.ts:189-189` (source)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Accepted as written. GENUINELY NEW (first raised against the just-committed pluginAcceptsInput).

Claim disposition: true. pluginAcceptsInput (resolverPlugins.ts:137) compiles new RegExp(descriptor.pattern, 'i') for every accepted-input descriptor on every call, and it is called per request on the POST /api/items/from-source path (server.ts:189 requireAcceptedInput -> pluginAcceptsInput) and again inside runResolverPlugin (resolverPlugins.ts:155). loadResolverPlugins validates each pattern once at load time by constructing new RegExp(input.pattern) (resolverPlugins.ts:121) but discards the compiled object, so the compiled regexes are rebuilt per request rather than retained from the load-time validation. A second consequence the reviewer notes is real: because patterns are only validated transiently at load, a pattern that is valid as new RegExp(pattern) but not as new RegExp(pattern, 'i') (or any per-call divergence) would surface inside the per-request path rather than failing loud at startup.

Remediation disposition: aligned in direction (compile once at configuration load, fail loud there). No fix detail proposed; that is the remediation subagent's responsibility.

Policy basis: anti-slop brittleness/coupling — fixed-at-config-load values recompiled per request; and fail-loud-at-startup (bespoke-software-policy) for pattern validity. Root concern: resolver accepted-input regexes are fixed at config load but are compiled fresh on every from-source request instead of once, and pattern validity is not retained as a startup-proven invariant. Accepted; remediation to be specced independently.

### [General Review][tier1] Shared mutable importMode across tests creates cascading-failure risk
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: c9fdac7211ae88b0b50e8b954ceae251588e25f326a105ca47ce4cf5418f6be2 -->

**Location:** `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:19-19`
**Violated invariant:** Test state must not be shared across tests via mutable module-level variables. Each test must fully control its own fault-injection conditions.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'importMode' src/server/serverErrors.test.ts`
**Symptom:** `let importMode: 'fail' | 'success' = 'fail'` is a module-level mutable variable shared by all tests in the describe block, controlling whether a shared import HTTP server returns success or failure.
**Source:** src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:19 — `let importMode: 'fail' | 'success' = 'fail';`
**Consequence:** If a test fails after changing `importMode`, the next test inherits the stale mode, producing cascading failures that are hard to diagnose. Also prevents parallel test execution.
**Evidence:** `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:19-19` (source), `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:142-176` (source), `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:267-267` (source), `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:341-341` (source), `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:350-350` (source)

replies.author: dzackgarza
replies.body:
Disposition (independent disposition subagent): Investigate before action. GENUINELY NEW (test-quality, first raised against serverErrors.test.ts as committed).

Claim disposition: true as a structural description. let importMode: 'fail' | 'success' = 'fail' (serverErrors.test.ts:19) is a module-level mutable variable read inside the shared importServer handler (lines 153-157) and reassigned by individual tests (lines 231, 267, 270, 281, 292, 319, 335, 342, 351). The reviewer's stated failure mode is the concern: a test that fails after flipping importMode could leave a stale mode for the next test, and the shared mutable state blocks parallel execution within the describe block. Mitigating fact in current code: every test that actually reaches the import boundary sets importMode explicitly at its start, and the only test that does not (line 216) fails before the import server is touched, so there is no current cross-test correctness dependence on an inherited value.

Remediation disposition: underspecified. This is a test-isolation hygiene concern, not a banned-test-shape and not a proof-loop failure (the assertions themselves are proof-bearing real-boundary checks). Whether the shared-state pattern is worth restructuring is a test-quality judgment for the orchestrator; I propose no fix.

Policy basis: test-guidelines test-isolation / anti-gaming (shared mutable fault-injection state). Root concern: a module-level mutable flag drives a shared fixture server's behavior across all tests in the describe block, so test outcomes are coupled through hidden shared state rather than each test fully owning its fault-injection conditions. Left OPEN for orchestrator decision.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Classify malformed write-plugin responses as upstream failures**

When the Zotero write plugin returns HTTP 200 but the body is malformed JSON or no longer matches `ZoteroImportResultSchema`, this line throws `SyntaxError`/`ZodError` outside `ZoteroImportError`. The `/api/items/from-source` wrapper only maps `ZoteroImportError` to `upstream_boundary_failed`, so a write-plugin response-contract failure is reported as a 500 `internal_error` instead of the upstream 502 kind promised by this boundary; wrap the JSON/schema parse in `ZoteroImportError` as well.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
